ref: “A virgin will have a baby boy, and he will be called Immanuel,” which means “God is with us.”
text: “Ɔbitɛ gɔ loiɣe ɔrɛrɛ̃ to ɔ̃ala kayiri ɔɣe ɔbi birɛrɛĩ. Maatã wũ iyere sɔ Imanuel,” gɔ karɔ̃ nɖe sɔ Ɣaa piagu bo.

ref: more to the birth of the Messiah. The Birth of Jesus (Luke
text: Azur ɔɣe Zadok, Zadok ɔɣe Akim, Akim ɔɣe Elihud,

ref: This is how Jesus Christ was born. A young woman named Mary was engaged to Joseph from King David's family. But before they were married, she learned that she was going to have a baby by God's Holy Spirit.
text: Kumɛgɔ isɛ karɔ̃ fiɛ maɣe Yesu i ngbe ne. Makarɛ Maria matã Yosef, ɣɛɛ si ɔ̃akɔ wũ lɛ ɔ̃ ɔre ne, manya sɔ Maria ka ɔto kayiri iki i Siwarã Bielea ɔle ame.

ref: Joseph was a good man and did not want to embarrass Mary in front of everyone. So he decided to quietly call off the wedding.
text: Yosef ɖe ɔturi bɔlɔlɔa i Ɣaa anɔ, ɣɛɛ gɔ ɔ̃ito ɔbie sɔ ɔ̃apia Maria kunuarɛ ɔso ne, ɔbie sɔ ɔ̃anyua ɔrɔ̃go i iwɛrɛ ame.

ref: While Joseph was thinking about this, an angel from the Lord appeared to him in a dream. The angel said, “Joseph, the baby that Mary will have is from the Holy Spirit. Go ahead and marry her.
text: Ɔwi gɔ ɔto ɔbara adzuni wagbe i ɔ̃ iti ame ne, Ɣaa kpabo ɔwɛ̃ ɔɖi so ɔte wũ i kurɛ ame ɔɣere wũ sɔ, “Yosef, Igara David ɔwa tɛtɛ, daanigã sɔ aakɔ fɔ ɔre Maria abɔ so i kɔrɛ, ala kayiri ga ɔto ne, Siwarã Bielea kɔrɛ kabɔrɛ.

ref: Then after her baby is born, name him Jesus, because he will save his people from their sins.”
text: Ɔto ɔ̃aɣe ɔbirɛrɛĩ, tã wũ iyere sɔ Yesu, alasɔ ɔ̃ loaɖi ɔ̃ maturi ɔbɔrɛgu i ma akpi ame ne.”

ref: So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet had said,
text: Nwagbe ɔɖuɖu ɔba sɔ si nnɛ i Ɣaa ɔɣɛ ɔkigu i ɔ̃ kanyaɖidze ɔwɛ̃ iso aaba i ne ame sɔ,

ref: After Joseph woke up, he and Mary were soon married, just as the Lord's angel had told him to do.
text: Ne ɔso gɔ Yosef ɔta ne, ɔyiri Maria lɛ kumɛgɔ i Ɣaa kpabo ɔɣere wũ awe.

ref: But they did not sleep together before her baby was born. Then Joseph named him Jesus.
text: Ɣɛɛ ne, Yosef iirɛgu wũ gbɔgbɔɔgbɔ Maria ɔɣe ɔ̃ ɔbi ɖeakatɔ̃ gɔ nɖe ɔbi birɛrɛĩ, fiɛ Yosef ɔtã wũ iyere sɔ Yesu.

ref: When Jesus was born in the village of Bethlehem in Judea, Herod was king. During this time some wise men from the east came to Jerusalem
text: Maɣe Yesu i Betlehem ɔmagɛ̃ ame i Yudea ku ɔwi gɔ ame i Herodes to ɔɖe sigara. Ɔwi gɔmɔ ame ne, manɔgbadze ma nɣe ara i awãmi iso ɔbɔrɛ i kuɣɛ̃kabɔrɛkɔ̃ maba Yerusalem Ɔmagɛ̃ ame.

ref: and said, “Where is the child born to be king of the Jews? We saw his star in the east and have come to worship him.”
text: Gɔ maba ne, makarɛ sɔ, “Lɛ maɣe ɔbi gɔ loaɖe ma Yudase igara? Bonya ɔ̃ iwãmi i kuɣɛ̃kabɔrɛkɔ̃ ɔso boba sɔ si boba boasumu wũ.”

ref: When King Herod heard about this, he was worried, and so was everyone else in Jerusalem.
text: Gɔ Igara Herodes ɔnɔ itɔ̃me nɛgbe ne, itidza ɔ̃ gu mma ɔɖuɖu mpia i Yerusalem gbaã.

ref: Herod brought together the chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses and asked them, “Where will the Messiah be born?”
text: Ne ɔso ɔkpere masɔrɛdze makpakpa ɔɖuɖu gu mmara matedze ɔsirakanya ɔkarɛ ma sɔ, “Lɛ maaɣe Kristo?”

ref: They told him, “He will be born in Bethlehem, just as the prophet wrote,
text: Ne maɣere wũ sɔ, “Maɣɛ sɔ maaɣe wũ i Betlehem ɔmagɛ̃ ame i Yudea ala Ɣaa kanyaɖidze ɔwɛ̃ ɔtsɛrɛ ɔsɛ sɔ,

ref: ‘Bethlehem in the land of Judea, you are very important among the towns of Judea. From your town will come a leader, who will be like a shepherd for my people Israel.’ ”
text: ‘Fɔ Betlehem ne, maturi sɛ maɣɛ sɔ ɔmagɛ̃ kosoi aɖe i Yudea karɔ̃ iso. Ɣɛɛ iiɖe ngbɔ iɖe! Alasɔ fɔ ame i tɔ̃ɖedze siare gɔ loakɔ wũ kaɖe Israel aabɔrɛ.’ ”

ref: Herod secretly called in the wise men and asked them when they had first seen the star.
text: Ne kama ne, Herodes ɔkpere mafɔ ma lobɔrɛ i kuɣɛ̃kabɔrɛkɔ̃ ɔnɔwɛ̃ ɔkɛlɛgu kawɛrɛkɔ̃ sɔ si ɔtsue ɔwi gɔ tutuutu manya iwãmi nɛmɔ.

ref: He told them, “Go to Bethlehem and search carefully for the child. As soon as you find him, let me know. I also want to go and worship him.”
text: Ɔɖi ma ɔri sɔ makɛlɛ Betlehem ku atɔ̃me wagbe sɔ, “Misɛ mianyɔnyɔ ɔbi gɔgbe kukaakɔ, si minya wũ ne, mikpese miba miaɣere me sɔ mme wũ loasɛ loasumu wũ.”

ref: When the men went into the house and saw the child with Mary, his mother, they knelt down and worshiped him. They took out their gifts of gold, frankincense, and myrrh and gave them to him.
text: Gɔ mabo i iyo ame fiɛ manya ɔbi gu ɔ̃ ɔnyi Maria ne, mapɛ agɛgɛ̃ masumu wũ. Gɔ mabusi ma akpo ne, maɖi karatã sɛɛ ga nɖe sikãrɛtɛa, korobo gu ɔlufui matã wũ.

ref: Later they were warned in a dream not to return to Herod, and they went back home by another road. The Escape to Egypt
text: Ne kama gɔ makpese ne, Ɣaa ɔsɛ̃ ɔtã ma i kurɛ ame sɔ madaasɛ maaki i Herodes kɔrɛ. Ne ɔso maki ɔri mama marui makɛlɛ ma aɖe. Yosef Ɔteregu Maria Gu Yesu Ɔkɛlɛgu Egipte Karɔ̃ Iso.

ref: After the wise men had gone, an angel from the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream and said, “Get up! Hurry and take the child and his mother to Egypt! Stay there until I tell you to return, because Herod is looking for the child and wants to kill him.”
text: Gɔ mafɔ mamɔ ɔɔrui kama ne, Ɣaa kpabo ɔwɛ̃ ɔɖi so ɔte Yosef i kurɛ ame ɔɣere wũ sɔ, “Herodes to ɔ̃abie ɔri gɔ iso ɔ̃aki ɔ̃aɖoe ɔbi, ne ɔso ta si akɔ wũ gu ɔ̃ ɔnyi si ateregu ma akɛlɛgu Egipte. Miwe i mmɔ gbɔgbɔɔgbɔ loaɣere mi sɔ mikpese miba.”

ref: That night, Joseph got up and took his wife and the child to Egypt,
text: Kasɛ̃ gamɔ ame ame ne, Yosef ɔta ɔkɔ ɔbi gu ɔnyi marui matere makɛlɛ Egipte.

ref: where they stayed until Herod died. So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet had said, “I called my son out of Egypt.” The Killing of the Children
text: Mawe i mmɔ gbɔgbɔɔgbɔ Herodes ɔkpi. Iba ngbɔ sɔ si nnɛ i Ɣaa kanyaɖidze ɔwɛ̃ ɔtsɛrɛ ɔsɛ aaba i ne ame sɔ, “Ta Egipte lokpere wũ Ɔbi lobɔrɛgu.” Herodes Ɔtã Maɖoe Mabi Birɛrɛĩ Ɔɖuɖu I Betlehem.

ref: When Herod found out that the wise men from the east had tricked him, he was very angry. He gave orders for his men to kill all the boys who lived in or near Bethlehem and were two years old and younger. This was based on what he had learned from the wise men.
text: Gɔ Herodes ɔnya sɔ manɔgbadze ma lobɔrɛ i kuɣɛ̃kabɔrɛkɔ̃ mmɔ ɔsɔsɔla wũ ne, sikpã ɔmɔɛ̃ wũ gbaã. Ɔpia makpakpɛ̃dze sɔ maɖoe mabi birɛrɛĩ ɔɖuɖu ma i Betlehem gu simagɛ̃ dzɛ loki silɔ ame. Mma ɔɖuɖu loɔfɔ ita i akɔ anyɔ iba karɔ̃ si ikategu ɔwi gɔ i mmamɔ ɔɣɛ sɔ manya iwãmi kanya.

ref: So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet Jeremiah had said,
text: Mabi iɖoe nɛgbe ɔba sɔ si nnɛ i Ɣaa kanyaɖidze Yeremia ɔɣɛ ɔsɛ aaba i ne ame sɔ,

ref: “In Ramah a voice was heard crying and weeping loudly. Rachel was mourning for her children, and she refused to be comforted, because they were dead.” The Return from Egypt
text: “Kulu kuwɛ̃ to i Rama. Kaku gu isoma kulu kuɖe! Rahel nto ɔbiɛ ɔ̃ mabi, fiɛ ɔ̃ito ɔbie sɔ malora wũ, ala sɔ ɔ̃ mabi ɔkpi.” Yosef Ɔkɔ Maria Gu Yesu Makpese Makɛlɛ Nasaret.

ref: After King Herod died, an angel from the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph while he was still in Egypt.
text: Gɔ Herodes ɔkpi ne, Ɣaa kpabo ɔwɛ̃ ɔɖi so ɔte Yosef i kurɛ ame i Egipte.

ref: The angel said, “Get up and take the child and his mother back to Israel. The people who wanted to kill him are now dead.”
text: Ɔɣere wũ sɔ, “Ta si akɔ ɔbi gu ɔnyi si mikɛlɛ Israel karɔ̃ iso alasɔ mma lobie sɔ maaɖoe ɔbi ɔɔkpi.”

ref: Joseph got up and left with them for Israel.
text: Ne ɔso Yosef ɔta ɔkɔ ɔbi gu ɔnyi marui makɛlɛ Israel karɔ̃ iso.

ref: But when he heard that Herod's son Archelaus was now ruler of Judea, he was afraid to go there. Then in a dream he was told to go to Galilee,
text: Ɣɛɛ gɔ Yosef ɔnɔ sɔ Arkelao masɛra sigara i ɔ̃ ɔse kanya i Yudea ne, ɔnigã mmɔ ikɛlɛ, ne ɔso i kurɛ ame ne, Ɣaa ɔɣere wũ sɔ ɔkɛlɛ Galilea karɔ̃ iso.

ref: and they went to live there in the town of Nazareth. So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet had said, “He will be called a Nazarene.”
text: Ɔsɛ ɔ̃awe i ɔmagɛ̃ gɔ marɔ sɔ Nasaret ame sɔ itɔ̃me nɛ i Ɣaa kanyaɖidze ɔwɛ̃ ɔɣɛ ɔsɛ aba i ne ame sɔ, “Maakpere wũ sɔ ɔ Nasaretse,” aaba i ne ame.

ref: Do something to show you have really given up your sins.
text: Mite i mi nsɛgbai ame sɔ miɔfiniki mibɔrɛ i mi akpi ame.

ref: He said, “Turn back to God! The kingdom of heaven will soon be here.”
text: sɔ, “Mifiniki i mi situ ame ala Ɣaa sigara ɔɖewi ɔɔɣo.”

ref: John wore clothes made of camel's hair. He had a leather strap around his waist and ate grasshoppers and wild honey.
text: Awu wa i Yohanes ɔpia ne, kapuipɔngɔ siwɛrɛ̃ masu mawe ala, fiɛ ɔsu bɔi ɔko kafiami ɔtɔ so ɔsɔ. Maɣendu gu kugbadzedzei loɖe ɔ̃ aɖera.

ref: From Jerusalem and all Judea and from the Jordan River Valley crowds of people went to John.
text: Maturi ɔbɔrɛ i Yerusalem gu Yudea ɔɖuɖu gu Yordan ɔwore kama abuiti ɔɖuɖu ame maba ɔ̃ kɔrɛ.

ref: They told how sorry they were for their sins, and he baptized them in the river.
text: Gɔ maɖi ma akpi maɣɛ ne, Yohanes ɔpie ma Ɣaa ndu i Yordan ɔwore ame.

ref: Many Pharisees and Sadducees also came to be baptized. But John said to them: You bunch of snakes! Who warned you to run from the coming judgment?
text: Gɔ Yohanes ɔnya ma Farisise gu ma Zadukise gbodzoo ma loba sɔ ɔpie ma Ɣaa ndu ne, ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Mi ma lonyanyarĩ lɛ maɣɛ! Midaasɔsɔla so iki i Ɣaa itupie ifɔ ame sɔ miawo ɔtere i Ɣaa sikpã dzɛ nto siba i kayiiso kanya!

ref: An ax is ready to cut the trees down at their roots. Any tree that doesn't produce good fruit will be chopped down and thrown into a fire.
text: “Minyɔ! Maɔkarã kaɣɛ i adziri iso koko sɔ maatu wã ta siɖu ame. Kudziri biara gɔ loisɛ kuɣɔ̃ abi sɛɛ ne, maaɖaɛ kɔ̃ mapɛyu matsuedza.”

ref: His threshing fork is in his hand, and he is ready to separate the wheat from the husks. He will store the wheat in a barn and burn the husks in a fire that never goes out. The Baptism of Jesus (Mark
text: Ɔto ɔ̃ ifɛra nɛ ɔ̃afɛ ala simɔɣo ɔɖi i kamɔ ame. Ɔ̃asu aɖera ɔfere i kawu ame, ɣɛɛ simɔɣo ne, ɔ̃atsuedza sɛ̃ ku ɔtɔ gɔ loisɛ ɔnyi!” Yohanes Ɔpie Yesu Ɣaa Ndu.

ref: Jesus left Galilee and went to the Jordan River to be baptized by John.
text: Ɔwi gɔmɔ ame ne, Yesu ɔbɔrɛ i Galilea ɔba Yohanes kɔrɛ i Yordan ɔkpokpo sɔ Yohanes si ɔpie wũ Ɣaa ndu.

ref: But John kept objecting and said, “I ought to be baptized by you. Why have you come to me?”
text: Ɣɛɛ gɔ Yohanes to ɔbie sɔ ɔfinikira ɔ̃ adzuni ɔso ne, ɔɣere wũ sɔ, “Mme ikote sɔ loaba fɔ kɔrɛ apie me Ɣaa ndu ne iiɖe fɔ loaba wũ kɔrɛ!”

ref: Jesus answered, “For now this is how it should be, because we must do all God wants us to do.” Then John agreed.
text: Ɣɛɛ Yesu ɔtã wũ mmuai sɔ, “Tã ise ngbɔ si boawo ira biara nɛ i Ɣaa ɔbie sɔ bobara ɔbara.” Ne Yohanes ɔnɔ karɔ̃ ne.

ref: So Jesus was baptized. And as soon as he came out of the water, the sky opened, and he saw the Spirit of God coming down on him like a dove.
text: Gɔ ɔpie Yesu ndu ɔro fiɛ ɔto ɔbɔrɛ i ɔwore ame ne, kuɖokpo ɔbusi kutã wũ. Ɔnya Ɣaa Siwarã ɔso lɛ ilɔpɔ siba siasia wũ iso.

ref: Then a voice from heaven said, “This is my own dear Son, and I am pleased with him.”
text: Ne manɔ silɔ ɔbɔrɛ i kato siɣɛ sɔ, “Wũ Ɔbi gɔ loisɛ lopia kuɣɛ i ngbe ne, ɔbo me anɔ gbaã.”

ref: When Jesus heard that John had been put in prison, he went to Galilee.
text: Gɔ Yesu ɔnɔ sɔ maɔmɔɛ̃ Yohanes mapia i iyo ne, ɔrui ɔkɛlɛ Galilea.

ref: But instead of staying in Nazareth, Jesus moved to Capernaum. This town was beside Lake Galilee in the territory of Zebulun and Naphtali.
text: Ɔ̃iwe i Nasaret, ɣɛɛ ɔkɛlɛ Kapernaum, ɔmagɛ̃ gɔ nse i Galilea itubu kanya i Zebulon gu Naftali karɔ̃ iso.

ref: After Jesus had gone without eating for
text: Gɔ ɔnyi kanya akpɛnɛ sina kasɛ̃ sina ne, kukã ɔkpɛ̃ wũ.

ref: Then the devil came to him and said, “If you are God's Son, tell these stones to turn into bread.”
text: Mmɔ i Tsirise ɔba ɔ̃ kɔrɛ ɔɣere wũ sɔ, “Si fɔ nɖe Ɣaa Ɔbi ne, ɣere ata wagbe sɔ akpese aɖera.”

ref: Jesus answered, “The Scriptures also say, ‘Don't try to test the Lord your God!’ ”
text: Yesu ɔtã wũ mmuai sɔ, “Ɔko Sekelea ɔɣɛ ana sɔ, ‘Daala Bosate Ɣaa anyɔ.’ ”

ref: Finally, the devil took Jesus up on a very high mountain and showed him all the kingdoms on earth and their power.
text: Tsirise ɔledza Yesu ikɔ ikɛlɛgu kube kɔlɛa kuwɛ̃ itititɛ̃. Ɔte wũ kayiiso sigarakuɖekɔ̃ ɔɖuɖu ku kɔ̃ sile.

ref: The devil said to him, “I will give all this to you, if you will bow down and worship me.”
text: Ne ɔɣere Yesu sɔ, “Si aapɛ agɛgɛ̃ atã me asɔrɛ me ne, loasu ara wagbe ɔɖuɖu lotã-ɔ.”

ref: Jesus answered, “Go away Satan! The Scriptures say: ‘Worship the Lord your God and serve only him.’ ”
text: Ne Yesu ɔtã wũ mmuai sɔ, “Satana, rui me i kɔrɛ! Ɔko Sekelea ɔɣɛ sɔ, ‘Bosate Ɣaa ɔnɔwɛ̃ nɖe ngɔ lokote sɔ maapɛ agɛgɛ̃ matã masɔrɛ.’ ”

ref: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet Isaiah had said,
text: Ɔsɛ ɔ̃awe i mmɔ, ne ɔso nnɛ i Ɣaa Kanyaɖidze Yesaya ɔɣɛ ɔba i ne ame sɔ,

ref: Right then the two brothers dropped their nets and went with him.
text: Ɔluwɛ̃ ne, manyua ma asabu masɛ marui masiai wũ.

ref: While Jesus was walking along the shore of Lake Galilee, he saw two brothers. One was Simon, also known as Peter, and the other was Andrew. They were fishermen, and they were casting their net into the lake.
text: Gɔ Yesu to ɔkila i Galilea ibu kɔrɛ ne, ɔnya manyiibi inyɔ, Simon gɔ marɔ Petro gu ɔ̃ ɔnyiibi Andrea to mapia asabu ala makpɛɖidze maɖe.

ref: Jesus walked on until he saw James and John, the sons of Zebedee. They were in a boat with their father, mending their nets. Jesus asked them to come with him.
text: Gɔ ɔrui i mmɔ ɔsɛ kɛkɛĩ ne, ɔnya manyiibi inyɔ mama, Yakobo gu Yohanes. Ma gu ma ɔse Zebedeo si i ɔkolo ame mato maledza ma asabu fiɛ Yesu ɔkpere ma.

ref: At once they left the boat and their father and went with Jesus. Jesus Teaches, Preaches, and Heals (Luke
text: Ɔluwɛ̃ marui manyua ɔkolo gu ma ɔse masɛ masiai wũ. Yesu Ɔte Ɣaa Itɔ̃me, Ɔsa Sinyɛ Ana.

ref: Jesus went all over Galilee, teaching in their synagogues and preaching the good news about God's kingdom. He also healed every kind of disease and sickness.
text: Yesu ɔwe ɔki i Galilea abuiti ɔɖuɖu ame ɔto ɔte Ɣaa itɔ̃me i ma Yudase kusarɛkɔ̃, ɔɣɛ Sigarakaɖekɔ̃ iso Itɔ̃me Bielea, ɔsa mma ɔɖuɖu nto manyɛ sinyɛ agbãagbã.

ref: News about him spread all over Syria, and people with every kind of sickness or disease were brought to him. Some of them had a lot of demons in them, others were thought to be crazy, and still others could not walk. But Jesus healed them all.
text: Ɔ̃ iso itɔ̃me ɔki ikã i Siria karɔ̃ ɔɖuɖu iso, ne ɔso maturi ɔsore mma ɔɖuɖu nto manyɛ sinyɛ agbãagbã, mma i so to sibo, mma i siwarã lalaa to siɖaa, mma nto manyɛ kakpebubu gu mma lofɛfɛ̃ marɛ ɔɖuɖu ne, Yesu ɔsa ma ɔɖuɖu.

ref: Large crowds followed Jesus from Galilee and the region around the ten cities known as Decapolis. They also came from Jerusalem, Judea, and from across the Jordan River.
text: Maturi gbodzoo ɔsiai wũ. Ma mawɛ̃ ɔbɔrɛ i Galilea gu Simagɛ̃ Iweo gu Yerusalem gu Yudea gu nrɔ̃ mɛ mpia i Yordan ɔwore kama isɛ ame.

ref: When Jesus saw the crowds, he went up on the side of a mountain and sat down. Blessings (Luke
text: Gɔ Yesu ɔnya maturi gbodzoo ma nsiai wũ ne, ɔya ɔsɛ ɔ̃asɛ i kabemi iso fiɛ ɔ̃ marasuãdze ɔba maasɛ wũ i kɔrɛ.

ref: You must obey God's commands better than the Pharisees and the teachers of the Law obey them. If you don't, I promise you will never get into the kingdom of heaven. Anger
text: Ne ɔso nto loɣere mi sɔ miibawo Ɣaa sigarakaɖekɔ̃ ame ɔbo si mi Ɣaa kuɖɔɛ iso ibara iikɔlɛ iɖo mmara matedze gu ma Farisise ɔkpɛ. Yesu Ɔte Ara I Sikpã Ina Iso.

ref: So if you are about to place your gift on the altar and remember that someone is angry with you,
text: Ne ɔso si asu karatã ato abɔ Ɣaa isɔrɛkasege iso fiɛ anyigi i mmɔ sɔ irere pia i fɔ ɔnyii ɔtu ame i fɔ iso ne,

ref: and he taught them:
text: Ne ɔte ma ara ɔɣɛ sɔ,

ref: God blesses those people who depend only on him. They belong to the kingdom of heaven!
text: “So si siɣɔ mma nɣe sɔ mawɛrɛba maɖe i Ɣaa itsue ame. Alasɔ ma ɔkpɛ nɖe Ɣaa sigarakaɖekɔ̃.

ref: God blesses those people who grieve. They will find comfort!
text: So si siɣɔ mma nto mamararã so ala ma i Ɣaa aatui kɔrɔ̃ i kagbegbe ne.

ref: God blesses those people who are humble. The earth will belong to them!
text: So si siɣɔ mma lobɔ so karɔ̃, alasɔ ma loaɖe kayiiso ara ne.

ref: God blesses those people who are merciful. They will be treated with mercy!
text: So si siɣɔ mma nsɛ manyɔ maturi nnya, ala ma i Ɣaa aanyɔ nnya ne.

ref: God blesses those people whose hearts are pure. They will see him!
text: So si siɣɔ mma situ ame lokpa, ala ma loanya Ɣaa ne.

ref: You are the light for the whole world. A city built on top of a hill cannot be hidden,
text: “Mi nɖe ikpawaĩ mitã kayiiso ɔɖuɖu. Ɔmagɛ̃ gɔ matsue masia i kube iso ne, ɔ̃ibawo ɔwɛrɛ.

ref: But I tell you not to swear by anything when you make a promise! Heaven is God's throne, so don't swear by heaven.
text: Ɣɛɛ mme nto loɣere mi kiniɔ sɔ daatarĩgu kuira ɖuɖuuɖu si ato akã ndamu. Daatarĩgu kato ala mmɔ nɖe Ɣaa sigaraiyara ne.

ref: The earth is God's footstool, so don't swear by the earth. Jerusalem is the city of the great king, so don't swear by it.
text: Daatarĩgu karɔ̃ ala mmɔ nɖe Ɣaa ngba kasiakɔ̃ ne. Daatarĩgu Yerusalem ana ala Magara ɔɖuɖu Igara Ɔmagɛ̃ ɔɖe.

ref: You are the salt for everyone on earth. But if salt no longer tastes like salt, how can it make food salty? All it is good for is to be thrown out and walked on.
text: “Mi nse lɛ mma mitã maturi ɔɖuɖu, ɣɛɛ si mma ɔyu me ɔmɛrɛ̃ ne, maisibawo ɔri kuwɛ̃ iso ɔki ɔpia me ɔmɛrɛ̃. Misiina kusia kukuwɛ̃ iɖo sɔ maasu me mawiri maturi ɔsɛ me iso.

ref: and no one lights a lamp and puts it under a clay pot. Instead, it is placed on a lampstand, where it can give light to everyone in the house.
text: Kuwɛ̃ iisɛ ɔtsue ɔkaniɛ ɔsu kapoi ɔfu iso, ɣɛɛ ɔ̃atara ɔsia i kakɔlɛkɔ̃ sɔ ɔ̃asa ɔtã ɔbiara.

ref: Don't suppose I came to do away with the Law and the Prophets. I did not come to do away with them, but to give them their full meaning.
text: “Loiba lotindza Mose mmara gu Ɣaa kanyamaɖidze ara ite ɖuɖuuɖu, ɣɛɛ loba sɔ si wã ibiara aba i ne ame.

ref: You know our ancestors were told, “Do not murder” and “A murderer must be brought to trial.”
text: “Ɔwiɔ ne, minɔ maɣɛ sɔ, ‘Daaɖoe ɔturi.’ Ngɔ loɖoe ɔturi ne, ɔkote ɔtã atɔ̃me iɣɛgu.

ref: But I promise you if you are angry with someone, you will have to stand trial. If you call someone a fool, you will be taken to court. And if you say that someone is worthless, you will be in danger of the fires of hell.
text: Ɣɛɛ nto loɣere mi sɔ ngɔ lonagu ɔ̃ ɔnyii sikpã ne, ɔkote ɔtã atɔ̃me iɣɛgu. Ngɔ loaɣere ɔ̃ ɔnyiibi sɔ, ‘Ailɛ atã kuira’ ne, makpakpa aaɣɛgu wũ atɔ̃me. Fiɛ ngɔ loakpere ɔ̃ ɔnyiibi sɔ, ‘Bɔi aɖe’ ne, ɔkote ɔtã Ɣaa kutsue ikpadzɛ̃ itɔkpe ame ikɛlɛ.

ref: leave your gift there in front of the altar. Make peace with that person, then come back and offer your gift to God.
text: nyua fɔ karatã sɛ i isɔrɛkasegetɔ̃ si akpese akɛlɛ mi gu fɔ ɔnyii si misɛ miafere kanya ndu fiɛ aaba aasu fɔ karatã atã Ɣaa.

ref: Before you are dragged into court, make friends with the person who has accused you of doing wrong. If you don't, you will be handed over to the judge and then to the officer who will put you in jail.
text: “Si atsue sɔ fɔ ɔnyii to ɔbie sɔ ɔ̃abara-ɔ itɔ̃me ne, nyɔ sɔ miaɣɛ mirara so fiɛ miabo atɔ̃mekaɣɛkɔ̃. Si iiɖe ngbɔ ne, fɔ gu ngɔ lona itɔ̃me to ɔ̃aɖi-ɔ ɔtã atɔ̃meɣɛdze fiɛ atɔ̃meɣɛdze ana aasu-ɔ ɔtã madzuɛ̃se mapia-ɔ i iyo.

ref: I promise you will not get out until you have paid the last cent you owe. Marriage
text: Gbaã nto loɣere-ɔ sɔ ato aawe i iyo karɛkɔ̃ gbɔgbɔɔgbɔ ɔwi gɔ atã kuso gɔ ato kapɛrɛ tsɔra aro. Ara Ite I Asɔrɔ̃ra Iso.

ref: You have been taught that a man who divorces his wife must write out divorce papers for her.
text: “Masɛ maɣɛ sɔ, ‘Ɔrɛrɛ̃ gɔ losɛ̃ ɔ̃ ɔre si ɔtã wũ ɔko gɔ loate sɔ ɔ̃ɔsɛ̃ ɔ̃ ɔre.’

ref: You know our ancestors were told, “Don't use the Lord's name to make a promise unless you are going to keep it.”
text: “Ɔwiɔ ne minɔ masɛ maɣɛ ana sɔ, ‘Daatu ndamu, Ɣɛɛ bara i nnɛ aɣɛ sɔ aabara atã Ɣaa iso.’

ref: You know you have been taught, “An eye for an eye and a tooth for a tooth.”
text: “Minɔ masɛ maɣɛ sɔ, ‘Si abiɛ fɔ ɔlaa inɔmi ne, maabiɛ fɔ ire, fiɛ si akpa fɔ ɔlaa inyɛ ne, maakpa fɔ ire fɔ wũ.’

ref: But I tell you not to try to get even with a person who has done something to you. When someone slaps your right cheek, turn and let that person slap your other cheek.
text: Ɣɛɛ kiniɔ ne, nto loɣere mi sɔ, daabara fɔ ɔlaa ikpi nɛ ɔbara-ɔ atikuso. Si ɔrere ɔpɛ-ɔ ɔrɔ̃ta i kuɖearɔ̃ kutsueti ne, finikira kunyɔare tã wũ si ɔpɛ.

ref: If someone sues you for your shirt, give up your coat as well.
text: Si ɔrere ɔbara-ɔ itɔ̃me sɔ si ɔfɔ fɔ awu wa apia asɛ karɔ̃ ne, ɖi nwa apia asia kato ana tã wũ.

ref: If a soldier forces you to carry his pack one kilometer, carry it two kilometers.
text: Ngbɔ ame si kpakpɛ̃dze ɔmɔɛ̃-ɔ sikitii sɔ soregu wũ ɔ̃ ato si asɛ igu iwɛ̃ ne, sɛgu wũ agu anyɔ.

ref: When people ask you for something, give it to them. When they want to borrow money, lend it to them. Love (Luke
text: Si ɔturi ɔkarɛ-ɔ ira ne, tã wũ, fiɛ si ɔrere ɔba sɔ ɔba ɔnyɛ ira i fɔ kɔrɛ ne, nyɛ tã wũ. Yesu Ɔte Ara I Matsirise Iɖɔɛ Iso.

ref: You have heard people say, “Love your neighbors and hate your enemies.”
text: “Misɛ minɔ maɣɛ sɔ, ‘Ɖɔɛ mma nto maɖɔɛ-ɔ si atsiri mma lotsiri-ɔ.’

ref: But I tell you to love your enemies and pray for anyone who mistreats you.
text: Ɣɛɛ kiniɔ ne, nto loɣere mi sɔ miɖɔɛ mma lotsiri mi si mikparama kayi i mma nto mati mi kama iti

ref: Then you will be acting like your Father in heaven. He makes the sun rise on both good and bad people. And he sends rain for the ones who do right and for the ones who do wrong.
text: si miakpese mi Ɔse gɔ i kato mabi. Alasɔ ɔ̃ nsɛ ɔtã kuɣɛ̃ ɔbɔrɛ kutã maturi sɛɛ gu malalaa ɔɖuɖu fiɛ ɔsɛ ɔtã kado ɔpɛ ɔtã maturi bɔlɔlɔa gu maturi nyanyarĩa.

ref: If you love only those people who love you, will God reward you for this? Even tax collectors love their friends.
text: Si misɛ miɖɔɛ mma nsɛ maɖɔɛ mi ɔnɔwɛ̃ ne, be kuso miafɔ i Ɣaa kɔrɛ? Ma lampofɔdze ma wũ masɛ mabara ngbɔ.

ref: If you greet only your friends, what's so great about this? Don't even unbelievers do that?
text: Fiɛ si fɔ malaa ɔnɔwɛ̃ asɛ aya ne, mme siba ipɛ aba gɔ mma loiɖe mafɔɖedze ma wũ masɛ mabara ngbɔ?

ref: But you must always act like your Father in heaven.
text: Ne ɔso milɛ miwiri lɛ kumɛgɔ i mi Ɔse gɔ i kato lɛ ɔwiri.

ref: When you do good deeds, don't try to show off. If you do, you won't get a reward from your Father in heaven.
text: “Minyɔ so kukaakɔ sɔ miibabara mi kasumu kubarara i ɔgbami sɔ maturi aanya. Alasɔ si mibara ngbɔ ne, mi Ɔse Ɣaa gɔ i kato siina kukuso ɔ̃atã mi.

ref: When you give to the poor, don't let anyone know about it.
text: Ne ɔso si abuai wɛrɛba ne, daatã nane fɔ ɔlaa gɔ lomatã-ɔ si ɔtsue nnɛ abara,

ref: When you pray, don't be like those show-offs who love to stand up and pray in the synagogues and on the street corners. They do this just to look good. I can assure you that they already have their reward.
text: “Fiɛ si mito mikparama kayi ne, midaase lɛ mma nsɛ mabara ara si mabo maturi anɔ. Masɛ mabie sɔ maata maya makparama kayi i kasɔrɛkɔ̃ gu kuɣa ame sɔ maturi si manya ma. Lobie sɔ lotã mi itsue sɔ ma kuso ame maɔfɔ mmɔ ne.

ref: When you pray, go into a room alone and close the door. Pray to your Father in private. He knows what is done in private and will reward you.
text: Ɣɛɛ fɔ si ato akparama kayi ne, kɛlɛ fɔ iyo kame si asɛ̃ iyo apia so fiɛ aakpere fɔ Ɔse gɔ maisɛ manya ala anɔmi, iyɔ gɔ ɔ̃ ɔsɛ ɔnya ta awɛrɛra ne, ɔto ɔ̃atã-ɔ kuso.

ref: When you pray, don't talk on and on as people do who don't know God. They think God likes to hear long prayers.
text: “Fiɛ si ato akparama kayi ne, daaɣɛ atɔ̃me gbodzoo wa nna karɔ̃ ala ngbɔ i maɣɔsɔrɛdze sɛ mabara ku adzuni sɔ mmɔ i ma aɣɔ aakã ma atsue ne.

ref: Don't be like them. Your Father knows what you need even before you ask.
text: Midaase lɛ ma, ala mi Ɔse ka ɔɣe ara wa lonya mi koko fiɛ miakarɛ wũ.

ref: You should pray like this: Our Father in heaven, help us to honor your name.
text: Ne ɔso si mito mikparama kayi ne, miɣɛ sɔ, ‘Bo Ɔse gɔ i kato, tã marɔ fɔ iyere sɛɛ.

ref: Come and set up your kingdom, so that everyone on earth will obey you, as you are obeyed in heaven.
text: Fɔ sigara iɖe si siba i bo ndɛ̃. Maabara fɔ kuɖɔɛ i kayiiso lɛ kumɛgɔ masɛ mabara i kato.

ref: If you forgive others for the wrongs they do to you, your Father in heaven will forgive you.
text: “Si misu maturi akpi mitsɛ ma ne, mi Ɔse gɔ i kato ana to ɔ̃asu mi are ɔtsɛ mi.

ref: But if you don't forgive others, your Father will not forgive your sins. Worshiping God by Going without Eating
text: Ɣɛɛ si miisu maturi akpi mitsɛ ma ne, mi Ɔse ana iibasu mi are ɔtsɛ mi.” Yesu Ɔte Ara I Kanya Inyi Iso.

ref: Instead, comb your hair and wash your face.
text: Si mito minyi kanya ne, mifore anɔmi si mitara mi ati kukaakɔ miledza so.

ref: Then others won't know you are going without eating. But your Father sees what is done in private, and he will reward you. Treasures in Heaven (Luke
text: Ne loatã sɔ maturi iibatsue sɔ ato anyi kanya, ɣɛɛ fɔ Ɔse gɔ maisɛ manya, fiɛ ɔsɛ ɔnya ara wɛrɛa aatã-ɔ kuso. Kato Nɖe Ara Ka Kɔlakɔ̃ Sɛɛ.

ref: But more than anything else, put God's work first and do what he wants. Then the other things will be yours as well.
text: Ɣɛɛ nnɛ iso i mi anɔ si arɛtɛ nɖe sɔ Ɣaa si ɔɖe sigara i mi iso gu si mibara sibɔlɔlɔ ara iyɔ ɔto ɔ̃asu ara wagbe ɔɖuɖu ɔtã mi.

ref: Don't worry about tomorrow. It will take care of itself. You have enough to worry about today.
text: Ne ɔso midaamararã so i kɔrãkaɖe ga loisɛ̃ iso. Kɔrãkaɖe amɔrɛ̃ si ase ise ala nɔme are ɔɔfe isi.

ref: Don't store up treasures on earth! Moths and rust can destroy them, and thieves can break in and steal them.
text: “Midaakɔla karana mitã so i kayi gagbe ame ala ngbe ne, makorokpɛ to maanina kã ɣee kapɛ matɔ, fiɛ mayukukpe ana to maawo kukui ɔbiɛ mabo iyo mayu kã.

ref: Instead, store up your treasures in heaven, where moths and rust cannot destroy them, and thieves cannot break in and steal them.
text: Ɣɛɛ ne, mikɔla karana misɛ so i kato. Ala mmɔ ne, makorokpɛ iisɛ manina ara, kaisɛ kapɛ matɔ fiɛ mayukukpe ana iisɛ mabo i mmɔ mayu ara.

ref: Your heart will always be where your treasure is. Light (Luke
text: Alasɔ ngbegɔ i fɔ karana se ne, mmɔ i fɔ ɔtu ana aawe ne. Yesu Ɔte Sɔ Anɔmi Nɖe Kuturidziri Ikpawaĩ.

ref: But when your eyes are bad, everything is dark. If the light inside you is dark, you surely are in the dark. Money (Luke
text: Ɣɛɛ si fɔ anɔmi iikpa ne, fɔ kuturidziri ɔɖuɖu to kuase guduu. Ne ɔso si fɔ ikpawaĩ ɔgudu ne, fɔ idududu ɔwodoro gbaã! Yesu Ɔte Ara I Ɣaa Gu Karana Isiai Iso.

ref: You cannot be the slave of two masters! You will like one more than the other or be more loyal to one than the other. You cannot serve both God and money. Worry (Luke
text: “Kuwɛ̃ iibawo makɔse inyɔ ɔsumu. Si iiɖe ngbɔ ne, ɔto ɔ̃aɖɔɛ ɔwɛ̃ ɔtsiri ɔwɛ̃. Ɣee ɔ̃awarɛ ɔwɛ̃ ɔta ɔya ɔwɛ̃ iso. Miibawo Ɣaa gu karana ɔsɔrɛ.

ref: Don't worry and ask yourselves, “Will we have anything to eat? Will we have anything to drink? Will we have any clothes to wear?”
text: “Ne ɔso midaasɛ mimararã so sɔ ‘Be boaɖe? Be boanɛ? Be boapia?’

ref: I tell you not to worry about your life. Don't worry about having something to eat, drink, or wear. Isn't life more than food or clothing?
text: Ne ɔso nto loɣere mi sɔ midaamararã so i mi ngbã, nnɛ miaɖe gu nnɛ mianɛ, ɣee mi sosina gu nnɛ miapia iso. Ɛɛbɛrɛ ngbã iifɔ kuɣa miɖo aɖera fiɛ kuturidziri iifɔ kuɣa kuɖo arapia?

ref: Look at the birds in the sky! They don't plant or harvest. They don't even store grain in barns. Yet your Father in heaven takes care of them. Aren't you worth much more than birds?
text: Minyɔ kato kurodzai gɔ loisɛ kukpɛ̃ fiɛ kuisɛ kusɔrɛ̃ ɔraɖui, kuna siwu ɣɛɛ mi Ɔse gɔ i kato sɛ ɔkararã kɔ̃! Miifɔ kuɣa miɖo kurodzai?

ref: Why worry about clothes? Look how the wild flowers grow. They don't work hard to make their clothes.
text: “Be ɔso mito mimararã so i nnɛ miapia iso? Minyɔ ipo ame adziri kumɛgɔ asɛ amɔ. Aisɛ ata ɖɔti ɣee awe sikati.

ref: But I tell you that Solomon with all his wealth wasn't as well clothed as one of them.
text: Ɣɛɛ nto loɣere mi sɔ Igara Salomo kɔra si ɔkɛrɛ so ne, ɔ̃isɛ ɔlɛ ɔnyɔ lɛ kudziri gɔ lofere aɣɔ̃la.

ref: God gives such beauty to everything that grows in the fields, even though it is here today and thrown into a fire tomorrow. God will surely do even more for you! Why do you have such little faith?
text: Ɣaa nsɛ ɔkɛrɛ sikua ngbɔ. Sikua dzɛ mpia nɔme kɔrãkaɖe maɔkpɛ̃ sɛ̃ mafere i ɔtɔ ame matsuedza. Si Ɣaa to ɔnyɔ sikua iso ngbɔ ne, ɔ̃ibabara ɔtã mi ma loɖo sikua? Be ɔso i mi kafɔkaɖe ɔfoforo ngbɔ?

ref: Only people who don't know God are always worrying about such things. Your Father in heaven knows you need all of these.
text: Ara wagbe i maɣɔsɔrɛdze sɛ mabie ne, ɣɛɛ mi Ɔse gɔ i kato ɣe sɔ nwagbe ɔɖuɖu ɔnya mi.

ref: Don't condemn others, and God won't condemn you.
text: “Midaaɣɛgu maturi atɔ̃me si Ɣaa iibaɣɛgu mi atɔ̃me.

ref: God will be as hard on you as you are on others! He will treat you exactly as you treat them.
text: Alasɔ ɔri gɔ iso aɣɛgu fɔ ɔlaa atɔ̃me akigu ne, ɔ̃ ame iso i Ɣaa aɣɛgu-ɔ ne. Kaboũsuai ga igbã ako ala ara atã fɔ ɔlaa ne, kã ame igbã maasu mako matã-ɔ ne.

ref: You can see the speck in your friend's eye, but you don't notice the log in your own eye.
text: “Nda ato abara fiɛ ato anya iku nɛ mpia i fɔ ɔlaa inɔmi fiɛ aito abara kuira i idzirikpo nɛ mpia fɔ mɔmɔ inɔmi iso?

ref: How can you say, “My friend, let me take the speck out of your eye,” when you don't see the log in your own eye?
text: Nda aabara fiɛ aaɣere fɔ ɔlaa sɔ, ‘Tã loɖi-ɔ iku i inɔmi.’ ku ɔwi gɔ i idzirikpo pia-ɔ i inɔmi

ref: You're nothing but show-offs! First, take the log out of your own eye; then you can see how to take the speck out of your friend's eye.
text: Ladze! Ɖe katɔ̃ ɖi idzirikpo nɛ mpia i fɔ mɔmɔ inɔmi tɔtɔ fiɛ aanya kukaakɔ aɖi iku i fɔ ɔlaa inɔmi.

ref: As bad as you are, you still know how to give good gifts to your children. But your heavenly Father is even more ready to give good things to people who ask.
text: Si mi ma nɖe makpibaradze kɔra ɣe kumɛgɔ miatã mi mabi ara sɛɛ ne, iyɔ mi Ɔse gɔ i kato to ɔ̃atã mma nto makarɛ wũ ara sɛɛ ɔɖo.

ref: Would any of you give your hungry child a stone, if the child asked for some bread?
text: “Ɣese ɔwɛ̃ aasu ita ɔtã ɔ̃ ɔbi gɔ lokarɛ wũ aɖera?

ref: Ask, and you will receive. Search, and you will find. Knock, and the door will be opened for you.
text: “Mikarɛ ara si maatã mi ara, mibie ara si miana, mipɛ kukui kɔrɔ̃ si maasese mi iyo.

ref: Everyone who asks will receive. Everyone who searches will find. And the door will be opened for everyone who knocks.
text: Alasɔ ɔbiara gɔ nsɛ ɔkarɛ ira sɛ ɔfɔ. Ɔbiara gɔ nsɛ ɔbie ira sɛ ɔna ne, fiɛ ɔbiara gɔ nsɛ ɔkpura kukui kɔrɔ̃ ne, masɛ masese matã wũ.

ref: Would you give your child a snake if the child asked for a fish?
text: Ɣee ɔsu aɣɛ ɔtã ɔ̃ ɔbi si ɔkarɛ wũ ikpɛ?

ref: Treat others as you want them to treat you. This is what the Law and the Prophets are all about. The Narrow Gate (Luke
text: Ne ɔso ira biara nɛ miabie sɔ maturi si mabara matã mi ne, mi wũ mibara wã mitã ma. Nnɛgbe ame kere i Mose mmara gu Ɣaa kanyamaɖidze ara wa matsɛrɛ masɛ to ate ne. Miki I Kayogodɔ̃ Ga Lomɔmɔrĩ Ame.

ref: Anyone who hears and obeys these teachings of mine is like a wise person who built a house on solid rock.
text: “Ne ɔso ngɔ nsɛ ɔnɔ wũ itɔ̃me fiɛ ɔsɛ ɔbara i ne iso ne, ɔse lɛ nɔgbadze gɔ lotsue ɔ̃ iyo ɔsia i ita iso.

ref: But the gate to life is very narrow. The road that leads there is so hard to follow that only a few people find it. A Tree and Its Fruit (Luke
text: Ɣɛɛ kayogodɔ̃ ga nkpa ngbã mɛ loisɛ miro ame ne, kase mɔmɔrĩ, kã ɔri ana iso isɛ ba ɔle fiɛ maturi kɛkɛĩ pɛ nsɛ i ɔ̃ iso. Kudziri Biara Ba Kɔ̃ Abi.

ref: Watch out for false prophets! They dress up like sheep, but inside they are wolves who have come to attack you.
text: “Minyɔ so i mila Ɣaa kanyamaɖidze ma nsɛ maba mi kɔrɛ iso. I anɔmi ame ne, manyɔ lɛ masɛrɛrerɔ̃, ɣɛɛ aketeke maɖe.

ref: You can tell what they are by what they do. No one picks grapes or figs from thornbushes.
text: Ma nsɛgbai miasu minya ma miɖi ite. Kuwɛ̃ iisɛ ɔtɔtɔrɛ figi abi i miyu kudziri iso, fiɛ maisɛ matɔtɔrɛ adziribi ɖea i itongbã iso.

ref: A good tree produces good fruit, and a bad tree produces bad fruit.
text: Ne ɔso ne, kudziri sɛɛ ne, abi sɛɛ kusɛ kuɣɔ̃ fiɛ kudziri lalaa ana ne, abi lalaa kusɛ kuɣɔ̃.

ref: A good tree cannot produce bad fruit, and a bad tree cannot produce good fruit.
text: Kudziri sɛɛ iibawo abi lalaa ɔɣɔ̃, fiɛ ngbɔ ame i kudziri lalaa iibawo abi sɛɛ ɔɣɔ̃ ne.

ref: Every tree producing bad fruit will be chopped down and burned.
text: Kudziri biara gɔ loisɛ kuɣɔ̃ abi sɛɛ ne, masɛ matu kɔ̃ mapɛyu matsuedza.

ref: You can tell who the false prophets are by their deeds. A Warning (Luke
text: Ne ɔso mila kanyamaɖidze ne, ma nsɛgbai miasu minya ma miɖi ite. Mma Igbã Loakɛlɛ Ɣaa Sigarakaɖekɔ̃.

ref: Anyone who hears my teachings and doesn't obey them is like a foolish person who built a house on sand.
text: Ɣɛɛ ɔbiara gɔ nsɛ ɔnɔ wũ itɔ̃me fiɛ ɔ̃isɛ ɔbara i ne iso ne, ɔse lɛ dzimifɔ gɔ lotsue ɔ̃ iyo ɔsia i iwarĩ iso.

ref: Rain poured down, rivers flooded, and the winds blew and beat against that house. Finally, it fell with a crash.
text: Kado ɔpɛ, siwore ɔyi, awɛ ɔkpɛ̃ amɔɛ̃ iyo fiɛ irɔ̃ teteree ibiɛbiɛ.” Yesu Ɔte Ara Gu Ɔle.

ref: Not everyone who calls me their Lord will get into the kingdom of heaven. Only the ones who obey my Father in heaven will get in.
text: “Iiɖe ɔbiara gɔ nsɛ ɔɣere me sɔ, ‘Bosate, Bosate,’ loakɛlɛ Ɣaa sigarakaɖekɔ̃. Ɣɛɛ mma nto mabara wũ Ɔse gɔ i kato kuɖɔɛ loasɛ mmɔ.

ref: On the day of judgment many will call me their Lord. They will say, “We preached in your name, and in your name we forced out demons and worked many miracles.”

text: Mmɔ loaɣere ma sɔ, ‘Loiɣe mi kukakɔi! Mi ara lalaa mabaradze, mirui me i kɔrɛ.’ Yesu Ɔte Ara I Ayo Matsuedze Agbã Anyɔ Iso.

ref: Rain poured down, rivers flooded, and winds beat against that house. But it was built on solid rock, and so it did not fall.
text: Kado ɔpɛ, siwore ɔyi, awɛ ɔkpɛ̃ teteree ɣɛɛ iyo iirɔ̃ ala mapia ne karɔ̃ ame kukaakɔ.

text: Ɣɛɛ mma nɖe sigarakaɖekɔ̃ mabi ne, maafuĩdza ma mapia i idududu nɛ mpia i kayoma ame. Mmɔ i kaku gu inyɛwe aawe ne.”

ref: That evening many people with demons in them were brought to Jesus. And with only a word he forced out the evil spirits and healed everyone who was sick.
text: Ne kutsuɛ ne, makɔ maturi gbodzoo ma i siwarã lalaa si i iso mabɔ Yesu. Ɔsu itɔ̃me iwɛ̃ ɔsa siwarã lalaa ɔɖi i ma ame fiɛ ɔsa mma ɔɖuɖu nto manyɛ.

ref: Suddenly a man with leprosy came and knelt in front of Jesus. He said, “Lord, you have the power to make me well, if only you wanted to.”
text: Ne ɔrɛrɛ̃ ɔwɛ̃ gɔ nto ɔnyɛ ɔnyɛ rɛtɛa ɔba ɔ̃apɛ agɛgɛ̃ i ɔ̃ katɔ̃ ɔɣere wũ sɔ, “Bosate! Si aɖɔɛ ne, aawo me ɔsa lose sekelee.”

ref: Jesus put his hand on the man and said, “I want to! Now you are well.” At once the man's leprosy disappeared.

ref: “I will go and heal him,” Jesus replied.
text: Yesu Ɔɣere wũ sɔ, “Loaba loasa wũ.”

ref: When Jesus heard this, he was so surprised that he turned and said to the crowd following him, “I tell you in all of Israel I've never found anyone with this much faith!
text: Gɔ Yesu ɔnɔ atɔ̃me wagbe ne, ibara wũ ɣaa, ne ɔɣere mma nsiai wũ sɔ, “I ɔnukuare ame ne, loinya kafɔkaɖe siare gagbe igbã lonya i Israel karɔ̃ ɔɖuɖu iso.

ref: “Lord, my servant is at home in such terrible pain that he can't even move.”
text: “Bosate! Wũ ɔɖabo rɛ ɔto ɔnyɛ, ɔ̃ito ɔwo ɔta gɔ so to sibo wũ kukaakɔ.”

ref: Many people will come from everywhere to enjoy the feast in the kingdom of heaven with Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.
text: Nto loɣere mi sɔ maturi gbodzoo to maabɔrɛ i kuɣɛ̃kabɔrɛkɔ̃ gu kɔ̃ iyo kabokɔ̃ maba maasɛ i ɔpɔrɔ̃ katɔ̃ maɖe ara ku Abraham gu Isak gu Yakob i Ɣaa sigarakaɖekɔ̃.

ref: Then Jesus said to the officer, “You may go home now. Your faith has made it happen.” Right then his servant was healed. Jesus Heals Many People (Mark
text: Ne Yesu ɔɣere kpakpɛ̃dzekpakpa gɔmɔ sɔ, “Kɛlɛ! Iaba itã-ɔ lɛ kumɛgɔ afɔ aɖe awe.” Ɔwi gɔmɔ ame ame i iyo ne, ɔɖabo ɔna isosarɛ. Yesu Ɔsa Maturi Gbodzoo.

ref: Suddenly they shouted, “Jesus, Son of God, what do you want with us? Have you come to punish us before our time?”
text: Ɔluwɛ̃ ne, mala kulu maɣɛ sɔ, “Fɔ Ɣaa Ɔbi, be mpia i bo gu-ɔ ndɛ̃? Ɔba ɔɔba sɔ aba aakpadzɛ̃ bo kutsue aɖegu ɔwi gɔ masɛ katɔ̃?”

text: Nnɛ ɔbara ngbe ɔtã sɔ nnɛ i Ɣaa Kanyaɖidze Yesaya ɔɣɛ ɔsɛ ɔba i ne ame sɔ, “Ɔ̃ mɔmɔ ɔɖu iti i bo isobo kayirinɔ, fiɛ ɔsa bo sinyɛ ɔɖuɖu.” Maturi Inyɔ Mawɛ̃ Ɔbie Sɔ Maasiai Yesu.

ref: so the disciples went over to him and woke him up. They said, “Lord, wake up! Save us before we drown!”

text: Yesu gu ɔ̃ marasuãdze ɔbo i ɔkolo ɔwɛ̃ ame marui.

text: Ɔluwɛ̃ ne, awɛ siare awɛ̃ ɔtsɛ ikpɛ̃ i itubu iso gɔ itã ndu to mita mibo i ɔkolo ame, ɣɛɛ Yesu rɛ ɔto ɔrɛ sirɛ.

ref: The men in the boat were amazed and said, “Who is this? Even the wind and the waves obey him.” Two Men with Demons in Them (Mark
text: Ibara ma ɔɖuɖu ɣaa, ne mato makarɛ so sɔ, “Mme ɔturi igbã ɔɖe ngbe fiɛ ta awɛ gu ndu to makã wũ atsue?” Yesu Ɔsa Maturi Inyɔ Ma I Siwarã Lalaa Si I Iso.

ref: After Jesus had crossed the lake, he came to shore near the town of Gadara and started down the road. Two men with demons in them came to him from the tombs. They were so fierce that no one could travel that way.
text: Gɔ Yesu ɔwara itubu ɔbo i Gadara ibuiti ame ne, maturi inyɔ ma ame i siwarã lalaa si i iso ɔbɔrɛ i ɔkpolo gɔ ame masɛ mabiara maturi maba maasarɛgu wũ. Maba silalaa sɔ kuwɛ̃ iisɛ ɔki i ndɛ̃ mmɔ ɔfe.

text: Yesu ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Mikɛlɛ ma ame.” Ne ɔso sibɔrɛ sisɛ siabo i ma ame. Makparako ikuri ɔɖuɖu ɔtere kpitikpitikpiti maso kabemi mabo i itubu ame makpi.

ref: The people taking care of the pigs ran to the town and told everything, especially what had happened to the two men.
text: Mma nto manyɔ makparako iso ɔtere makɛlɛ ɔmagɛ̃ ame masɛ maaɣere ma magɛ̃se nnɛ ɔɖuɖu loba gu nnɛ loba i mma iso i siwarã lalaa si iso.

ref: Jesus got into a boat and crossed back over to the town where he lived.

ref: Jesus heard them and answered, “Healthy people don't need a doctor, but sick people do.

text: “Ngbɔ ame i kuwɛ̃ iisɛ ɔsu ndã ɣɛtɛ ɔfere i bɔi ɔko akpo kuere ame ne. Si abara ngbɔ fiɛ ndã ɣɛtɛ ɔtsɛ ifu ne, miatã sɔ akpo kuere aabiɛ gɔ ndã gu bɔi ɔko akpo ɔɖuɖu aanina. Ne ɔso ndã ɣɛtɛ ne, bɔi ɔko akpo ɣɛtɛ ame masɛ mafere me sɔ wã anyɔ aase kukaakɔ.” Yesu Ɔbuai Ɔrɔ̃go Ɔwɛ̃ Gu Yairo Ɔbi.

ref: News about this spread all over that part of the country. Jesus Heals Two Blind Men
text: Itɔ̃me nɛgbe ɔki ikã i karɔ̃ ndɛ̃ mɛmɔ ɔɖuɖu iso. Yesu Ɔsa Manɔbiɛdze Inyɔ Mawɛ̃.

ref: Some teachers of the Law of Moses said to themselves, “Jesus must think he is God!”

ref: But I will show you that the Son of Man has the right to forgive sins here on earth.” So Jesus said to the man, “Get up! Pick up your mat and go on home.”
text: Loate mi sɔ mme, Ɔturi Awune Ɔbi, ba ɔle i kayi gagbe ame sɔ loasu akpi lotsɛ.” Ne ɔso ɔɣere ngɔ lofɛfɛ̃ ɔrɛ sɔ, “Ta ya si asu fɔ ɔkala si akɛlɛ iyo.”

text: Ɔrɛrɛ̃ ɔta ɔya ɔrui ɔkɛlɛ iyo.

text: Misɛ mianyɔnyɔ itɔ̃me nɛ mpia i Ɔko Sekelea ame sɔ, ‘Nnyainyɔ nto lobie ne iiɖe mabɔi isu ifɛ̃ ara itã’ karɔ̃. Alasɔ loiba lokpere mma nsɛ mabu so maturi sɛɛ sɔ mafiniki, ɣɛɛ makpibaradze loba lokpere.” Makarɛ Yesu Ara I Kanya Inyi Iso.

ref: As Jesus and his disciples were on their way, some people brought to him a man who could not talk because a demon was in him.

ref: Later, Jesus and his disciples were having dinner at Matthew's house. Many tax collectors and other sinners were also there.

ref: Some Pharisees asked Jesus' disciples, “Why does your teacher eat with tax collectors and other sinners?”

ref: Jesus turned. He saw the woman and said, “Don't worry! You are now healed because of your faith.” At that moment she was healed.
text: Yesu ɔfiniki ɔnyɔ, ne ɔnya ɔrɔ̃go ne. Ɔɣere wũ sɔ, “Na ɔtu, wũ ɔbi, fɔ kafɔkaɖe ɔɔsa-ɔ.” Ɔluwɛ̃ ne, ɔna isosarɛ.

text: Nwagbe kama ne, Ndupiedze Yohanes marasuãdze ɔba Yesu kɔrɛ maba maakarɛ wũ sɔ, “Be ɔso i bo gu ma Farisise sɛ bonyi kanya ɔwi biara, ɣɛɛ fɔ marasuãdze iisɛ manyi?”

ref: Jesus answered: The friends of a bridegroom aren't sad while he is still with them. But the time will come when he will be taken from them. Then they will go without eating.
text: Yesu ɔtã ma mmuai sɔ, “Mibu sɔ ɔrɔ̃go kɔdze malaa aamararã so ayi kumɛgɔ i ɔrɔ̃go kɔdze pia i ma kɔrɛ? Ikpa sekelee sɔ maibabara ngbɔ! Ɣɛɛ iyi to iba gɔ mato maakɔ ɔrɔ̃go kɔdze maruigu i ma kɔrɛ. Ɔwi gɔmɔ mato maanyi kanya ne.

ref: No one uses a new piece of cloth to patch old clothes. The patch would shrink and tear a bigger hole.
text: “Kuwɛ̃ iisɛ ɔta ɔkati kuere ala kakatisɛi ɣɛtɛ, alasɔ si abara ngbɔ ne, kakatisɛi ɣɛtɛ gamɔ to kaakarĩ karui iyoi nɛ loabɛ iɖo iɖeakatɔ̃.

ref: She had said to herself, “If I can just touch his clothes, I will be healed.”
text: Ɔbara ngbɔ ala ɔɣere so sɔ, “Si lopɛgu ɔ̃ awu kutsueti ne, loto loana isosarɛ.”

ref: When Jesus went into the home of the official and saw the musicians and the crowd of mourners,
text: Ne Yesu ɔkɛlɛ tɔ̃ɖedze gɔmɔ iyo ne. Gɔ ɔnya kudadã mafɛ̃dze gu mma nto mabiɛ kaku gu kulu titiiti ne,

text: Ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Mibɔrɛ ne ɔbi iikpi, sirɛ kere ɔto ɔrɛ.” Gɔ ɔɣɛ ngbɔ ne, mama wũ.

ref: But after the crowd had been sent out of the house, Jesus went to the girl's bedside. He took her by the hand and helped her up.

ref: After Jesus had gone indoors, the two blind men came up to him. He asked them, “Do you believe I can make you well?” “Yes, Lord,” they answered.

ref: Jesus touched their eyes and said, “Because of your faith, you will be healed.”

ref: But they left and talked about him to everyone in that part of the country. Jesus Heals a Man Who Could Not Talk
text: Ɣɛɛ gɔ mabɔrɛ i mmɔ ne, mapɛ ne imomo i Yesu iso i karɔ̃ gamɔ ɔɖuɖu iso. Yesu Ɔsa Ɔmumu Ɔwɛ̃.

ref: But the Pharisees said, “The leader of the demons gives him the power to force out demons.” Jesus Has Pity on People
text: Ɣɛɛ ma Farisise ɔɣɛ sɔ, “Siwarã lalaa igara lotã wũ ɔle ɔto ɔsu ɔsa siwarã lalaa.” Maturi Ara Ɔɖo Yesu Kayiri.

ref: Jesus went to every town and village. He taught in their synagogues and preached the good news about God's kingdom. Jesus also healed every kind of disease and sickness.

ref: He said to his disciples, “A large crop is in the fields, but there are only a few workers.

text: Ne ɔso mitã kakpɛkɔ̃ Sate iti sɔ ɔsese marabaradze ɔ̃ kakpɛkɔ̃ ame.”

ref: Philip, Bartholomew, Thomas, Matthew the tax collector, James the son of Alphaeus, and Thaddaeus were also apostles.
text: gu Filipo gu Bartolomeo gu Toma gu lampofɔdze Mateo gu Yakobo gɔ nɖe Alfeo ɔbi gu Tadeo

text: Si makɔ mi makɛlɛgu atɔ̃mekaɣɛkɔ̃ ne, midaamararã so sɔ be miasɛ miaɣɛ ɣee nda miaɣɛ mikigu. Ɣɛɛ si ɔwi ɔɔɣo ne, Ɣaa to ɔ̃atã mi nnɛ miaɣɛ.

text: “Minyɔ! Nto lopia mi lɛ masɛrɛrerɔ̃ i magidi ndɛ̃. Ne ɔso mikpa anɔ lɛ maɣɛ, ɣɛɛ miɣɔ lɛ alɔpɔ.

ref: The others were Simon, known as the Eager One, and Judas Iscariot, who later betrayed Jesus. Instructions for the Twelve Apostles (Mark
text: gu Simon gɔ nsɛ ɔɖɔɛ ɔ̃ kaɖe gu Yuda Iskariot gɔ loaɖi Yesu ɔtã. Mikɛlɛ Miaɣɛ Itɔ̃me.

ref: Jesus sent out the twelve apostles with these instructions: Stay away from the Gentiles and don't go to any Samaritan town.
text: Yesu ɔpia marɛrɛ̃ iweo-inyɔ magbe ɔtã ma mmara sɔ, “Midaasɛ mma loiɖe ma Yudase karɔ̃ iso gu ma Samariase simagɛ̃ ame.

ref: And don't carry a traveling bag or an extra shirt or sandals or a walking stick. Workers deserve their food.
text: Midaasu marawãnyidze akpo, awu pia aka anyɔ, ndokota ɣee ɔsɛdziri i mi irisɛ ame. Ikote sɔ maatã rabaradze ɔ̃ aɖera.

ref: So when you go to a town or a village, find someone able and willing to have you as their guest and stay with them until you leave.
text: “Si mibo ɔmagɛ̃ ɣee kasɛkɔ̃ biara ame ne, mibie ngɔ lonɔ karɔ̃ sɔ ɔ̃afɔ mi. Mibo i ɔ̃ kɔrɛ si miwe i mmɔ gbɔgbɔɔgbɔ miarui.

ref: I promise you the day of judgment will be easier for the towns of Sodom and Gomorrah than for that town. Warning about Trouble (Mark
text: Nto losia ita lotã mi sɔ iase pɔkɔsɔɔ itã Sodom gu Gomora maturi ku atɔ̃me iɣɛyi iɖo ɔmagɛ̃ gɔmɔ maturi. Maturi To Maakpɛ̃gu Mi.

ref: Brothers and sisters will betray one another and have each other put to death. Parents will betray their own children, and children will turn against their parents and have them killed.
text: “Ɔwi gɔmɔ ame ne, maturi to maaɖi ma manyii matã sɔ maɖoe fiɛ mase ana to maaɖi ma mabi matã. Mabi to maata maya ma maɣese iso maɖi ma matã maɖoe.

ref: Watch out for people who will take you to court and have you beaten in their synagogues.
text: Minyɔ so kukaakɔ i maturi iso alasɔ mato maaɖi mi matã mamɔɛ̃ makɛlɛgu atɔ̃mekaɣɛkɔ̃, mato maapɛ mi i ma kusarɛkɔ̃.

ref: Because of me, you will be dragged before rulers and kings to tell them and the Gentiles about your faith.
text: Wũ ɔso ne, mato maamɔɛ̃ mi makɛlɛgu matɔ̃ɖedze gu magara katɔ̃ sɔ maɣɛgu mi atɔ̃me, ɣɛɛ miɣɛ Itɔ̃me Bielea i mma loiɖe ma Yudase katɔ̃.

ref: But you will not really be the one speaking. The Spirit from your Father will tell you what to say.
text: Alasɔ iiɖe mi loaɖe ika, ɣɛɛ mi Ɔse Siwarã loaɖe ika sikigu i mi iso.

ref: Everyone will hate you because of me. But if you remain faithful until the end, you will be saved.
text: Ɔbiara to ɔ̃atsiri mi ala wũ ɔso, ɣɛɛ ngɔ loaya teteree i kafɔkaɖe ame isɛ iabo kawirikɔ̃ ne, Ɣaa to ɔ̃atã wũ ngbã.

ref: When people mistreat you in one town, hurry to another one. I promise you before you have gone to all the towns of Israel, the Son of Man will come.
text: Si makpɛ̃gu mi i ɔmagɛ̃ ɔwɛ̃ ame ne, mitere mikɛlɛ ɔmama ame. Lobie sɔ lotã mi itsue sɔ miibaro karabara ɔbara i Israel simagɛ̃ ɔɖuɖu ame fiɛ Ɔturi Awune Ɔbi aakpese ɔba.

ref: Students are not better than their teacher, and slaves are not better than their master.
text: “Rasuãdze kuwɛ̃ iisɛ ɔɖo ɔ̃ ratedze, fiɛ ɔɖabo kuwɛ̃ ana iisɛ ɔɖo ɔ̃ ɔkɔse.

ref: It is enough for students to be like their teacher and for slaves to be like their master. If people call the head of the family Satan, what will they say about the rest of the family? The One To Fear (Luke
text: Ne ɔso ikote sɔ iawe rasuãdze ɔmɛrɛ̃ sɔ ɔ̃akotegu ɔ̃ ratedze, fiɛ ɔɖabo ɔ̃ wũ ne, iawe wũ ɔmɛrɛ̃ sɔ ɔ̃akotegu ɔ̃ ɔkɔse. Si mato makpere iyo ɔse sɔ Siwarã lalaa igara ne, mme iyere igbã maibakpere iyo maturi? Midaanigã Maturi.

ref: Don't be afraid of people. They can kill you, but they cannot harm your soul. Instead, you should fear God who can destroy both your body and your soul in hell.
text: Midaanigã mma loaɖoe siturina, ɣɛɛ maibawo ɔkala ɔɖoe. Ɣɛɛ minigã Ɣaa gɔ loawo siturina gu ɔkala ɔɖuɖu ɔwɔra i itɔkpe ame.

ref: Aren't two sparrows sold for only a penny? But your Father knows when any one of them falls to the ground.
text: Kapɛrɛ kere masɛ marɔdɛ̃ kunagini kunyɔ, ɣɛɛ maisɛ maɖoe kukawɛ̃ si mi Ɔse gɔ i Kato iitɔrã.

ref: Even the hairs on your head are counted.
text: Ɣɛɛ ta mi siwɛrɛ̃ dzɛ i mi ati iso ne, Ɣaa ɣe sɛ̃ kukame.

ref: So don't be afraid! You are worth much more than many sparrows. Telling Others about Christ (Luke
text: Ne ɔso midaanigã alasɔ mifɔ kuɣa miɖo kunagini. Idaakpɛ̃-ɔ Kunuarɛ Sɔ Aɖe Yesu Ɔre.

ref: If you tell others you belong to me, I will tell my Father in heaven you are my followers.
text: “Ngɔ loibakpɛ̃ kunuarɛ i wũ iso fiɛ ɔ̃aɣɛ i maturi katɔ̃ sɔ wũ ɔre ɔɖe ne, mme wũ loaɣɛ i wũ Ɔse gɔ i Kato kɔrɛ sɔ wũ ɔre ɔɖe.

ref: But if you reject me, I will tell my Father in heaven you don't belong to me. Not Peace, but Trouble (Luke
text: Ɣɛɛ ngɔ loasɛ̃ me i maturi katɔ̃ sɔ ɔ̃iɣe me ne, mme wũ loasɛ̃ wũ i wũ Ɔse gɔ i Kato kɔrɛ. Maturi To Maaɣɛɣɛ So.

ref: Don't think I came to bring peace to the earth! I came to bring trouble, not peace.
text: “Midaabu sɔ isobuɛ iɖe losu lobɔ kayiiso. Ooɣo! Iɣɛɣɛso iɖe lobɔ.

ref: I came to turn sons against their fathers, daughters against their mothers, and daughters-in-law against their mothers-in-law.
text: Mabirɛrɛĩ to maata maya ma mase iso, fiɛ mabirɔ̃gomi ana to maata maya ma manyi iso. Mabi mare to maata maya i ma marã manyi iso.

ref: Your worst enemies will be in your own family.
text: Ɔturi mɔmɔ iyo maturi loaɖe ɔ̃ matsirise.

ref: If you love your father or mother or even your sons and daughters more than me, you are not fit to be my disciples.
text: “Ɔbiara gɔ loaɖɔɛ ɔ̃ ɔse ɣee ɔ̃ ɔnyi ɔɖo me iibawo wũ rasuãdze ɔɖe. Ngbɔ ame ngɔ nsɛ ɔɖɔɛ ɔ̃ ɔbi birɛrɛĩ ɣee ɔ̃ ɔbi birɔ̃gomi ɔɖo me iibawo wũ rasuãdze ɔɖe ne.

ref: And unless you are willing to take up your cross and follow me, you are not fit to be my disciples.
text: Ngɔ loisore ɔ̃ inyɛwe ɔnyigbã ɔsiai wũ nsɛgbai ne, ɔ̃ibawo wũ rasuãdze ɔɖe.

ref: If you try to save your life, you will lose it. But if you give it up for me, you will surely find it. Rewards (Mark
text: Ɔbiara gɔ loabie sɔ ɔ̃akɔkɔ ɔ̃ ngbã to ɔ̃ayura me, ɣɛɛ ɔbiara gɔ loasu ɔ̃ ngbã ɔtã ala wũ ɔso to ɔ̃ana ngbã. Ɣaa To Ɔ̃atã Ɔbiara Gɔ Lofɔ Yesu Masiaidze Kuso.

ref: Anyone who welcomes a prophet, just because that person is a prophet, will be given the same reward as a prophet. Anyone who welcomes a good person, just because that person is good, will be given the same reward as a good person.
text: Ɔbiara gɔ loafɔ Ɣaa kanyaɖidze ne, ɔto ɔ̃ana kaɖekɔ̃ i kanyaɖidze kuso ame. Ngɔ lofɔ ɔturi bɔlɔlɔa ala ɔ̃ sibɔlɔlɔ ɔso ne, ɔto ɔ̃afɔ sibɔlɔlɔ kuso.

ref: Anyone who welcomes you welcomes me. And anyone who welcomes me also welcomes the one who sent me.
text: “Ɔbiara gɔ loafɔ mi ne, mme iɖe ɔfɔ fiɛ ngɔ lofɔ me ne, ɔfɔ ngɔ lopia me katɔ̃me.

ref: And anyone who gives one of my most humble followers a cup of cool water, just because that person is my follower, will be rewarded.
text: Mitã ikpa mi sekelee sɔ ɔrere gɔ loatã wũ masiaidze koso magbe ndu yululua ikɔi ala wũ ɔso ne, ɔto ɔ̃afɔ ne kuso kokooko.”

ref: As John's followers were going away, Jesus spoke to the crowds about John: What sort of person did you go out into the desert to see? Was he like tall grass blown about by the wind?
text: Ɔwi gɔ mma i Yohanes ɔpia katɔ̃me ɔɔkpese mato makɛlɛ ne, Yesu ɔtsɛ ika iɖe i ɔ̃ iso iɣere mma losakanya i mmɔ sɔ, “Ɔwi gɔ mibɔrɛ mikɛlɛ Yohanes kɔrɛ i fafuĩ ame ne, be iɖe minyɔ ɔri sɔ mianya? Iyata nɛ i ɔwiri to ɔtidza?

ref: John the Baptist did not go around eating and drinking, and you said, “That man has a demon in him!”
text: Gɔ Yohanes ɔba ɔ̃ito ɔɖe ara ɔnɛ ndã mɛ loamɔɛ̃ ne, maturi sɔ siwarã lalaa mpia i ɔ̃ ame.

ref: After Jesus had finished instructing his twelve disciples, he left and began teaching and preaching in the towns.
text: Ɔwi gɔ i Yesu ɔɔro ɔ̃ marasuãdze katɔ̃me ɔpia ne, ɔrui i mmɔ ɔkɛlɛ sɔ ɔ̃ate ara ɔɣɛ Ɣaa Itɔ̃me i simagɛ̃ dzɛ mpia i mmɔ ame.

ref: John was in prison when he heard what Christ was doing. So John sent some of his followers
text: Gɔ ndupiedze Yohanes gɔ mamɔɛ̃ marara i iyo ɔnɔ ara wa i Kristo to ɔbara ne, ɔpia ɔ̃ marasuãdze mawɛ̃ Yesu kɔrɛ.

ref: to ask Jesus, “Are you the one we should be looking for? Or must we wait for someone else?”
text: Masɛ maakarɛ wũ sɔ, “Fɔ nɖe ngɔ maɣɛ sɔ ɔ̃aba ɛɛbɛrɛ bonyɔ ɔmama ɔri?”

ref: Jesus answered, “Go and tell John what you have heard and seen.
text: Ne Yesu to ɔtã ma mmuai sɔ, “Mikpese miaɣere Yohanes ara wa minya gu nwa minɔ.

ref: The blind are now able to see, and the lame can walk. People with leprosy are being healed, and the deaf can hear. The dead are raised to life, and the poor are hearing the good news.
text: Manɔbiɛdze to manya, mabafã to masɛ, mma nto manyɛ ɔnyɛ rɛtɛa iso to isekele, makpise to mata, fiɛ mato maɣere mawɛrɛba ana Itɔ̃me Bielea.

ref: God will bless everyone who doesn't reject me because of what I do.”
text: So aaɣɔ mma i wũ kurabarara iita kuya iso.”

ref: What kind of man did you go out to see? Was he someone dressed in fine clothes? People who dress like that live in the king's palace.
text: Be mibɔrɛ misɛ mianyɔ? Ngɔ mpia ɔgo awu? Mma mpia ɔgo awu ne, magara ayo mapia.

ref: What did you really go out to see? Was he a prophet? He certainly was. I tell you that he was more than a prophet.
text: Mibua miɣere me nnɛ misɛ mianyɔ. Ɣaa kanyaɖidze? Gbaã nto loɣere mi sɔ ɔɖo Ɣaa kanyaɖidze.

ref: I tell you no one ever born on this earth is greater than John the Baptist. But whoever is least in the kingdom of heaven is greater than John.
text: Gbaã nto loɣere mi sɔ Yohanes ɔɖo ɔturi igbã biara gɔ maɣe i kayi gagbe ame. Ɣɛɛ ngɔ lokoso ɔɖo i Ɣaa sigarakaɖekɔ̃ ɔɖo wũ.

ref: From the time of John the Baptist until now, violent people have been trying to take over the kingdom of heaven by force.
text: Ita i ɔwi gɔ i Ndupiedze Yohanes ɔtsɛ ɔ̃ itɔ̃me iɣɛ ipɛ i nɔme ne, maturi to makpɛ̃gu Ɣaa sigarakaɖekɔ̃, ɣɛɛ mma i anɔ ɔrɛtɛ mato mabie Ɣaa sigara iɖe kotoo loabo i mmɔ.

ref: And if you believe them, John is Elijah, the prophet you are waiting for.
text: Fiɛ si mibie sɔ miafɔ ma miɖe ne, minɔ sɔ Yohanes nɖe Eliya gɔ maɣɛ masɛ sɔ ɔ̃aba.

ref: You people are like children sitting in the market and shouting to each other,
text: “Kiniɔ ne, be loasu nɔme maturi lokateragu? Mase lɛ mabiɛtɛ ma nto maɣa kaɣa i kuɣa ame. Ma malaa to maɣere ma sɔ,

ref: “We played the flute, but you would not dance! We sang a funeral song, but you would not mourn!”
text: ‘Boka isoɣɔ aka botã mi, miitɔ, boɖi sikubiɛnɔ ana botã mi, mmɔ ana miibiɛ kaku.’

ref: But the Son of Man goes around eating and drinking, and you say, “That man eats and drinks too much! He is even a friend of tax collectors and sinners.” Yet Wisdom is shown to be right by what it does. The Unbelieving Towns (Luke
text: Ɣɛɛ gɔ mme, Ɔturi Awune Ɔbi, ɔba fiɛ loto loɖe ara lonɛ ndã ne, maturi to maɣɛ sɔ, ‘Minyɔ ɖeɖei gu tarɛ̃dze gɔgbe! Ɔɖe ma lampofɔdze gu makpibaradze ɔlaa.’ Ɣɛɛ ne, Ɣaa iwola sɛ ite so i ɔturi nsɛgbai ame.” Simagɛ̃ Siwɛ̃ Maturi Iifɔ Yesu Maɖe.

ref: In the towns where Jesus had worked most of his miracles, the people refused to turn to God. So Jesus was upset with them and said:
text: Simagɛ̃ dzɛ ame i Yesu ɔbara awawãra gbodzoo maturi iifiniki, ne ɔso ɔbiɛ ma ɔɣɛ sɔ,

ref: People of Capernaum, do you think you will be honored in heaven? You will go down to hell! If the miracles that took place in your town had happened in Sodom, it would still be standing.
text: Mi Kapernaum maturi ne, i mi adzuni ame ne, mito mibie sɔ miakɔlɛ so kato misɛ miabo kato, ɣɛɛ Ɣaa to ɔ̃abɔ mi karɔ̃ mikɛlɛ ta ikpibiɔ. Alasɔ si awawãra wa lobara i fɔ ame mabara i Sodom ne, tee ɔmagɛ̃ gɔmɔ se ipɛ i nɔme.

ref: So I tell you on the day of judgment the people of Sodom will get off easier than you. Come to Me and Rest (Luke
text: Nto loɣere-ɔ sɔ Ɣaa to ɔ̃anyɔ Sodom nnya ɔɖo-ɔ ku atɔ̃me iɣɛyi ɔɖo mi.” Yesu Ɔɣere Maturi Sɔ Maana Iwarã I Ɔ̃ Kɔrɛ.

ref: At that moment Jesus said: My Father, Lord of heaven and earth, I am grateful that you hid all this from wise and educated people and showed it to ordinary people.
text: Ɔwi gɔmɔ ame ne, Yesu ɔɣɛ sɔ, “Tete, Kato gu Karɔ̃ Sate, nto lole-ɔ sɔ ara wa lowɛrɛ manɔgbadze gu makotsuese ne, aɖi wã ate mabiɛtɛ.

ref: Yes, Father, this is what pleased you.
text: Alasɔ gbaã ne Tete, ngbɔ ikote-ɔ ne.

ref: My Father has given me everything, and he is the only one who knows the Son. The only one who truly knows the Father is the Son. But the Son wants to tell others about the Father, so they can know him too.
text: “Tete ɔsu ara ɔɖuɖu ɔpia me i nrɔɔ̃ ame. Kuwɛ̃kuwɛ̃ iiɣe Ɔbi iɖo Ɔse fiɛ kuwɛ̃ ana iiɣe Ɔse iɖo Ɔbi gu mma i Ɔbi ɔbie sɔ ɔ̃aɖi ɔ̃ Ɔse ɔte.

ref: If you are tired from carrying heavy burdens, come to me and I will give you rest.
text: “Mibore miba wũ kɔrɛ mi ma ɔɖuɖu nsore ato fiɛ iɔɖa mi si loatã mi iwarã.

ref: Take the yoke I give you. Put it on your shoulders and learn from me. I am gentle and humble, and you will find rest.
text: Misu wũ ɔnyigbã misia so iso si misuã ara mibɔrɛgu i wũ kɔrɛ si miana iwarã alasɔ wũ kayiri ɔɣɔ fiɛ lobɔ so karɔ̃.

ref: This yoke is easy to bear, and this burden is light.
text: Wũ ɔnyigbã se pɔkɔsɔɔ ku isore fiɛ wũ ato wa loasorera mi ana se foforoo.”

ref: Some Pharisees noticed this and said to Jesus, “Why are your disciples picking grain on the Sabbath? They are not supposed to do this!”
text: Gɔ ma Farisise ɔnya ne, maɣere Yesu sɔ, “Nyɔ, fɔ marasuãdze to mabara nnɛ i mmara iitã ɔri sɔ maabara ku Iwarãyi.”

ref: The crowds were so amazed they asked, “Could Jesus be the Son of David?”
text: Nnɛgbe ɔbara iturikuri nɛmɔ ɔɖuɖu ɣii, ne mato makarɛ so sɔ, “Iiɖe Igara David Ɔwa gɔ maɣɛ masɛ sɔ ɔ̃aba ɔɖe ngbe ɣɛɛ?”

ref: But when I force out demons by the power of God's Spirit, it proves that God's kingdom has already come to you.
text: Ɣɛɛ si Ɣaa Siwarã ɔle ame nto losa siwarã lalaa ne, mitsue kukaakɔ sɔ Ɣaa sigara ɔɖewi ɔɔba ɔ̃abo mi ndɛ̃.

ref: Haven't you read in the Law of Moses that the priests are allowed to work in the temple on the Sabbath? But no one says they are guilty of breaking the law of the Sabbath.
text: Ɛɛbɛrɛ miika i mmara ame sɔ masɔrɛdze ma nsɛ mabara karabara i isɔrɛyo sɛ matu Iwarãyi mmara ku Iwarãyi biara ɣɛɛ maiɖe ipɔ?

ref: He went into the house of God, and then they ate the sacred loaves of bread that only priests are supposed to eat.
text: Ɔbo i Ɣaa iyo ame ɔsɛ ɔ̃asu aɖera wa masu masɔrɛ Ɣaa maɖe gɔ iikote sɔ ɔ̃ gu ɔ̃ maturi aaɖe iɖo Ɣaa masɔrɛdze ɔnɔwɛ̃.

ref: Don't you know what the Scriptures mean when they say, “Instead of offering sacrifices to me, I want you to be merciful to others?” If you knew what this means, you would not condemn these innocent disciples of mine.
text: Ɣaa ɔɣɛ i Ɔko Sekelea ame sɔ, ‘Nnyainyɔ nto lobie ne iiɖe mabɔi ma mafɛ̃ ala ara matã me.’ Si ɔnɔ minɔ itɔ̃me nɛgbe karɔ̃ kukaakɔ ne, tee mma loiɖe ipɔ ne, miibatã ma ipɔ.

ref: Jesus left and went into one of their synagogues,
text: Gɔ Yesu ɔrui i mmɔ ne, ɔkɛlɛ ma Yudase kasarɛkɔ̃ kawɛ̃.

ref: Then Jesus told the man, “Hold out your hand.” The man did, and it became as healthy as the other one.
text: Ne Yesu ɔɣere ngɔ kɔrɔ̃ lokarĩ sɔ, “Nabarã fɔ kɔrɔ̃.” Ɔnabarã kɔ̃, ɔluwɛ̃ ne, ilɛ wũ kɔrɔ̃ karĩa ɔse lɛ ngɔ nsarɛ.

ref: When Jesus found out what was happening, he left there and large crowds followed him. He healed all of their sick,
text: Gɔ Yesu ɔnɔ sɔ maɔbara adzuni sɔ maaɖoe wũ ne, ɔrui i mmɔ. Maturi gbodzoo ɔsiai wũ fiɛ ɔsa manyɛse ɔɖuɖu.

ref: but warned them not to tell anyone about him.
text: Ɣɛɛ ɔsɛ̃ ɔtã ma sɔ madaaɖi wũ mate ngɔ ɔɖe.

ref: So God's promise came true, just as Isaiah the prophet had said,
text: Ɔbara ngbɔ, ne ɔso Ɣaa itɔ̃me nɛ maɣɛ i Kanyaɖidze Yesaya ɔko ame aaba i ne ame gbaã sɔ,

ref: “Here is my chosen servant! I love him, and he pleases me. I will give him my Spirit, and he will bring justice to the nations.
text: “Wũ ɔɖabo gɔ loɖi lotã so i ngbe ne. Ɔmɔɛ̃ me ɔtu fiɛ loipia wũ kuɣɛ. Loafedza wũ Siwarã lofere wũ iso sɔ ɔ̃apɛ wũ atɔ̃me iɣɛgu imomo ɔtã abuiti ɔɖuɖu.

ref: If you are not on my side, you are against me. If you don't gather in the harvest with me, you scatter it.
text: “Ngɔ loiɣɛ me i kama ne, ɔta ɔɣɛ me iso, fiɛ ngɔ loito ɔsagu me ara kanya ne, ara ɔto ɔsamarã.

ref: Some people brought to Jesus a man who was blind and could not talk because he had a demon in him. Jesus healed the man, and then he was able to talk and see.
text: Mawɛ̃ ɔkɔ ɔrɛrɛ̃ ɔwɛ̃ gɔ ame i siwarã lalaa pia fiɛ ɔɖe nɔbiɛdze gu ɔmumu mabɔ Yesu kɔrɛ. Yesu Ɔsa wũ ne ɔso ɔtsɛ ika iɖe fiɛ ɔtsɛ ara inya ana.

ref: Jesus knew what they were thinking, so he said to them: Any kingdom where people fight each other will end up ruined. And a town or family that fights will soon destroy itself.
text: Yesu ɣe ma adzuni, ne ɔso ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Karɔ̃ biara ga maturi loɣɛɣɛ so fiɛ mata maɣɛ so iso aakpese ikpakpi. Ngbɔ ame i ɔmagɛ̃ ɣee iyo biara nɛ maturi loɣɛɣɛ so fiɛ mata maɣɛ so iso iibawo ɔya ne.

ref: If I use the power of Beelzebul to force out demons, whose power do your own followers use to force them out? Your followers are the ones who will judge you.
text: Si mi sɔ Beelzebul lotã me ɔle fiɛ nto losa siwarã lalaa ne, iyɔ nna ɔle iso i mi masiaidze to maki mato masa sɛ̃? Nnɛ i mi masiaidze to mabara loatã mi ipɔ.

ref: When an evil spirit leaves a person, it travels through the desert, looking for a place to rest. But when the demon doesn't find a place,
text: “Si masa siwarã lalaa maɖi i ɔturi ame ne, sisɛ sikɛlɛ siawe siki sibie kawekɔ̃ i kakorekɔ̃. Si siina kukakɔi ne,

ref: it goes off and finds seven other evil spirits even worse than itself. They all come and make their home there, and the person ends up in worse shape than before. That's how it will be with you evil people of today. Jesus' Mother and Brothers (Mark
text: Iyɔ siakpese sikɛlɛ siakɔ siwarã lalaa aka akɔdzɛ mama dzɛ lonyanyarĩ siɖo sɛ̃ sibua so iso. Maabo i ngɔmɔ ame mawe, ne ɔso ɔ̃ kawirikɔ̃ to kaanyanyarĩ kaɖo ɔ̃ karɔ̃kasɛkɔ̃. Ngbɔ tutuutu iase itã nɔme maturi ma lonyanyarĩ ngbe ne.” Mabe Nɖe Yesu Manyii Mɔmɔ?

ref: A good tree produces good fruit, and a bad tree produces bad fruit. You can tell what a tree is like by the fruit it produces.
text: “Si kudziri lɛ ne, kɔ̃ abi ana sɛ alɛ, ngbɔ ame si kudziri iilɛ ne, kɔ̃ abi ana iisɛ alɛ. Abi wa i kudziri ɔɣɔ̃ nsɛ iɖi ite kudziri gɔ igbã kuɖe.

ref: You are a bunch of evil snakes, so how can you say anything good? Your words show what is in your hearts.
text: Mi ma lonyanyarĩ lɛ maɣɛ! Nda miabara fiɛ miawo ara sɛɛ ɔɣɛ gɔ miɖe maturi lalaa? Alasɔ nnɛ loyi ɔturi ɔtu ame i kanya sɛ kaɣɛ.

ref: I promise you on the day of judgment, everyone will have to account for every careless word they have spoken.
text: Nto lotã mi itsue sɔ ɔbiara to ɔ̃atã itsuɛpɛ̃ biara nɛ ɔtua ɔsɛ kere kuso ku atɔ̃me iɣɛyi iso.

ref: On that day they will be told they are either innocent or guilty because of the things they have said. A Sign from Heaven (Mark
text: Alasɔ fɔ mɔmɔ kanya ame atɔ̃me maasu matã-ɔ ibɛ ɣee ipɔ.” Yesu Ɔsɛ̃ Sɔ Ɔ̃ito Ɔbara Iwawãra Kuiwɛ̃ Ɔte.

ref: Some Pharisees and teachers of the Law of Moses said, “Teacher, we want you to show us a sign from heaven.”
text: Mmara matedze mawɛ̃ gu ma Farisise mawɛ̃ ɔɣere wũ sɔ, “Ratedze, boto bobie sɔ bara iwawãra irere si bofɔ-ɔ boɖe.”

ref: But Jesus replied: You want a sign because you are evil and won't believe! But the only sign you will get is the sign of the prophet Jonah.
text: Ne Yesu ɔtã ma mmuai sɔ, “Minyɔ kumɛgɔ i kiniɔgbe maturi ba ɔtile fiɛ maisɛ manigã Ɣaa. Mato mabie iwawãra sɔ maanya, ɣɛɛ miibanya kuiwɛ̃ iɖo Ɣaa kanyaɖidze Yona ire igbã.

ref: He was in the stomach of a big fish for three days and nights, just as the Son of Man will be deep in the earth for three days and nights.
text: Kumɛgɔ ame i Yona ɔwe i ikpɛ iyiri ame akpɛnɛ atɛ kasɛ̃ aka atɛ ne, ngbɔ ame i Ɔturi Awune Ɔbi ana aawe i karɔ̃ ame akpɛnɛ atɛ kasɛ̃ aka atɛ ne.

ref: On the day of judgment the people of Nineveh will stand there with you and condemn you. They turned to God when Jonah preached, and yet here is something far greater than Jonah.
text: I atɔ̃me iɣɛyi ne, Niniwe maturi to maatã mi ipɔ alasɔ mafiniki gɔ Yona ɔɣere ma Ɣaa itɔ̃me. Ɣɛɛ lobie sɔ mitsue sɔ ngɔ loɖo Yona pia i mi ndɛ̃ ngbe.

ref: The Queen of the South will also stand there with you and condemn you. She traveled a long way to hear Solomon's wisdom, and yet here is something much greater than Solomon. Return of an Evil Spirit (Luke
text: Atɔ̃me iɣɛyi ne, Igararɔ̃go gɔ lobɔrɛ i bo karɔ̃ kala isɛ to ɔ̃ata ɔya ɔtã mi kiniɔgbe maturi ipɔ alasɔ ɔsɛ ɔri dzoroo ɔbɔrɛ i kayi iti tsɔra ɔba ɔ̃akã Salomo atɔ̃me wa ame i inɔgba pia atsue. Ɣɛɛ nto loɣere mi sɔ ngɔ inɔgba loɖo Salomo ire pia i mi ndɛ̃ ngbe. Siwarã Lalaa Aakpese Siba Siabie Kawekɔ̃.

ref: it says, “I will go back to the home I left.” When it gets there and finds the place empty, clean, and neat,
text: sisɛ siɣere so sɔ, ‘Loakpese lokɛlɛ iyo nɛ ame masa me maɖi.’ Siaba sianya sɔ mmɔ se ikunu, maɔfiɛ iyo ame, maledza ira biara isɛ kukaakɔ.

ref: While Jesus was still speaking to the crowds, his mother and brothers came and stood outside because they wanted to talk with him.
text: Ɔwi gɔ i Yesu to ɔɣɛ nwagbe ne, ɔ̃ ɔnyi gu manyiibi ɔba maɣɛ i inyamɔ mato mabie sɔ maɖegu wũ ika.

ref: Someone told Jesus, “Your mother and brothers are standing outside and want to talk with you.”
text: Ne ɔso mma nɣɛ i mmɔ ɔwɛ̃ ɔɣere wũ sɔ, “Nyɔ, fɔ ɔnyi gu manyiibi ɣɛ i inyamɔ mato mabie sɔ manya-ɔ.”

ref: Jesus answered, “Who is my mother and who are my brothers?”
text: Yesu ɔtã mmuai sɔ, “Nna nɖe wũ ɔnyi gu manyiibi?”

ref: Then he pointed to his disciples and said, “These are my mother and my brothers!
text: Ne ɔsɔ̃ ɔ̃ marasuãdze kɔrɔ̃ ɔɣɛ sɔ, “Minyɔ wũ ɔnyi gu manyiibi!

ref: Anyone who obeys my Father in heaven is my brother or sister or mother.”
text: Ɔbiara gɔ nsɛ ɔbara Tete gɔ i Kato kuɖɔɛ ne, ɔ̃ nɖe wũ ɔnyiibi rɛrɛ̃ gu ɔnyiiko gu wũ ɔnyi ne.”

ref: Such large crowds gathered around him that he had to sit in a boat, while the people stood on the shore.
text: Gɔ mma loki malɔ wũ ɔsi ɔso ne, ɔya ɔbo i ɔkolo ame i ndu iso fiɛ maturi ka maɣɛ i ɔkpokpo.

ref: Then he taught them many things by using stories. He said: A farmer went out to scatter seed in a field.
text: Ɔte ma ara gbodzoo i agbã ame. Ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Kpɛ̃dze ɔwɛ̃ ɔsɛ ɔraɖui kasakɔ̃.

ref: Other seeds fell on thin, rocky ground and quickly started growing because the soil wasn't very deep.
text: Ɔwɛ̃ ɔɣo i ata iso ngbegɔ i sise iisi, ne ɔso ɔre mala.

ref: Some other seeds fell where thornbushes grew up and choked the plants.
text: Ɔraɖui ɔwɛ̃ ana ɔɣo i kuposɛ̃ ame, ne ɔso gɔ ɔre ne, kuɣa kufu wũ iso.

ref: Jesus' disciples came to him and asked, “Why do you use stories to speak to the people?”
text: Ne marasuãdze ɔba maakarɛ Yesu sɔ, “Be ɔso fiɛ asɛ asu agbã ate maturi ara?”

ref: Everyone who has something will be given more. But people who don't have anything will lose even what little they have.
text: Alasɔ ngɔ mba ara ne, maatã wũ ara ɔ̃ ara ɔsi abua, ɣɛɛ ngɔ ina ne, kɛkɛĩ gɔ ame ɔba ne, maamɔɛ̃ mafɔ wũ iso.

ref: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet Isaiah had said, “These people will listen and listen, but never understand. They will look and look, but never see.
text: Itã sɔ Kanyaɖidze Yesaya itɔ̃me nɛ ɔɣɛ ɔsɛ ɔba i ne ame sɔ, ‘Mmagbe to maanɔ ara, manɔ ara, ɣɛɛ maibanɔ karɔ̃! Mato maanyɔ ara, manyɔ ara, ɣɛ maibanya maɖi ite.

ref: Many prophets and good people were eager to see what you see and to hear what you hear. But I tell you they did not see or hear. Jesus Explains the Story about the Farmer (Mark
text: Nto loɣere mi sɔ ne, Ɣaa kanyamaɖidze gbodzoo gu maturi bɔlɔlɔa ɔbie teteree sɔ si manya ara wa mito minya, ɣɛɛ mainya, ɣee si manɔ ara wa mito minɔ, ɣɛɛ mainɔ.” Yesu Ɔɖi Ɔraɖuisadze Igbã Karɔ̃.

ref: The seeds that fell on rocky ground are the people who gladly hear the message and accept it at once.
text: Ɔraɖui gɔ loɣo i ata karɔ̃ iso se lɛ ngɔ lonɔ fiɛ ɔfɔ ne ku isoɣɔ ɔluwɛ̃.

ref: The seeds that fell along the road are the people who hear the message about the kingdom, but don't understand it. Then the evil one comes and snatches the message from their hearts.
text: Ngɔ nto lonɔ Sigarakaɖekɔ̃ iso itɔ̃me fiɛ ɔ̃ito ɔnɔ karɔ̃ se lɛ ɔraɖui gɔ loɣo i ɔri kuruɛ. Tsirise sɛ ɔba ɔ̃amɔɛ̃ itɔ̃me nɛ ɔnɔ ɔɖi i ɔ̃ ɔtu ame.

ref: The seeds that fell among the thornbushes are also people who hear the message. But they start worrying about the needs of this life and are fooled by the desire to get rich. So the message gets choked out, and they never produce anything.
text: Ɔraɖui gɔ loɣo i kuposɛ̃ ame nɖe ngɔ lonɔ itɔ̃me, ɣɛɛ imararãso gu karana itere isiai sɛ iɣa ifu itɔ̃me sɔ ɔ̃isɛ ɔwo abi ɔɣɔ̃.

ref: The seeds that fell on good ground are the people who hear and understand the message. They produce as much as
text: Ɔraɖui gɔ loɣo i karɔ̃ sɛɛ iso nɖe ngɔ lonɔ itɔ̃me fiɛ ɔnɔ ne karɔ̃. Ɔsɛ ɔɣɔ̃ abi, mawɛ̃ abi kulafa, mma sikuɔ fiɛ mawɛ̃ ana abi sitɛ.” Yesu Ɔpɛ Igbã I Sikua Iso.

ref: Jesus then told them this story: The kingdom of heaven is like what happened when a farmer scattered good seed in a field.
text: Yesu ɔpɛ ma igbã mama ana ɔɣɛ sɔ, “Ɣaa sigarakaɖekɔ̃ se lɛ ɔrɛrɛ̃ ɔwɛ̃ gɔ lopɛ ɔraɖui sɛɛ i ɔ̃ kakpɛkɔ̃ ame.

ref: But while everyone was sleeping, an enemy came and scattered weed seeds in the field and then left.
text: Kasɛ̃ gɔ maturi ka marɛ ne, ɔ̃ tsirise ɔsɛ ɔ̃asa sikua abi i ɔraɖui ame ɔledza ibɔrɛ malaa.

ref: When the plants came up and began to mature, the farmer's servants could see the weeds.
text: Ɔwi gɔ i kamɔ ɔmɔ fiɛ kaatsɛ iɣe ne, sikua dzɛ i tsirise ɔsa ana ɔre.

ref: The servants came and asked, “Sir, didn't you scatter good seed in your field? Where did these weeds come from?”
text: “Ɔrɛrɛ̃ gɔmɔ maɖabo ɔba maatu wũ maɣere wũ sɔ, ‘Bokɔse, iiɖe kamɔ sɛɛ kaɖe ako i kakpɛkɔ̃ ame? Lɛ i sikua dzɛgbe ɔse i kamɔ ame?’

ref: “An enemy did this,” he replied. His servants then asked, “Do you want us to go out and pull up the weeds?”
text: “Ne kakpɛkɔ̃ sate to ɔɣere ma sɔ, ‘Tsirise ɔɖe lobara karabara gagbe.’ “Ne maɖabo to makarɛ wũ sɔ, ‘Ato abie sɔ borurui sɛ̃ boɖi?’

ref: “No!” he answered. “You might also pull up the wheat.
text: “Ɔtã ma mmuai sɔ, ‘Ooɣo. Si misɛ miatsɛ sikua irurui ne, mito miarurui kamɔ ana mibua i sɛ̃ iso.

ref: Although it is the smallest of all seeds, it grows larger than any garden plant and becomes a tree. Birds even come and nest on its branches.
text: Idziribi nɛgbe ɔkoso iɖo atsɔra ɔɖuɖu, ɣɛɛ si imɔ ne, isɛ imɔ iɖo ayo ame kakpɛkɔ̃ ame siraɖui ɔɖuɖu ikpese kadzirimi ga akpa ame i kurodzai aabara ayo kuwe.” Yesu Ɔpɛ Igbã I Inyagɛ̃ Kua Iso.

ref: Jesus told them another story: The kingdom of heaven is like what happens when a farmer plants a mustard seed in a field.
text: Yesu Ɔledza ma igbã mama ipɛ iɣere ma sɔ, “Ɣaa sigarakaɖekɔ̃ se lɛ idziribi kosoi iwɛ̃ nɛ i ɔrɛrɛ̃ ɔwɛ̃ ɔsu ɔsɛ ɔ̃apɛ i ɔ̃ ayo ame kakpɛkɔ̃ ame.

ref: Jesus used stories when he spoke to the people. In fact, he did not tell them anything without using stories.
text: Yesu ɔki i agbã ame ɔte iturikuri nɛgbe ara wagbe ɔɖuɖu. Ɔ̃isɛ ɔɖe ma ika si iiɖe igbã ɔto ɔpɛ ma.

ref: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet had said, “I will use stories to speak my message and to explain things hidden since the creation of the world.” Jesus Explains the Story about the Weeds
text: Ɔbara ngbɔ gɔ itã sɔ nnɛ i Ɣaa kanyaɖidze ɔɣɛ ɔsɛ ɔba i ne ame sɔ, “Loaɖe ma ika i igbã ame. Fiɛ loaɖi ara wɛrɛa wa mpia ita i kayi karɔ̃kasɛkɔ̃ lote ma.” Yesu Ɔɖi Igbã Nɛ Ɔpɛ I Sikua Iso Karɔ̃.

ref: After Jesus left the crowd and went inside, his disciples came to him and said, “Explain to us the story about the weeds in the wheat field.”
text: Gɔ Yesu ɔɔɖi iturikuri nɛmɔ ɔri fiɛ ɔba iyo ne, ɔ̃ marasuãdze ɔba ɔ̃ kɔrɛ maba maaɣere wũ sɔ, “Ɖi igbã nɛ apɛ i sikua dzɛ mpia i ɔraɖui ame mmɔ karɔ̃ tã bo.”

ref: Jesus answered: The one who scattered the good seed is the Son of Man.
text: Yesu ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Ngɔ nto ɔpɛ ɔraɖui nɖe mme, Ɔturi Awune Ɔbi.

ref: The field is the world, and the good seeds are the people who belong to the kingdom. The weeds are those who belong to the evil one,
text: Kakpɛkɔ̃ nɖe kayiiso. Mma nɖe Ɣaa sigarakaɖekɔ̃ maturi nɖe ɔraɖui gɔ mapɛ i kakpɛkɔ̃ ame. Tsirise masiaidze nɖe sikua.

ref: and the one who scattered them is the devil. The harvest is the end of time, and angels are the ones who bring in the harvest.
text: Tsirise gɔ losa sikua nɖe Ɔbosam. Ɔraɖui ɔsɔrɛ̃wi nɖe kayi kawirikɔ̃, fiɛ mma loasɔrɛ̃ ɔraɖui nɖe Ɣaa makpabo.

ref: Weeds are gathered and burned. That's how it will be at the end of time.
text: “Kumɛgɔ masa sikua kanya fiɛ matsuedza sɛ̃ ne, ngbɔ ame iase ku kayi kawirikɔ̃ ne.

ref: The Son of Man will send out his angels, and they will gather from his kingdom everyone who does wrong or causes others to sin.
text: Mme, Ɔturi Awune Ɔbi, to loapia wũ makpabo sɔ masa mma ɔɖuɖu lokɔ maturi mapia i ikpi ame gu mma lobara ara lalaa kanya.

ref: Then he will throw them into a flaming furnace, where people will cry and grit their teeth in pain.
text: Mato maasu ma mafuĩdza mapia i itɔkpe nɛ nto itsue ame ngbegɔ maabiɛ kaku maɖo arɔĩ makpa.

ref: But everyone who has done right will shine like the sun in their Father's kingdom. If you have ears, pay attention! A Hidden Treasure
text: Ɣɛɛ Ɣaa maturi to maafiɛ lɛ kuɣɛ̃ i ma Ɔse sigarakaɖekɔ̃ ame. Ngɔ mba atsue si ɔnɔ nnɛ nto loɣɛ! Yesu Ɔpɛ Igbã I Karana Ga Makɔla Iso.

ref: The kingdom of heaven is like what happens when someone finds a treasure hidden in a field and buries it again. Such a person is happy and goes and sells everything in order to buy that field. A Valuable Pearl
text: “Ɣaa sigarakaɖekɔ̃ se lɛ karana ga makɔla i kakpɛkɔ̃ ame fiɛ ɔrɛrɛ̃ ɔwɛ̃ ɔnya ɔsu ɔledza isu ikɔla. Ɔna isoɣɔ gbaã, ne ɔso ɔsɛ ɔ̃asu ɔ̃ ira biara ɔrɔdɛ̃ ɔsu sikã ɔsɛ ɔ̃aɣa kakpɛkɔ̃ gamɔ.” Yesu Ɔpɛ Igbã I Kunyi Gɔ Lofɔ Kuɣa Iso.

ref: The kingdom of heaven is like what happens when a shop owner is looking for fine pearls.
text: Yesu ɔɣere ma ana sɔ, “Ɣaa sigarakaɖekɔ̃ se lɛ kuɣaɖese ɔwɛ̃ gɔ nto ɔbie anyi wa lofɔ kuɣa.

ref: After finding a very valuable one, the owner goes and sells everything in order to buy that pearl. A Fish Net
text: Gɔ ɔsɛ ɔ̃anya kuwɛ̃ gɔ lobua kufɔ kuɣa ne, ɔsɛ ɔ̃asu ɔ̃ ira biara nɛ ɔba ɔrɔdɛ̃ ɔsu sikã ɔsɛ ɔ̃aɣa kɔ̃. Yesu Ɔpɛ Igbã I Asabu Wa Mapia Iso.

ref: The kingdom of heaven is like what happens when a net is thrown into a lake and catches all kinds of fish.
text: “Ana ne, Ɣaa sigarakaɖekɔ̃ ɔre asabu wa mapia i ɔpo ame fiɛ maɖi akpɛ agbãagbã.

ref: When the net is full, it is dragged to the shore, and the fishermen sit down to separate the fish. They keep the good ones, but throw the bad ones away.
text: Gɔ asabu ɔyi ne, makpadzɛ̃ wã mabɔ ɔkpokpo fiɛ marɛrɛ akpɛ ame, maɖi nwa masɛ maɖe mafere i kusɛsɛi ame. Nwa maiɖe ana ne, mabodza wã mawiri.

ref: That's how it will be at the end of time. Angels will come and separate the evil people from the ones who have done right.
text: Ngbɔ ame iase ku kayi kawirikɔ̃ ne. Ɣaa makpabo to maabɔrɛ makɛlɛ maarɛrɛ maturi sɛɛ maɖi i malalaa ndɛ̃.

ref: Then those evil people will be thrown into a flaming furnace, where they will cry and grit their teeth in pain. New and Old Treasures
text: Mato maasu maturi lalaa mafuĩdza mapia i itɔkpe ame ngbegɔ maabiɛ kaku maɖo arɔĩ makpa.” Yesu Ɔte Ara I Ɔnukuare Atɔ̃me Kuere Gu Aɣɛtɛ Iso.

ref: Jesus asked his disciples if they understood all these things. They said, “Yes, we do.”
text: Yesu ɔkarɛ ma sɔ, “Minɔ ara wa loɣere mi i ngbe karɔ̃?” Matã mmuai sɔ, “Aĩ, bonɔ.”

ref: So he told them, “Every student of the Scriptures who becomes a disciple in the kingdom of heaven is like someone who brings out new and old treasures from the storeroom.” The People of Nazareth Turn against Jesus (Mark
text: Ne ɔɣere ma ana sɔ, “Mmaratedze biara gɔ lokpese Ɣaa sigarakaɖekɔ̃ rasuãdze ne, ɔse lɛ iyosate gɔ nsɛ ɔɖi ara kuere gu aɣɛtɛ ɔbɔrɛgu i ɔ̃ iyo ame ɔte.” Ma Nasaretse Ɔsɛ̃ Yesu.

ref: When Jesus had finished telling these stories, he left
text: Gɔ Yesu ɔpɛ agbã wagbe ɔro ne, ɔrui i mmɔ.

ref: and went to his hometown. He taught in their synagogue, and the people were so amazed that they asked, “Where does he get all this wisdom and the power to work these miracles?
text: Ɔkpese ɔkɛlɛ ma aɖe fiɛ ɔsɛ ɔ̃ate ara i ma kasarɛkɔ̃ gɔ ibara mma lonɔ ɔ̃ atɔ̃me ɣaa mato makarɛ sɔ, “Lɛ i ngɔgbe ɔsuã inɔgba nɛgbe ɔbɔrɛgu? Fiɛ lɛ ɔbɔrɛgu awawãra wagbe igbã?

ref: Isn't he the son of the carpenter? Isn't Mary his mother, and aren't James, Joseph, Simon, and Judas his brothers?
text: Iiɖe rawedze ɔbi ɔɖe ngbe ɣɛɛ? Iiɖe Maria ɔbi ɔɖe fiɛ ɔ̃ manyiibi nɖe Yakobo gu Yosef gu Simon gu Yuda ma mpia i bo ndɛ̃?

ref: Don't his sisters still live here in our town? How can he do all this?”
text: Ɔ̃ manyiiko ana pia i bo ndɛ̃ ni! Lɛ kowũ ɔna ara wagbe ɔɖuɖu ɔbɔrɛgu?”

ref: So the people were upset because of what he was doing. But Jesus said, “Prophets are honored by everyone, except the people of their hometown and their own family.”
text: Ɔ̃ ara ɔtsue ma anɔ, ne ɔso maifɔ wũ. Ne ɔso ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Ɣaa kanyaɖidze ba kuwarɛ i kakɔi biara iɖo ɔ̃ mɔmɔ ɔmagɛ̃ gu ɔ̃ maturi kɔrɛ.”

ref: And because the people did not have any faith, Jesus did not work many miracles there.
text: Gɔ maifɔ wũ maɖe ɔso ne, ɔ̃iwo awawãra gbodzoo ɔbara i ma ndɛ̃.

ref: When Jesus got out of the boat, he saw the large crowd. He felt sorry for them and healed everyone who was sick.
text: Gɔ Yesu ɔso ɔbɔrɛ i ɔkolo ame fiɛ ɔnya maturi gbodzoo ma loba ne, ma ara ɔɖo wũ kayiri fiɛ ɔsa mma nto manyɛ. Yesu Ɔtã Maturi Gbodzoo Aɖera Maɖe.

ref: “Come on!” Jesus said. Peter then got out of the boat and started walking on the water toward him.
text: Yesu ɔtã mmuai sɔ, “Ba!” Ne ɔso Petro ɔso ɔbɔrɛ i ɔkolo ame ɔtsɛ isɛ i ndu iso ikɛlɛ Yesu kɔrɛ.

ref: so much he swore to give her whatever she wanted.
text: Ne ɔkã ndamu ɔɣɛ sɔ, “Loatã-ɔ ira biara nɛ aakarɛ me.”

ref: and told his officials, “This is John the Baptist! He has come back from death, and that's why he has the power to work these miracles.”
text: Ne ɔto ɔɣere ɔ̃ marabaradze sɔ, “Ndupiedze Yohanes loɔta ɔbɔrɛ i makpise ame ɔpia ngbã! Ne ɔso ɔba ɔle ɔto ɔbara awawãra ɔbara ngbɔ ne.”

ref: But the girl's mother told her to say, “Here on a serving plate I want the head of John the Baptist!”
text: Ɔbirɔ̃gomi ɔnyi ɔɣere wũ sɔ ɔkarɛ Ndupiedze Yohanes iti. Ne ɔso ɔsɛ ɔ̃aɣere igara sɔ, “Tã me Ndupiedze Yohanes iti i kategelei iso ngbe kiniɔ.”

ref: It was taken on a serving plate to the girl, and she gave it to her mother.
text: Masu ituriti masia i kategelei iso masu mabɔ ɔbirɔ̃gomi fiɛ ka ɔsu ɔkɛlɛgu ɔ̃ ɔnyi.

ref: John's followers took his body and buried it. Then they told Jesus what had happened. Jesus Feeds Five Thousand (Mark
text: Gɔ Yohanes marasuãdze ɔnɔ ne, masɛ maasu wũ masɛ maabiara fiɛ ka masɛ maaɣere Yesu. Yesu Ɔtã Maturi Gbodzoo Aɖera Maɖe.

ref: But when Peter saw how strong the wind was, he was afraid and started sinking. “Save me, Lord!” he shouted.
text: Ɣɛɛ gɔ ɔnya kumɛgɔ i ɔwiri to ɔkpɛ̃ teteree ne, ɔnigã ɔtsɛ ndu iwã. Ne ɔla kulu teteree sɔ, “Bosate, ɖi me!”

ref: But they said, “We have only five small loaves of bread and two fish.”
text: Ne marasuãdze ɔɣere wũ sɔ, “Abolo abo aru gu akpɛ fɛ̃a anyɔ kere nɖe aɖera wa boba.”

ref: After Jesus heard about John, he crossed Lake Galilee to go to some place where he could be alone. But the crowds found out and followed him on foot from the towns.
text: Gɔ Yesu ɔnɔ nnɛ loba Yohanes iso ne, ɔbo i ɔkolo ame ɔwara ɔkɛlɛ ngbegɔ i maturi na. Ɣɛɛ gɔ maturi gbodzoo ɔtsue ne, masɛ ngba mabɔrɛ i ma simagɛ̃ ame makɛlɛ ɔ̃ kɔrɛ.

ref: Jesus replied, “They don't have to leave. Why don't you give them something to eat?”
text: Yesu ɔtã ma mmuai sɔ, “Iinya sɔ maakɛlɛ ne mi mɔmɔ mitã ma irere maɖe.”

ref: Jesus asked his disciples to bring the food to him,
text: Ne Yesu ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Misu mibɔ me i ngbe.”

ref: and he told the crowd to sit down on the grass. Jesus took the five loaves and the two fish. He looked up toward heaven and blessed the food. Then he broke the bread and handed it to his disciples, and they gave it to the people.
text: Ɔɣere maturi sɔ masɛ i karɔ̃ i sikua iso fiɛ ka ɔsu abolo abo aru gu akpɛ fɛ̃a anyɔ, ɔnyɔ kato ɔpɛ Ɣaa siba. Ɔbɛbɛse ɔsu ɔtã ɔ̃ marasuãdze sɔ masu maɣɛɣɛ maturi.

ref: After everyone had eaten all they wanted, Jesus' disciples picked up twelve large baskets of leftovers.
text: Gɔ ma ɔɖuɖu ɔɖe ara mami fiɛ marasuãdze ɔsa nwa lobuburi kanya ne, ayi kusɛsɛi kuweo-kunyɔ.

ref: At once, Jesus made his disciples get into a boat and start back across the lake. But he stayed until he had sent the crowds away.
text: Ɔluwɛ̃ Yesu ɔtã ɔ̃ marasuãdze ɔya mabo i ɔkolo ame sɔ mawara makɛlɛ si ɔ̃ wũ ɔɖi maturi tsɔra ɔri.

ref: Then he went up on a mountain where he could be alone and pray. Later in the evening, he was still there.
text: Gɔ ɔ̃ɔɖi ma ɔri ɔro ne, ɔya ɔkɛlɛ kabemi iso ɔsɛ ɔ̃akparama kayi. Si iabara kutsuɛ buɛɛ ne, Yesu ɔnɔwɛ̃ loɔkuti ɔɣɛ i mmɔ.
