ref: By this time the boat was a long way from the shore. It was going against the wind and was being tossed around by the waves.
text: Ɔwi gɔgbe ame ne, ɔkolo ka ɔsia i itubu ndɛ̃, awɛ to akpɛ̃ fiɛ ndu to misɛgu ɔkolo mikpesegu.

ref: A little while before morning, Jesus came walking on the water toward his disciples.
text: Ɔɖesɛrã ne, Yesu ɔsɛ i ndu iso ɔba ɔ̃atu ɔ̃ marasuãdze.

ref: When they saw him, they thought he was a ghost. They were terrified and started screaming.
text: Gɔ manya wũ ɔsɛ i ndu iso ne, ɔtu ɔkpa ma maɣɛ sɔ, “Kpise ɔɖe!” Mala kulu i ifɔ̃ ame.

ref: At once, Jesus said to them, “Don't worry! I am Jesus. Don't be afraid.”
text: Ɣɛɛ ɔluwɛ̃ ne, Yesu ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Mina ɔtu! Midaanigã. Mme loɖe!”

ref: Peter replied, “Lord, if it really is you, tell me to come to you on the water.”
text: Ne Petro ɔɣere wũ sɔ, “Bosate, si fɔ mɔmɔ aɖe ne, kpere me sɔ losɛ i ndu iso loba fɔ kɔrɛ.”

ref: At once, Jesus reached out his hand. He helped Peter up and said, “You surely don't have much faith. Why do you doubt?”
text: Ɔluwɛ̃ Yesu ɔɖi kɔrɔ̃ ɔmɔɛ̃ wũ ɔɣere wũ sɔ, “Fɔ kafɔkaɖe ɔtɛgɛlɛ, be ɔso fiɛ ato awararã?”

ref: When Jesus and Peter got into the boat, the wind died down.
text: Ma inyɔɔ ɔya mabo i ɔkolo ame kere ne ɔwiri ɔnyua ɔkpɛ̃ ne.

ref: The men in the boat worshiped Jesus and said, “You really are the Son of God!” Jesus Heals Sick People in Gennesaret (Mark
text: Ne ɔso marasuãdze ma i ɔkolo ame ɔpɛ agɛgɛ̃ matã Yesu maɣɛ sɔ, “Gbaã ne, Ɣaa Ɔbi aɖe!” Yesu Ɔsa Manyɛse I Genezaret.

ref: Jesus and his disciples crossed the lake and came to shore near the town of Gennesaret.
text: Gɔ mawara itubu ne, maba maabo ɔkpokpo gɔ i Genezaret karɔ̃ iso.

ref: The people found out he was there, and they sent word to everyone who lived in this part of the country. So they brought all the sick people to Jesus.
text: Maturi ɔnya Yesu maɖi ite, ne ɔso masɛ maakɔ manyɛse ma ɔɖuɖu mpia i abuiti wamɔ ame ɔɖuɖu mabɔ wũ.

ref: They begged him just to let them touch his clothes, and everyone who did was healed.
text: Mapɛ wũ nrɔɔ̃ sɔ ɔtã manyɛse si mapɛgu bidɛ ɔ̃ awu kutsueti. Mma ɔɖuɖu lopɛgu wũ ɔna isosarɛ.

ref: Jesus answered, “Every plant that my Father in heaven did not plant will be pulled up by the roots.
text: Yesu ɔtã ma mmuai sɔ, “Kudziri biara gɔ iiɖe Tete gɔ i kato lopia ne, maarui kɔ̃ mapɛyu.

ref: “Why don't your disciples obey what our ancestors taught us to do? They don't even wash their hands before they eat.”
text: “Be ɔso fiɛ fɔ marasuãdze iisɛ mabara i bo mawa kubarara iso? Masɛ̃ maisɛ mafore nrɔɔ̃ i bo mmara kanya fiɛ masɛ maɖe aɖera.”

ref: But you let people get by without helping their parents when they should. You let them say that what they have has been offered to God.
text: Ɣɛɛ mi wũ mito mite sɔ si ɔrere ba ira nɛ ikote sɔ ɔ̃asu ɔbuai ɔ̃ maɣese fiɛ ɔɣɛ sɔ, ‘Nnɛ loasu lotã-ɔ loɔsu lotã Ɣaa.’ ne, iisinya sɔ ɔturi aawarɛ ɔ̃ ɔse ɣee ɔ̃ ɔnyi.

ref: Is this any way to show respect to your parents? You ignore God's commands in order to follow your own teaching.
text: Ne ɔso iki i mi kurabarara iso ne, miɔsɛ̃ Ɣaa mmara, mimɔɛ̃ mi mawa mire mito.

ref: It is useless for you to worship me, when you teach rules made up by humans.” What Really Makes People Unclean (Mark
text: Siefe kere mato mabara wũ karabara. Mato mate maturi mmara i wũ mire kanya.’ ” Ara Wa Lobɔrɛ I Ɔtu Ame Nsɛ Aɣɛra Ɔturi.

ref: Stay away from those Pharisees! They are like blind people leading other blind people, and all of them will fall into a ditch.”
text: Ne ɔso minyua ma. Manɔbiɛdze maɖe fiɛ mato makɔ manɔbiɛdze. Si nɔbiɛdze to ɔkɔ nɔbiɛdze ne, iwo ame i ma inyɔɔ aakpase mabo.”

ref: Jesus did not say a word. But the woman kept following along and shouting, so his disciples came up and asked him to send her away.
text: Ɣɛɛ Yesu iiɣɛ kuira. Ne ɔ̃ marasuãdze ɔba maaɣere wũ sɔ, “Sa wũ si ɔnyua kulu ɔla ɔtɛ̃ra bo atsue!”

ref: The food you put into your mouth doesn't make you unclean and unfit to worship God. The bad words that come out of your mouth are what make you unclean.”
text: Nnɛ i ɔturi ɔɖe iibawo wũ ɔpukutura, ɣɛɛ nnɛ nsɛ ibɔrɛ i ɔturi kanya ame nsɛ ipukutura wũ.”

ref: Then his disciples came over to him and asked, “Do you know you insulted the Pharisees by what you said?”
text: Ne ɔ̃ marasuãdze ɔbore mabo wũ maɣere wũ sɔ, “Aɣe sɔ nnɛ aɣɛ mmɔ ɔta iya ma Farisise iso?”

ref: Peter replied, “What did you mean when you talked about the things that make people unclean?”
text: Petro ɔɣere wũ sɔ, “Ɖi aɖera ɔturi iɣɛra igbã nɛmɔ karɔ̃ te bo.”

ref: Jesus then said: Don't any of you know by now what I am talking about?
text: Yesu ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Miinɔ itɔ̃me nɛ ana karɔ̃?

ref: Don't you know that the food you put into your mouth goes into your stomach and then out of your body?
text: Minɔ karɔ̃ sɔ ira biara nɛ nsɛ ibo i ɔturi kanya ame ne, isɛ ikɛlɛ ɔ̃ iyiri ame, kama ne, ɔ̃anyi ne ɔpɛyu lɛ ɔdziriiso awe.

ref: But the words that come out of your mouth come from your heart. And they are what make you unfit to worship God.
text: Ɣɛɛ atɔ̃me wa nsɛ abɔrɛ i ɔturi kanya ame ne, ɔ̃ ɔtu ame abɔrɛ fiɛ asɛ apukutura wũ.

ref: Out of your heart come evil thoughts, murder, unfaithfulness in marriage, vulgar deeds, stealing, telling lies, and insulting others.
text: Alasɔ ɔturi ɔtu ame i adzuni nyanyarĩa wa nsɛgu ituriɖoe, maturire isiai gu marã irɔ, asɔrɔ̃ iɖe, kayu, mila aɖansiɛ iɖe, anyakpi iɣɛ i maturi iso sɛ ibɔrɛ.

ref: These are what make you unclean. Eating without washing your hands will not make you unfit to worship God. A Woman's Faith (Mark
text: Nwagbe nsɛ aɣɛra ɔturi ne. Iiɖe nrɔɔ̃ mɛ maifore fiɛ maɖe ara nsɛ iɣɛra ɔturi.” Yesu Ɔsa Ɔrɔ̃go Ɔwɛ̃ Ɔbi Birɔ̃gomi.

ref: Jesus said, “I was sent only to the people of Israel! They are like a flock of lost sheep.”
text: Mmɔ i Yesu ɔɣɛ sɔ, “Israel maturi ma nse lɛ masɛrɛ ma loyu kɔrɛ kere i Ɣaa ɔpia me.”

ref: Large crowds came and brought many people who were paralyzed or blind or lame or unable to talk. They placed them, and many others, in front of Jesus, and he healed them all.
text: Maturi gbodzoo ɔba ɔ̃ kɔrɛ. Gɔ mato maba ne, masɛgu mma lofɛfɛ̃ mafu, manɔbiɛdze, mabafã, mamumu gu manyɛse agbãagbã. Makɔ ma mabɔ Yesu katɔ̃.

ref: Jesus left and went to the territory near the towns of Tyre and Sidon.
text: Yesu ɔbɔrɛ i mmɔ ɔkɛlɛ Tiro gu Sidon awo ame.

ref: Suddenly a Canaanite woman from there came out shouting, “Lord and Son of David, have pity on me! My daughter is full of demons.”
text: Mmɔ i ɔ Kananse rɔ̃go ɔwɛ̃ gɔ lobɔrɛ ndɛ̃ mɛmɔ ɔba Yesu kɔrɛ ku kulu teteree ɔba ɔ̃atã wũ iti sɔ, “Wũ Sate David ɔwa, nyɔ me nnya! Wũ ɔbi birɔ̃gomi to ɔwe inyɛ gbaã i siwarã lalaa nrɔɔ̃ ame.”

ref: The woman came closer. Then she knelt down and begged, “Please help me, Lord!”
text: Ɣɛɛ ne, ɔrɔ̃go gɔmɔ ɔba ɔ̃apɛ agɛgɛ̃ i ɔ̃ katɔ̃ ɔtã wũ iti sɔ, “Wũ Sate, buai me!”

ref: Jesus replied, “It isn't right to take food away from children and feed it to dogs.”
text: Yesu ɔɣere wũ sɔ, “Iilɛ sɔ maasu mabiɛtɛ aɖera matã makpɛkpɛ.”

ref: “Lord, this is true,” the woman said, “but even puppies get the crumbs that fall from their owner's table.”
text: Ɔrɔ̃go ɔtã wũ mmuai sɔ, “Wũ Sate gbaã iɖe, ɣɛɛ makpɛkpɛ sɛ marɛrɛ aɖera wa lokpakpase i makɔse ɔpɔrɔ̃ iso afere i karɔ̃ maɖe.”

ref: Jesus answered, “Dear woman, you really do have a lot of faith, and you will be given what you want.” At that moment her daughter was healed. Jesus Heals Many People
text: Gɔ Yesu ɔnɔ atɔ̃me wagbe ne, ɔɣere wũ sɔ, “Ɔrɔ̃go, fɔ kafɔkaɖe ɔmɔ gbaã! Nnɛ ato akarɛ si iba itã-ɔ.” Ɔluwɛ̃ ɔbi birɔ̃gomi ko losarɛ. Yesu Ɔsa Maturi Gbodzoo.

ref: From there, Jesus went along Lake Galilee. Then he climbed a hill and sat down.
text: Yesu ɔrui i mmɔ ɔsɛ Galilea itubu kuruɛkuruɛ ɔsɛ ɔ̃aya kabemi kawɛ̃ ɔkpase ɔsɛ i karɔ̃.

ref: Everyone was amazed at what they saw and heard. People who had never spoken could now speak. The lame were healed, the paralyzed could walk, and the blind were able to see. Everyone was praising the God of Israel. Jesus Feeds Four Thousand (Mark
text: Gɔ maturi ɔnya sɔ mamumu to maɖe aka, mabafã ɔta masɛ, mma lofɛfɛ̃ mafu ɔna ɔle fiɛ manɔbiɛdze to manya ara ne, male Israel Ɣaa. Yesu Ɔtã Maturi Gbodzoo Aɖera Maɖe.

ref: Jesus called his disciples together and told them, “I feel sorry for these people. They have been with me for three days, and they don't have anything to eat. I don't want to send them away hungry. They might faint on their way home.”
text: Yesu ɔkpere ɔ̃ marasuãdze ɔbɔ so i kɔrɛ ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Mmagbe ara to akpɛ̃ me nnya, alasɔ maɔwe i wũ kɔrɛ akpɛnɛ atɛ fiɛ mana kuira nɛ maaɖe. Loito lobie sɔ makɛlɛ ayo ku kukã, si iiɖe ngbɔ ne, mato maasɛ maafɛ̃ i ɔri.”

ref: His disciples said, “This place is like a desert. Where can we find enough food to feed such a crowd?”
text: Ɔ̃ marasuãdze ɔkarɛ wũ sɔ, “Fafuĩ gɔgbe ame ne, lɛ boana aɖera botã maturi gbodzoo magbe ɔɖuɖu fiɛ maaɖe mami?”

ref: Jesus asked them how much food they had. They replied, “Seven small loaves of bread and a few little fish.”
text: Yesu ɔkarɛ ma sɔ, “Abolo kuboi kurɛ̃ mito?” Ne matã wũ mmuai sɔ, “Abolo kuboi kukɔdzɛ gu kufarai wiriwiria kurɛ̃saã.”

ref: After Jesus had told the people to sit down,
text: Ne Yesu ɔɣere maturi ɔɖuɖu sɔ masɛ i karɔ̃ ne.

ref: he took the seven loaves of bread and the fish and gave thanks. He then broke them and handed them to his disciples, who passed them around to the crowds.
text: Ɔsu abolo kuboi kukɔdzɛ gu kufarai ne ɔpɛ Ɣaa siba i wã iti ne. Ɔbɛbɛse ɔsu ɔtã ɔ̃ marasuãdze fiɛ masu maɣɛɣɛ maturi.

ref: Everyone ate all they wanted, and the leftovers filled seven large baskets.
text: Ma ɔɖuɖu ɔɖe ara mami, ne gɔ marasuãdze ɔsa akutia kanya ne, mana kusɛsɛi kukɔdzɛ.

ref: After Jesus had sent the crowds away, he got into a boat and sailed across the lake. He came to shore near the town of Magadan.
text: Yesu ɔɖi maturi ɔri fiɛ ɔ̃ wũ ka ɔya ɔbo i ɔkolo ame ɔwara ɔkɛlɛ Magadan karɔ̃ iso.

ref: Don't you know by now that I am not talking to you about bread? Watch out for the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees!
text: Nda ɔso fiɛ miito minɔ karɔ̃ sɔ iiɖe abolo iso loto loɖe ika? Midzuɛ̃ so i ma Farisise gu ma Zadukise inyagɛ̃ kua iso!”

ref: When Jesus and his disciples were near the town of Caesarea Philippi, he asked them, “What do people say about the Son of Man?”
text: Yesu gu ɔ̃ marasuãdze ɔkɛlɛ Kaisarea Filipi ɔmagɛ̃ kɔrɛ. Mmɔ iɖe ɔkarɛ ɔ̃ marasuãdze sɔ, “Nna i maturi sɛ maɣɛ sɔ mme Ɔturi Awune Ɔbi ɖe?”

ref: Finally, the disciples understood that Jesus wasn't talking about the yeast used to make bread, but about the teaching of the Pharisees and Sadducees. Who Is Jesus? (Mark
text: Mmɔ ikpa marasuãdze sɔ iiɖe inyagɛ̃ kua gɔ masɛ masu mabara abolo iso ɔto ɔɖe ika ne, ma Farisise gu ma Zadukise ara ite ɔto. Petro Ɔɖi Ɔte Sɔ Yesu Nɖe Kristo.

ref: He told them: If the sky is red in the evening, you say the weather will be good.
text: Ɣɛɛ Yesu ɔtã ma mmuai sɔ, “Si kuɣɛ̃ to kusɛ kuabo iyo fiɛ kuɖokpo ɔrɛtɛ ne, misɛ miɣɛ sɔ, ‘Kayi to kaakpa kɔrãkaɖe.’

ref: But if the sky is red and gloomy in the morning, you say it is going to rain. You can tell what the weather will be like by looking at the sky. But you don't understand what is happening now.
text: Ngbɔ ame si mita ku kaya fiɛ kuɖokpo ɔrɛtɛ, kayi ɔnyɔ guduu ne, misɛ miɣɛ sɔ, ‘Kado to ɔ̃apɛ nɔme.’ Misɛ miwo kuɖokpo ɔnyɔ ɔɣɛ kumɛgɔ i kayi aase ɣɛɛ miito miwo ɔwi gɔ ame mipia ɔnya ɔɖi ite!

ref: The disciples answered, “Some people say you are John the Baptist or maybe Elijah or Jeremiah or some other prophet.”
text: Matã wũ mmuai sɔ, “Mawɛ̃ sɔ Ndupiedze Yohanes, mawɛ̃ ana sɔ Eliya, fiɛ mawɛ̃ ana sɔ Yeremia ɣee Ɣaa kanyaɖidze ɔ aɖe.”

ref: From then on, Jesus began telling his disciples what would happen to him. He said, “I must go to Jerusalem. There the nation's leaders, the chief priests, and the teachers of the Law of Moses will make me suffer terribly. I will be killed, but three days later I will rise to life.”
text: Ita i ɔwi gɔmɔ ne, Yesu ɔtsɛ ɔ̃ marasuãdze iɣere iɖi sɔ, “Nto loakɛlɛ Yerusalem fiɛ loawe inyɛ gbaã i makpakpa, masɔrɛdze makpakpa gu mmara matedze nrɔɔ̃ ame. Mato maaɖoe me, ɣɛɛ ikpɛnɛ tɛare ne, Ɣaa to ɔ̃atara me lowe ngbã.”

ref: Jesus then warned them, “Watch out! Guard against the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees.”
text: Ne Yesu to ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Midzuɛ̃ so i ma Farisise gu ma Zadukise inyagɛ̃ kua iso.”

ref: The disciples talked this over and said to each other, “He must be saying this because we didn't bring along any bread.”
text: Ne matsɛ so ikarɛ sɔ, “Kumɛgɔ boito abolo ɔso fiɛ ɔto ɔɣere bo ngbɔ?”

ref: The Son of Man will soon come in the glory of his Father and with his angels to reward all people for what they have done.
text: Alasɔ Ɔturi Awune Ɔbi to ɔ̃aba i ɔ̃ Ɔse ikpawaĩ ame gu ɔ̃ makpabo fiɛ ɔ̃atã ɔbiara kuso i ɔ̃ karabara kanya.

ref: So I will call you Peter, which means “a rock.” On this rock I will build my church, and death itself will not have any power over it.
text: Ne ɔso nto loɣere-ɔ sɔ fɔ nɖe Petro, gɔ karɔ̃ nɖe ita siare, fiɛ ita siare nɛgbe iso loatsue wũ mafɔɖedze ikuri losia gɔ kukpi kɔra iibawo ma iso ɔɖe.

ref: Then Jesus asked, “But who do you say I am?”
text: Mmɔ i Yesu to ɔkarɛ ma sɔ, “Ne mi ɣɛɛ? Nna mibu sɔ loɖe?”

ref: Simon Peter spoke up, “You are the Messiah, the Son of the living God.”
text: Simon Petro ɔtara kanya sɔ, “Fɔ nɖe Kristo gɔmɔ, Ɣaa gɔ mba ngbã Ɔbi.”

ref: Jesus told him: Simon, son of Jonah, you are blessed! You didn't discover this on your own. It was shown to you by my Father in heaven.
text: Ne Yesu ɔɣɛ sɔ, “So si siɣɔ-ɔ Simon, Yona ɔbi, alasɔ iiɖe ɔturi awune loɖi ɔnukuare gɔgbe ɔte-ɔ, ɣɛɛ wũ Ɔse gɔ i kato ɔɖe.

ref: I will give you the keys to the kingdom of heaven, and God in heaven will allow whatever you allow on earth. But he will not allow anything you don't allow.
text: Fiɛ nto loasu Ɣaa sigarakaɖekɔ̃ sibɔi lotã-ɔ sɔ nnɛ asɛ̃ i karɔ̃ iso ne, maasɛ̃ ne i kato fiɛ nnɛ asese i karɔ̃ iso ne, maasese ne i kato.”

ref: Jesus told his disciples not to tell anyone he was the Messiah. Jesus Speaks about His Suffering and Death (Mark
text: Ne Yesu ka ɔsɛ̃ ɔtã ɔ̃ marasuãdze sɔ madaaɣere kuwɛ̃ sɔ ɔ̃ nɖe Kristo ne. Yesu Ɔɖe Ika I Ɔ̃ Inyɛwe Gu Kukpi Iso.

ref: Peter took Jesus aside and told him to stop talking like that. He said, “God would never let this happen to you, Lord!”
text: Petro ɔkɔ wũ ɔɣedza i kuruɛ ɔtã wũ itai ɔɣɛ sɔ, “Kuiyikuiyi daasiɣɛ itɔ̃me nɛgbe! Iibaba-ɔ iso ngbɔ!”

ref: Jesus turned to Peter and said, “Satan, get away from me! You're in my way because you think like everyone else and not like God.”
text: Yesu ɔfiniki ɔɣere Petro sɔ, “Re me kama, Satana! Ato abie sɔ akpesera so ɔri itɛ̃ra atã me alasɔ adzuni wagbe iibɔrɛ i Ɣaa kɔrɛ, ɣɛɛ ɔturi kɔrɛ abɔrɛ.”

ref: Then Jesus said to his disciples: If any of you want to be my followers, you must forget about yourself. You must take up your cross and follow me.
text: Ne Yesu to ɔɣere ɔ̃ marasuãdze sɔ, “Si mi ɔrere ɔbie sɔ ɔ̃asiai me ne, ɔsɛ̃ so si ɔsu ɔ̃ inyɛwe ɔnyigbã si ɔ̃awo me ɔsiai.

ref: If you want to save your life, you will destroy it. But if you give up your life for me, you will find it.
text: Alasɔ ɔbiara gɔ loakpɛ̃ i ɔ̃ ngbã iti ne, ɔto ɔ̃ayura me, ɣɛɛ ngɔ loayura ɔ̃ ngbã ala wũ ɔso ne, ɔto ɔ̃ana ngbã.

ref: What will you gain, if you own the whole world but destroy yourself? What would you give to get back your soul?
text: Mme kusia i ɔturi aana si kayiiso ɔɖuɖu karana ɔkpese ɔ̃ kare fiɛ ɔ̃ayura ɔ̃ mɔmɔ ngbã? Ɣee ira iwɛ̃ pia gɔ ɔturi aasu ɔnyuarã ala ɔ̃ ngbã?

ref: I promise you some of those standing here will not die before they see the Son of Man coming with his kingdom.
text: Nto loɣere mi gbaã sɔ mi ndɛ̃ mawɛ̃ i ngbe iibakpi fiɛ maanya Ɔturi Awune Ɔbi to ɔba lɛ Igara awe.”

ref: There in front of the disciples, Jesus was completely changed. His face was shining like the sun, and his clothes became white as light.
text: Gɔ mato manyɔ kere ne, Yesu kumiamia ɔkpaki, ɔ̃ katɔ̃ to kafiɛ lɛ kuɣɛ̃, fiɛ ɔ̃ arapia ana to afiɛ atsue anɔmi.

ref: Then the disciples understood Jesus was talking to them about John the Baptist. Jesus Heals a Boy (Mark
text: Mmɔ i marasuãdze ɔtsue sɔ Ndupiedze Yohanes ɔto ɔɣɛ ala ne. Yesu Ɔsa Ɔbirɛrɛĩ Ɔwɛ̃ Gɔ I Siwarã Lalaa Si Iso.

ref: Six days later Jesus took Peter and the brothers James and John with him. They went up on a very high mountain where they could be alone.
text: Ayi akuɔ kama ne, Yesu ɔkɔ Petro gu Yakobo gu Yohanes makɛlɛ kube kɔlɛa kuwɛ̃ iso ma ɔnɔwɛ̃.

ref: All at once Moses and Elijah were there talking with Jesus.
text: Ne marasuãdze itɛ magbe ɔnya Mose gu Eliya to maɖegu Yesu ika ne.

ref: So Peter said to him, “Lord, it is good for us to be here! Let us make three shelters, one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.”
text: Ne Petro ɔɣere Yesu sɔ, “Bosate, bo ngbe iba ɔbua ilɛ gbaã! Si abie ne, tã bopɛ akpayo atɛ i ngbe. Iwɛ̃ itã-ɔ, iwɛ̃ itã Mose fiɛ iwɛ̃ ana itã Eliya.”

ref: While Peter was still speaking, the shadow of a bright cloud passed over them. From the cloud a voice said, “This is my own dear Son, and I am pleased with him. Listen to what he says!”
text: Gɔ ɔtogu ika iɖe ne, idɔdɔ fiɛa iwɛ̃ ɔso iba iafu ma iso fiɛ silɔ siwɛ̃ ɔɖe ika sibɔrɛgu i idɔdɔ ame siɣɛ sɔ, “Wũ Ɔbi mɔmɔ gɔ loipia kuɣɛ i ngbe ne, mikã wũ atsue.”

ref: When the disciples heard the voice, they were so afraid they fell flat on the ground.
text: Gɔ marasuãdze ɔnɔ silɔ dzɛgbe ne, ifɔ̃ ɔmɔɛ̃ ma sɔ makɔlɛ mafu i karɔ̃.

ref: But Jesus came over and touched them. He said, “Get up and don't be afraid!”
text: Yesu ɔba ma kɔrɛ, ɔpɛgu ma ne ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Mita miya ne midaanigã.”

ref: When they opened their eyes, they saw only Jesus.
text: Gɔ matara anɔ kato ne, mainya kuwɛ̃ i mmɔ, iɖo Yesu ɔnɔwɛ̃.

ref: The disciples asked Jesus, “Don't the teachers of the Law of Moses say Elijah must come before the Messiah does?”
text: Ne marasuãdze to makarɛ Yesu sɔ, “Be ɔso fiɛ mmara matedze sɛ maɣɛ sɔ Eliya aaɖe katɔ̃ ɔba tɔtɔ?”

ref: Jesus told them, “Elijah certainly will come and get everything ready.
text: Yesu ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Gbaã iɖe sɔ Eliya aaɖe katɔ̃ ɔba ɔ̃aledza ira biara ɔsɛ.

ref: In fact, he has already come. But the people did not recognize him and treated him just as they wanted to. They will soon make the Son of Man suffer in the same way.”
text: Ɣɛɛ ne, nto loɣere mi sɔ Eliya ɔɔba koko, maturi iinya wũ maɖi ite. Mabara wũ nnɛ mabie fiɛ ngbɔ ame mato maabara Ɔturi Awune Ɔbi ɔtile ana ne.”

ref: Jesus and his disciples returned to the crowd. A man knelt in front of him
text: Gɔ makpese maba iturikuri kɔrɛ ne, ɔrɛrɛ̃ ɔwɛ̃ ɔba ɔ̃apɛ agɛgɛ̃ i Yesu kɔrɛ.

ref: and said, “Lord, have pity on my son! He has a bad case of epilepsy and often falls into a fire or into water.
text: Ne ɔɣere wũ sɔ, “Bosate, nyɔ wũ ɔbi nnya! Kakpebubu ɔnyɛ to ɔɖaa wũ gɔ isɛ inyɛdɛ̃ wũ teteree itã ɔpɛ ɔbo i ɔtɔ ɣee ndu ame.

ref: I brought him to your disciples, but none of them could heal him.”
text: Lokɔ wũ lobɔ fɔ marasuãdze sɔ masa wũ ɣɛɛ maiwo.”

ref: Jesus said, “You people are too stubborn to have any faith! How much longer must I be with you? Why do I have to put up with you? Bring the boy here.”
text: Ne Yesu ɔtã mmuai sɔ, “Mi kiniɔgbe maturi ma loifɔ maɖe fiɛ manyanyarĩ, ayi sirɛ̃ loawe i mi kɔrɛ isɛ iabo? Kɔ ɔbi bɔ me i ngbe!”

ref: Then Jesus spoke sternly to the demon. It went out of the boy, and right then he was healed.
text: Yesu ɔtɔ̃me ɔpia siwarã fiɛ sibɔrɛ i ɔbi ame. Ɔluwɛ̃ ne, ɔbi ɔsarɛ.

ref: Later the disciples went to Jesus in private and asked him, “Why couldn't we force out the demon?”
text: Ne marasuãdze ɔbore mabo wũ makarɛ wũ pɔkɔsɔɔ sɔ, “Be ɔso fiɛ boiwo siwarã lalaa ɔsa ɔɖi?”

ref: While Jesus and his disciples were going from place to place in Galilee, he told them, “The Son of Man will be handed over to people
text: Gɔ marasuãdze ɔɖuɖu ɔba maasakanya i Galilea ne, Yesu ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Mato maba maaɖi mme, Ɔturi Awune Ɔbi, matã maturi,

ref: who will kill him. But three days later he will rise to life.” All of this made the disciples very sad. Paying the Temple Tax
text: maɖoe, ɣɛɛ ikpɛnɛ tɛare ne, loto loaledza ita ibɔrɛ i makpise ame.” Itɔ̃me nɛgbe ɔɖaa marasuãdze gbaã. Ɣaa Isɔrɛyo Toto Itã.

ref: When Jesus and the others arrived in Capernaum, the collectors for the temple tax came to Peter and asked, “Does your teacher pay the temple tax?”
text: Gɔ Yesu gu ɔ̃ marasuãdze ɔba Kapernaum ne, mma nsɛ mafɔ kasɔrɛkɔ̃ toto ɔba maakarɛ Petro sɔ, “Mi ratedze ne, ɔsɛ ɔtã kasɔrɛkɔ̃ toto?”

ref: “Yes, he does,” Peter answered. After they had returned home, Jesus went up to Peter and asked him, “Simon, what do you think? Do the kings of this earth collect taxes and fees from their own people or from foreigners?”
text: Ne Petro ɔtã ma mmuai sɔ, “Ɔsɛ ɔtã!” Gɔ Petro ɔsɛ ɔ̃abo iyo ne, Yesu loɖe katɔ̃ ɔkarɛ wũ sɔ, “Simon, be nɖe fɔ adzuni i nnɛ nto loba lokarɛ-ɔ iso? Kayiiso magara lampo ne, mabe nsɛ matã? Karɔ̃ ga iso i igara to ɔɖe maturi ɛɛbɛrɛ mafɔ ma mpia i karɔ̃ gamɔ iso?”

ref: Peter answered, “From foreigners.” Jesus replied, “Then their own people don't have to pay.
text: Ne Petro ɔtã mmuai sɔ, “Mafɔ.” Yesu ɔtã wũ mmuai sɔ, “Ne karɔ̃ nɖe sɔ karɔ̃ gamɔ iso maturi iisɛ matã lampo gɔmɔ ne.

ref: But we don't want to cause trouble. So go cast a line into the lake and pull out the first fish you hook. Open its mouth, and you will find a coin. Use it to pay your taxes and mine.”
text: Ɣɛɛ si boibata boya i mmagbe iso ne, kɛlɛ itubu kanya si apia ɖaɖiwa. Ikpɛ ɖeakatɔ̃ nɛ aaɖi ne, pia kɔrɔ̃ i ne kanya ame ne ato aanya sikã dzɛ loawo atã bo gu-ɔ isɔrɛyo lampo si asu atã ma.”

ref: If your eye causes you to sin, poke it out and get rid of it. You would be better off to go into life with only one eye than to have two eyes and be thrown into the fires of hell. The Lost Sheep (Luke
text: Si fɔ inɔmi ana nsɛ itã abo i ikpi ame ne, were ne ɖi pɛyu! Ilɛ iɖo sɔ aawe inɔmi iwɛ̃ abo i ngbã ame iɖo sɔ aawe anɔmi anyɔ mafuĩdza-ɔ mapia i itɔkpe nɛ loisɛ inyi ame. Yesu Ɔte Ara I Sɛrɛrerɔ̃ Gɔ Loyu Iso.

ref: Don't you think you should show pity to someone else, as I did to you?”
text: Ikote sɔ fɔ wũ aanyɔ fɔ ɔlaa nnya lɛ kumɛgɔ lonyɔ-ɔ nnya.’

ref: But if you are as humble as this child, you are the greatest in the kingdom of heaven.
text: Ngɔ lonya ɔɖo i Ɣaa sigarakaɖekɔ̃ ame ne, ɔ̃ nɖe ngɔ lobɔ so karɔ̃ ɔkpesera so lɛ ɔbiɛtɛ gɔgbe awe.

ref: And when you welcome one of these children because of me, you welcome me. Temptations To Sin (Mark
text: “Ɔbiara gɔ loafɔ ɔbiɛtɛ gɔgbe i wũ iyere ame ne, mme iɖe ɔfɔ. Daakpadzɛ̃ Kuwɛ̃ Apia I Kalakanyɔ Ame.

ref: If the follower refuses to listen to them, report the matter to the church. Anyone who refuses to listen to the church must be treated like an unbeliever or a tax collector. Allowing and Not Allowing
text: Si mmɔ ana ɔ̃ikã ma atsue ne, ɣere mafɔɖedze ikuri ɔɖuɖu, ɣɛɛ si ɔ̃ikã ma atsue ana ne, ɖi wũ atsue lɛ ngɔ loifɔ ɔɖe ɣee lampofɔdze.

ref: It will be terrible for people who cause even one of my little followers to sin. Those people would be better off thrown into the deepest part of the ocean with a heavy stone tied around their necks!
text: “Ɔrere gɔ loatã ɔbiɛtɛ gɔ mba kafɔkaɖe i wũ ame ɔfiniki i kafɔkaɖe kama ne, si masu ɔwɛ̃ manyi kukɔtɛ siare masarã wũ i ɔme mafuĩdza wũ mapia i ɔpo ame ne, ialɛgu wũ iɖo.

ref: If your hand or foot causes you to sin, chop it off and throw it away! You would be better off to go into life paralyzed or lame than to have two hands or two feet and be thrown into the fire that never goes out.
text: Ne ɔso si fɔ kɔrɔ̃ ɣee kukpa sɛ kukɔ-ɔ kupia i ikpi ame ne, tu kɔ̃ pɛyu! Ilɛ iɖo sɔ aabo i ngbã ame sɔ ana kɔrɔ̃ ɣee kukpa iɖo sɔ aawe nrɔɔ̃ minyɔ ngba minyɔ mafuĩdza-ɔ mapia i itɔkpe nɛ loibanyi inya ame.

ref: If one of my followers sins against you, go and point out what was wrong. But do it in private, just between the two of you. If that person listens, you have won back a follower.
text: “Si fɔ ɔnyii ɔɣɛrɛ-ɔ ne, kɛlɛ ɔ̃ kɔrɛ si asɛ aaɖi ate wũ i mi gu wũ inyɔ ndɛ̃ kere. Si ɔkã-ɔ atsue ne, mi sinyii to siakɛlɛ i katɔ̃.

ref: Don't be cruel to any of these little ones! I promise you their angels are always with my Father in heaven.
text: Alasɔ Ɔturi Awune Ɔbi ɔba sɔ ɔ̃afɔ mma loyu ɔtã ma ngbã.

ref: Let me ask you this. What would you do if you had
text: “Si ɔturi ba masɛrɛ kulafa fiɛ ɔwɛ̃ ɔyu ne, be ɔ̃abara? Ɔ̃ibanyua kasiwɛ̃-kaiwɛ̃ ɔɣedza i kube ɔkparuɛ ɔsɛ ɔ̃anyɔnyɔ ngɔ nto ɔyu?

ref: I am sure that finding it would make you happier than having the
text: Fiɛ si ɔnya wũ ne, nto loɣere mi sɔ ɔ̃ana isoɣɔ i ɔwɛ̃ gɔ loyu ngbe iso ɔɖo kasiwɛ̃-kaiwɛ̃ ma loiyu.

ref: That's how it is with your Father in heaven. He doesn't want any of these little ones to be lost. When Someone Sins (Luke
text: Ngbɔ ame i mi ɔse gɔ i kato iibabie sɔ makosoi magbe kuwɛ̃ si ɔyu ne. Ɔri Gɔ Iso Aala Ɔnyii Fɔɖedze Gɔ Loɣɛrɛ-Ɔ.

ref: But if that one refuses to listen, take along one or two others. The Scriptures teach that every complaint must be proven true by two or more witnesses.
text: Ɣɛɛ si ɔ̃ikã-ɔ atsue ne, kɔ ɔturi ɔwɛ̃ ɣee inyɔ si misɛ ala Ɔko Sekelea ɔɣɛ sɔ, ‘Maɖansiɛ inyɔ ɣee itɛ kanya ame itɔ̃me iso maasia itɔ̃me biara ita.’

ref: I promise you God in heaven will allow whatever you allow on earth, but God will not allow anything you don't allow.
text: “Ne ɔso nto loɣere mi ɔɖuɖu sɔ, nnɛ misɛ̃ i kayiiso ngbe ne, Ɣaa aasɛ̃ ne i kato, fiɛ nnɛ miɖi ɔri i karɔ̃ iso ngbe ne, Ɣaa aasia kɔrɔ̃ i ne iso i kato ana.

ref: I promise that when any two of you on earth agree about something you are praying for, my Father in heaven will do it for you.
text: “Nto loledza mi iɣere sɔ ira biara nɛ i mi maturi inyɔ aatɔrã misia ita i kayi gagbe iso i kayi ikparama ame ne, wũ Ɔse gɔ i kato to ɔ̃abara ne ɔtã mi.

ref: Whenever two or three of you come together in my name, I am there with you. An Official Who Refused To Forgive
text: Alasɔ ngbegɔ i maturi inyɔ ɣee itɛ ɔsarɛ i wũ iɣere ame ne, lopia i ma ndɛ̃.” Igbã I Ɔɖabo Gɔ Loisu Ɔtsɛ Ɔ̃ Ɔlaa Iso.

ref: Peter came up to the Lord and asked, “How many times should I forgive someone who does something wrong to me? Is seven times enough?”
text: Petro ɔbore ɔbo Yesu ɔkarɛ wũ sɔ, “Bosate, ikpɛ irɛ̃ loasu lotsɛ wũ ɔnyii gɔ nto ɔɣɛrɛ me? Ikpɛ ikɔdzɛ?”

ref: This story will show you what the kingdom of heaven is like: One day a king decided to call in his officials and ask them to give an account of what they owed him.
text: “Ɣaa sigarakaɖekɔ̃ se lɛ ngbe. Ɔwi ɔwɛ̃ ne, igara ɔwɛ̃ ɔbie sɔ ɔ̃aki i ɔ̃ maɖabo siko ame ɔnyɔ kumɛgɔ mato mabara ɔ̃ karabara.

ref: As he was doing this, one official was brought in who owed him
text: Ku ɔ̃ isɛ karɔ̃ kere ne, makɔ ɔwɛ̃ gɔ nto wũ kuso titiiti mabɔ.

ref: But he didn't have any money to pay what he owed. The king ordered him to be sold, along with his wife and children and all he owned, in order to pay the debt.
text: Gɔ ɔɖabo gɔmɔ na sikã ɔ̃atã kuso ɔso ne, igara ɔtã sɔ marɔdɛ̃ ɔ̃ gu ɔ̃ ɔre gu mabi lɛ masande gu ira biara nɛ ɔba si masu matã kuso.

ref: The official got down on his knees and began begging, “Have pity on me, and I will pay you every cent I owe!”
text: “Ɔɖabo gɔgbe ɔpɛ agɛgɛ̃ i igara katɔ̃ ɔtã wũ iti sɔ, ‘Na ɔtu tã me ne nto loatã-ɔ kuso ɔɖuɖu gɔ loto-ɔ!’

ref: The king felt sorry for him and let him go free. He even told the official that he did not have to pay back the money.
text: Ɔrɛrɛ̃ gɔgbe ara ɔɖo igara kayiri ne ɔso ɔsu kuso ɔɖuɖu ɔtsɛ wũ ɔnyua wũ sɔ ɔkɛlɛ.

ref: The man got down on his knees and began begging, “Have pity on me, and I will pay you back.”
text: “Ɔ̃ malaa ɔtã wũ iti sɔ ‘Na ɔtu tã wũ ne ɔto ɔ̃tã-ɔ kuso ɔɖuɖu gɔ ɔto-ɔ!’

ref: But the first official refused to have pity. Instead, he went and had the other official put in jail until he could pay what he owed.
text: “Ɣɛɛ ɔsɛ̃, ɔtã mamɔɛ̃ wũ mapia i iyo gbɔgbɔɔgbɔ ɔ̃atã wũ kuso gɔ ɔto.

ref: When some other officials found out what had happened, they felt sorry for the man who had been put in jail. Then they told the king what had happened.
text: Gɔ ɔ̃ maɖabo laa ɔnya nnɛ loba ne, ibua iɖaa ma, ne masɛ maaɣere igara ira biara nɛ loba ne.

ref: The king called the first official back in and said, “You're an evil man! When you begged for mercy, I said you did not have to pay back a cent.
text: “Ne ɔso igara ɔpia makpere ɔɖabo mabɔ ɔɣere wũ sɔ, ‘Ɔɖabo lalaa aɖe! Losu fɔ kuso gɔ ato me ɔɖuɖu lotsɛ-ɔ alasɔ atã me iti.

ref: The king was so angry that he ordered the official to be tortured until he could pay back everything he owed.
text: Sikpã ɔkpɛ̃ igara gbaã ne ɔsu ɔɖabo gɔgbe ɔsese mapia wũ i iyo sɔ ɔwe mmɔ makpadzɛ̃ wũ kutsue gbɔgbɔɔgbɔ ɔ̃atã kuso gɔ ɔto wũ ɔro.”

ref: That is how my Father in heaven will treat you, if you don't forgive each of my followers with all your heart.
text: Ne Yesu ɔwiri sɔ, “Ngbɔ i wũ Ɔse gɔ i kato aabara mi ɔbiara si miisu mi manyii akpi mitsɛ ma ta mi situ ame ɔɖuɖu ne.”

ref: When Jesus finished teaching, he left Galilee and went to the part of Judea east of the Jordan River.
text: Gɔ Yesu ɔɣɛ ara wagbe ɔro ne, ɔrui i Galilea ɔkɛlɛ Yudea awo ame i Yordan Ɔwore kama isɛ.

ref: Some people are unable to marry because of birth defects or because of what someone has done to their bodies. Others stay single in order to serve God better. Anyone who can accept this teaching should do so.” Jesus Blesses Little Children (Mark
text: Alasɔ ara agbãagbã ɔso i marɛrɛ̃ iisɛ mayiri marɔ̃go. Mawɛ̃ ne, ngbɔ maɣe ma ne, mawɛ̃ ana ne, maturi lobara ma ngbɔ, fiɛ mawɛ̃ ana iisɛ mayiri marɔ̃go ala Ɣaa sigarakaɖekɔ̃ ɔso. Ngɔ loawo ara ite nɛgbe ɔfɔ si ɔfɔ ne.” Yesu Ɔtã Mabiɛtɛ Kusɛkusɛ.

ref: Large crowds followed him, and he healed their sick.
text: Maturi gbodzoo ɔsiai wũ fiɛ ɔsa ma i mmɔ.

ref: Some Pharisees wanted to test Jesus. They came up to him and asked, “Is it right for a man to divorce his wife for just any reason?”
text: Ma Farisise mawɛ̃ ɔba ɔ̃ kɔrɛ mala wũ manyɔ makarɛ wũ sɔ, “Bo mmara ɔtã ɔrɛrɛ̃ ɔri sɔ ɔ̃awo ɔ̃ ɔre ɔsɛ̃ i katɔ̃mei biara iso?”

ref: Jesus answered, “Don't you know in the beginning the Creator made a man and a woman?
text: Yesu ɔtã ma mmuai sɔ, “Miika i Ɔko Sekelea ame sɔ ta karɔ̃kasɛkɔ̃ ne, ɔrã gu ɔre i Ɣaa ɔbara?

ref: That's why a man leaves his father and mother and gets married. He becomes like one person with his wife.
text: Fiɛ Ɣaa ɔɣɛ sɔ, ‘Nnɛgbe ɔso i ɔrɛrɛ̃ aarui i ɔ̃ ɔse gu ɔnyi kɔrɛ ɔpɛ ɔmatã i ɔ̃ ɔre iso fiɛ ma inyɔɔ aakpese sosina siwɛ̃ ne.’

ref: Then they are no longer two people, but one. And no one should separate a couple God has joined together.”
text: Ne ɔso maisiɖe maturi inyɔ ɣɛɛ ɔwɛ̃. Ne ɔso kuwɛ̃ si ɔdaabɛse nnɛ i Ɣaa ɔsu ɔmatãra so iso.”

ref: The Pharisees asked Jesus, “Why did Moses say a man could write out divorce papers and send his wife away?”
text: Ne ma Farisise ɔkarɛ wũ sɔ, “Iyɔ be ɔso i Mose ɔɖi ɔri sɔ ɔrɛrɛ̃ aatã ɔ̃ ɔre isɛ̃so ɔko iyɔ ɔ̃ɔɖi wũ ɔri?”

ref: Jesus replied, “You are so heartless! That's why Moses allowed you to divorce your wife. But from the beginning God did not intend it to be that way.
text: Yesu ɔtã ma mmuai sɔ, “Mose ɔtã mi ɔri sɔ misɛ̃ mi mare ala mi atsuele ɔso, ɣɛɛ ne, iiɖe ngbɔ ise ta karɔ̃kasɛkɔ̃.

ref: I say if your wife has not committed some terrible sexual sin, you must not divorce her to marry someone else. If you do, you are unfaithful.”
text: Ne ɔso nto loɣere mi sɔ ɔrɛrɛ̃ biara gɔ loasɛ̃ ɔ̃ ɔre fiɛ iiɖe kama iɖe i ɔrɔ̃go ɔfe wũ ne, ɔɖe asɔrɔ̃ si ɔyiri ɔrɔ̃go mama.”

ref: Jesus told them, “Only those people who have been given the gift of staying single can accept this teaching.
text: Ne Yesu ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Iiɖe ɔturi biara loanɔ ara wagbe karɔ̃, mma i Ɣaa ɔtã kotoo loanɔ karɔ̃.

ref: Some people brought their children to Jesus, so he could place his hands on them and pray for them. His disciples told the people to stop bothering him.
text: Mawɛ̃ ɔkɔ mabiɛtɛ mabɔ Yesu sɔ ɔsia ma kɔrɔ̃ iso si ɔkparama kayi ɔtã ma, ɣɛɛ ɔ̃ marasuãdze ɔnyagalagu mmamɔ.

ref: A man came to Jesus and asked, “Teacher, what good thing must I do to have eternal life?”
text: Ɔwi ɔwɛ̃ ame ne, ɔporoporoi ɔwɛ̃ ɔba Yesu kɔrɛ ɔkarɛ wũ sɔ, “Ratedze, be nɖe ira sɛɛ nɛ loabara fiɛ loana ngbã mɛ loisɛ miro?”

ref: “Which ones?” the man asked. Jesus answered, “Do not murder. Be faithful in marriage. Do not steal. Do not tell lies about others.
text: Ne ɔkarɛ Yesu sɔ, “Mme mmara?” Yesu ɔtã wũ mmuai sɔ, “Daaɖoe ɔturi, daaɖe asɔrɔ̃, daayu kayu, daaɖaɛ fɔ ɔlaa kanya.

ref: After Jesus had placed his hands on the children, he left. A Rich Young Man (Mark
text: Yesu ɔsia ma nrɔɔ̃ iso, ɔtã ma kusɛkusɛ fiɛ ka ɔrui. Ɔporoporoi Ranase Ɔwɛ̃ Iso Itɔ̃me.

ref: Jesus said to him, “Why do you ask me about what is good? Only God is good. If you want to have eternal life, you must obey his commandments.”
text: Ne Yesu ɔtã wũ mmuai sɔ, “Be ɔso ato akarɛ me itɔ̃me i ira sɛɛ iso? Ɔturi ɔwɛ̃ saã pɛ nɖe ɔsɛɛ, fiɛ si ato abie ngbã mɛ loisɛ miro ne, mɔɛ̃ Ɣaa mmara ɔɖuɖu la.”

ref: But Jesus said, “Let the children come to me, and don't try to stop them! People who are like these children belong to God's kingdom.”
text: Yesu ɔɣɛ sɔ, “Mitã mabiɛtɛ si maba wũ kɔrɛ ne midaatɛ̃ ma ɔri, alasɔ Ɣaa sigarakaɖekɔ̃ se sitã ma igbã.”

ref: But many who are now first will be last, and many who are last will be first.
text: Ɣɛɛ gbodzoo ma nɖe maɖeakatɔ̃ kiniɔ to maakpese matsɔra fiɛ mawɛ̃ ma nɖe matsɔra kiniɔ to maakpese maɖeakatɔ̃.

ref: Respect your father and mother. And love others as much as you love yourself.”
text: Warɛ fɔ ɔse gu fɔ ɔnyi si aɖɔɛ fɔ ɔnyii lɛ kumɛgɔ ato aɖɔɛ so.”

ref: The young man said, “I have obeyed all of these. What else must I do?”
text: Ne ɔporoporoi gɔmɔ ɔtã mmuai sɔ, “Loɔbara ara wagbe ɔɖuɖu, be loɔkuti loabara?”

ref: Jesus replied, “If you want to be perfect, go sell everything you own! Give the money to the poor, and you will have riches in heaven. Then come and be my follower.”
text: Yesu ɔɣere wũ sɔ, “Si ato abie sɔ aayi pɛpɛɛpɛ ne, kɛlɛ aasu fɔ ira biara rɔdɛ̃ si asu sikã atsɛ mawɛrɛba, si aana kusia siare i kato. Si ɔɔro ne, kpese ba siai me.”

ref: When the young man heard this, he went away sad, because he was very rich.
text: Gɔ ɔporoporoi gɔgbe ɔnɔ nnɛgbe ne, ɔruigu isoma alasɔ ɔba karana gbaã.

ref: Jesus said to his disciples, “I tell you, it's terribly hard for rich people to get into the kingdom of heaven!
text: Ne Yesu to ɔɣere ɔ̃ marasuãdze sɔ, “Nto loɣere mi sɔ ito iabua iɖa fiɛ ranase aabo i Ɣaa sigarakaɖekɔ̃ ame.

ref: In fact, it's easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to get into God's kingdom.”
text: Nto loledza mi iɣere sɔ, ma mmɔ ibo ba ɔle iɖo kumɛgɔ i kapuipɔngɔ aaki kafe i ɔdzirikaãra iyoi ame.”

ref: When the disciples heard this, they were greatly surprised and asked, “How can anyone ever be saved?”
text: Gɔ marasuãdze ɔnɔ ara wagbe ne, ibara ma ɣii ne mato makarɛ sɔ, “Iyɔ nna loabua ɔna iɖi?”

ref: Jesus looked straight at them and said, “There are some things people cannot do, but God can do anything.”
text: Yesu ɔnyɔ ma i katɔ̃ ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Ɔturi kuwɛ̃ iibawo ɔbara, ɣɛɛ Ɣaa aawo ibiara ɔbara.”

ref: Peter replied, “Remember, we have left everything to be your followers! What will we get?”
text: Ne Petro to ɔɣɛ sɔ, “Nyɔ, iyɔ bo ma lonyua bo ira biara bosɛ fiɛ bosiai-ɔ ne, mme kusia boana?”

ref: Jesus answered: Yes, all of you have become my followers. And so in the future world, when the Son of Man sits on his glorious throne, I promise you will sit on twelve thrones to judge the twelve tribes of Israel.
text: Yesu ɔtã mmuai sɔ, “Nto loɣere mi i ɔnukuare ame sɔ, ɔwi gɔ i Ɔturi Awune Ɔbi aasɛ i ɔ̃ ifiɛ sigaraiyara iso i Kayi Ɣɛtɛ ga nto kaba ame ne, mi ma nɖe wũ marasuãdze iweo-inyɔ ne, mito miasɛ i sigara ayara iso miɖe sigara i Israel nlɛ aka aweo-anyɔ iso.

ref: All who have given up home or brothers and sisters or father and mother or children or land for me will be given
text: Ɔbiara gɔ lonyua ayo, manyiibi rɛrɛ̃ gu marɔ̃go, ɔ̃ ɔse gu ɔnyi, kukpɛkɔ̃ ɔsɛ ala wũ ɔso ne, ɔto ɔ̃afɔ ne ikpɛ kulafa fiɛ ɔ̃ana ngbã mɛ loisɛ miro ana.

ref: As Jesus was telling what the kingdom of heaven would be like, he said: Early one morning a man went out to hire some workers for his vineyard.
text: “Ɣaa sigarakaɖekɔ̃ se lɛ ɔrɛrɛ̃ ɔwɛ̃ gɔ lota kaya tutuutu ɔsɛ ɔ̃abie marabaradze sɔ masɛ maabara karabara i ɔ̃ adziribi kakpɛkɔ̃ ame.

ref: After he had agreed to pay them the usual amount for a day's work, he sent them off to his vineyard.
text: Ɔnɔ karɔ̃ sɔ ɔ̃atã ma ikpɛnɛ kuso lɛ kumɛgɔ masɛ matã fiɛ ɔsese ma sia.

ref: About nine that morning, the man saw some other people standing in the market with nothing to do.
text: “Lɛ iɖɔwũ kaiwɛ̃ ne, ɔledza kuɣa ame isɛ, ne ɔnya gɔ mawɛ̃ ɣɛ mmɔ maito mabara kuira.

ref: He promised to pay them what was fair, if they would work in his vineyard.
text: Ne ɔɣere ma ma wũ sɔ, ‘Mikɛlɛ wũ kakpɛkɔ̃ miabara karabara ne nto loatã mi ikpɛnɛ kuso.’

ref: Finally, about five in the afternoon the man went back and found some others standing there. He asked them, “Why have you been standing here all day long doing nothing?”
text: Ito ikɛlɛ iɖɔwũ iru ame ana gɔ ɔsɛ kuɣa ame ne, ɔledza marɛrɛ̃ mawɛ̃ inya gɔ maɣɛ i mmɔ. Ɔkarɛ ma sɔ, ‘Be ɔso mito miwɔra ikpɛnɛ siefe sɔ miito mibara kuira?’

ref: “Because no one has hired us,” they answered. Then he told them to go work in his vineyard.
text: “Ne matã mmuai sɔ, ‘Kuwɛ̃ iisu bo sɔ bosɛ boabara ɔ̃ karabara.’ “Ne ɔɣere ma sɔ, ‘Mi wũ mikɛlɛ wũ kakpɛkɔ̃ ame miabara karabara.’

ref: That evening the owner of the vineyard told the man in charge of the workers to call them in and give them their money. He also told the man to begin with the ones who were hired last.
text: “Gɔ kutsuɛ ɔɣo ne, kakpɛkɔ̃sate ɔɣere ngɔ nto ɔnyɔ marabaradze iso sɔ, ‘Kpere marabaradze bɔ si atã ma ma kuso. Sɛ karɔ̃ i mma lotsɔra sia ɔkɛlɛ iso si awirigu mma loɖe katɔ̃ makɛlɛ iso.’

ref: When the workers arrived, the ones who had been hired at five in the afternoon were given a full day's pay.
text: “Marabaradze ma lokɛlɛ ku iɖɔwũ iru ame ne, matã ma ɔbiara ikpɛnɛ kuso.

ref: they began complaining to the owner of the vineyard.
text: Gɔ mafɔ sikã ne, matsɛ kakpɛkɔ̃ sate iɣɛtɛ sɔ,

ref: They said, “The ones who were hired last worked for only one hour. But you paid them the same that you did us. And we worked in the hot sun all day long!”
text: ‘Mma lotsɔra sia ɔba ne, ɔɖɔwũ ɔwɛ̃ pɛ mabara karabara atoa sɔ bo ne, bobara karabara ikpɛnɛ ɔɖuɖu i kuɣɛ̃ ame, ɣɛɛ kuso kuwɛ̃ ɔtã bo!’

ref: The owner answered one of them, “Friend, I didn't cheat you. I paid you exactly what we agreed on.
text: “Ne kakpɛkɔ̃ sate ɔtã ma ɔwɛ̃ mmuai sɔ, ‘Ɔrɛrɛĩ, loiɖe mi amumu alasɔ kuso gɔ boɣɛ bosia ita kuɖe lotã mi.

ref: Take your money now and go! What business is it of yours if I want to pay them the same that I paid you?
text: Ne ɔso su fɔ sikã si akɛlɛ iyo. Mme mɔmɔ me lobie sɔ loatã mma lotsɔra ɔba lɛ kumɛgɔ lotã mi.

ref: Don't I have the right to do what I want with my own money? Why should you be jealous, if I want to be generous?”
text: Fiɛ loba ɔri sɔ lobara wũ sikã karabara kumɛgɔ loɖɔɛ, ne ɔso anɔ si adaarɛtɛ-ɔ i wũ ara itsɛ iso.’ ”

ref: Jesus then said, “So it is. Everyone who is now last will be first, and everyone who is first will be last.” Jesus Again Tells about His Death (Mark
text: Ne Yesu ɔwirigu ma sɔ, “Ne ɔso mma nɖe matsɔra to maakpese maɖeakatɔ̃ fiɛ mma nɖe maɖeakatɔ̃ to maakpese matsɔra.” Yesu Ɔɖe Ika I Ɔ̃ Kukpi Iso Ɔkpɛ Tɛare.

ref: When Jesus heard them, he stopped and asked, “What do you want me to do for you?”
text: Yesu ɔtui ɔya ne ɔkpere ma ɔkarɛ sɔ, “Be mito mibie sɔ lobara lotã mi?”

ref: Jesus felt sorry for them and touched their eyes. At once they could see, and they became his followers.
text: Ma ara ɔɖo Yesu kayiri, ne ɔpɛgu ma anɔmi matsɛ inya fiɛ marui masiai wũ ne.

ref: The crowd told them to be quiet, but they shouted even louder, “Lord and Son of David, have pity on us!”
text: Maturi ɔnyagalagu ma sɔ malo, ɣɛɛ makɔlɛ ma silɔ mabua sɔ, “Igara David Ɔbi nyɔ bo nnya soo!”

ref: As Jesus was on his way to Jerusalem, he took his twelve disciples aside and told them in private:
text: Gɔ Yesu to ɔkɛlɛ Yerusalem ne, ɔkpere ɔ̃ marasuãdze iweo-inyɔ ɔɣedza i kuruɛ ɔɣere ma sɔ,

ref: and then they will hand him over to foreigners who will make fun of him. They will beat him and nail him to a cross. But on the third day he will rise from death. A Mother's Request (Mark
text: Maaɖi me matã mma loiɖe ma Yudase mabara me aɣiãra, mapɛ me, fiɛ maapɛ me mamatãra i kudziri iso, ɣɛɛ ikpɛnɛ tɛare iso ne, nto loaledza ita iwe ngbã.” Yakobo Gu Yohanes Ɔnyi Ɔkarɛ Kasɛkɔ̃ Ɔtã Ɔ̃ Mabi.

ref: The mother of James and John came to Jesus with her two sons. She knelt down and started begging him to do something for her.
text: Zebedeo ɔre ɔkɔ ɔ̃ mabi inyɔ maba Yesu kɔrɛ, mapɛ agɛgɛ̃ makarɛ wũ sɔ ɔbuai ma.

ref: Jesus asked her what she wanted, and she said, “When you come into your kingdom, please let one of my sons sit at your right side and the other at your left.”
text: Ne Yesu ɔkarɛ ma sɔ, “Be mito mibie?” Ne ma ɔnyi ɔtã mmuai sɔ, “Tã sɔ wũ mabi inyɔ magbe aasɛ i fɔ kuɖearɔ̃ gu fɔ kubena iso ku ɔwi gɔ aaɖe sigara.”

ref: Jesus answered, “Not one of you knows what you are asking. Are you able to drink from the cup that I must soon drink from?” James and John said, “Yes, we are!”
text: Yesu ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Miiɣe nnɛ mito mikarɛ. Miawo inyɛwe gu kukpi gɔ ame loaki ɔki?” Ne matã mmuai sɔ, “Aĩ, boto boawo.”

ref: Jesus replied, “You certainly will drink from my cup! But it isn't for me to say who will sit at my right side and at my left. This is for my Father to say.”
text: Mmɔ i Yesu to ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Gbaã iɖe sɔ mito miawo. Ɣɛɛ ne, lona ɔri sɔ loaɖi mma loasɛ i wũ kuɖearɔ̃ gu kubena iso. Kusɛkɔ̃ gɔmɔ ne, mma i wũ Ɔse ɔledza ɔsɛ ɔkpɛ ɔɖe.”

ref: When the ten other disciples heard this, they were angry with the two brothers.
text: Gɔ marasuãdze iweo tsɔra ɔnɔ itɔ̃me nɛgbe ne, managu manyiibi inyɔ magbe sikpã.

ref: But Jesus called the disciples together and said: You know foreign rulers like to order their people around. And their great leaders have full power over everyone they rule.
text: Ne ɔso Yesu ɔkpere ma ɔɖuɖu ɔsirakanya ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Miɣe sɔ mma loiɖe ma Yudase magara sɛ mate so mapia mma mato maɖe iso, fiɛ ma matɔ̃ɖedze ana sɛ maɖe ma iso ku ɔle ɔɖuɖu.

ref: But don't act like them. If you want to be great, you must be the servant of all the others.
text: Ɣɛɛ ne, idaawe i mi ame ngbɔ, si mi ndɛ̃ ɔrere to ɔbie sikpakpa ne, ɔbara so sumudze ɔtã matsɔra.

ref: And if you want to be first, you must be the slave of the rest.
text: Fiɛ si mi ɔrere to ɔbie sɔ ɔ̃aɖe ɔɖeakatɔ̃ ne, ɔbara so ɔɖabo ɔtã matsɔra.

ref: The Son of Man did not come to be a slave master, but a slave who will give his life to rescue many people. Jesus Heals Two Blind Men (Mark
text: Mme Ɔturi Awune Ɔbi kɔra ne, loiba sɔ masumu me, ɣɛɛ sɔ loasumu losu wũ ngbã lotã ala maturi gbodzoo iɖi ɔso.” Yesu Ɔsa Manɔbiɛdze Inyɔ.

ref: Jesus was followed by a large crowd as he and his disciples were leaving Jericho.
text: Ɔwi gɔ i Yesu gu ɔ̃ marasuãdze to mabɔrɛ i Yeriko ne, maturi gbodzoo ɔsiai ma.

ref: Two blind men were sitting beside the road. And when they heard that Jesus was coming their way, they shouted, “Lord and Son of David, have pity on us!”
text: Manɔbiɛdze inyɔ ma nsi i ɔri kuruɛ ɔnɔ sɔ Yesu to ɔfe, ne ɔso matsɛ kulu teteree sɔ, “Yesu, Igara David Ɔbi, nyɔ bo nnya!”

ref: They answered, “Lord, we want to see!”
text: Matã mmuai sɔ, “Tete, boto bobie sɔ boanya ara!”

ref: Jesus said: I will tell you a story about a man who had two sons. Then you can tell me what you think. The father went to the older son and said, “Go work in the vineyard today!”
text: “Nda minya itɔ̃me nɛgbe? Ɔrɛrɛ̃ ɔwɛ̃ ɔwe gɔ ɔba mabirɛrɛĩ inyɔ. Ɔɣere kpakpare sɔ, ‘Ɔbi, sɛ aabara karabara i kakpɛkɔ̃ ame nɔme.’

ref: But when they saw the man's son, they said, “Someday he will own the vineyard. Let's kill him! Then we can have it all for ourselves.”
text: “Ɣɛɛ gɔ marabaradze ɔnya ɔbi ne, maɣere so sɔ, ‘Kakpɛkɔ̃ sate ɔbi gɔ loaɖe ɔ̃ ara i ngbe ne. Mitã boɖoe wũ si boaɖe ɔ̃ ara.’

ref: If anyone asks why you are doing this, just say, ‘The Lord needs them.’ He will at once let you have the donkeys.”
text: Si ɔrere ɔɣɛ ira irere ne, miɣere wũ sɔ, ‘Bosate nto ɔbie ma’ iyɔ ɔluwɛ̃ ne, ɔto ɔ̃aɖi ma arɔĩ.”

ref: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet had said,
text: Iba ngbɔ gɔ itã sɔ nnɛ i kanyaɖidze ɔɣɛ aaba i ne ame sɔ,

ref: “Announce to the people of Jerusalem: ‘Your king is coming to you! He is humble and rides on a donkey. He comes on the colt of a donkey.’ ”
text: “Miɣere Zion kaɖe sɔ, ‘Nyɔ, fɔ igara to ɔba fɔ kɔrɛ! Ɔsi i ɔfirimu gu ɔ̃ ibi iso i karɔ̃ ibɔso ame ɔto ɔba.’ ”

ref: The disciples left and did what Jesus had told them to do.
text: Ne ɔso marasuãdze ɔrui masɛ maabara lɛ kumɛgɔ i Yesu ɔpia ma.

ref: They brought the donkey and its colt and laid some clothes on their backs. Then Jesus got on.
text: Makɔ ɔfirimu gu ɔ̃ ibi mabɔ, maɖi ma sikati masia i mabɔi iso fiɛ Yesu ɔta ɔsɛ i ma iso.

ref: When Jesus came to Jerusalem, everyone in the city was excited and asked, “Who can this be?”
text: Gɔ Yesu ɔbo Yerusalem ɔmagɛ̃ ame ne, kulu ko nto ɣee, ne maturi to makarɛ sɔ, “Ne nna ɔɖe ngbe?”

ref: But the chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses were angry when they saw his miracles and heard the children shouting praises to the Son of David.
text: Ɣɛɛ gɔ masɔrɛdze makpakpa gu mmara matedze ɔnya awawãra wa ɔto ɔbara gu kumɛgɔ i mabiɛtɛ to mala kulu i isɔrɛyo ame sɔ, “Maale David Ɔbi!” ne, ikpɛ̃ ma sikpã.

ref: When the chief priests and the Pharisees heard these stories, they knew Jesus was talking about them.
text: Gɔ masɔrɛdze makpakpa gu ma Farisise ɔnɔ agbã wa i Yesu ɔpɛ ne, matsue sɔ ma ɔto.

ref: He told them, “The Scriptures say, ‘My house should be called a place of worship.’ But you have turned it into a place where robbers hide.”
text: Ne ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Matsɛrɛ i Ɔko Sekelea ame sɔ Ɣaa ɔɣɛ sɔ, ‘Maarɔ wũ iyo sɔ kayi kakparamakɔ̃,’ ɣɛɛ miɔsu ne mibara mayukukpe kawɛrɛkɔ̃!”

ref: If you have faith when you pray, you will be given whatever you ask for.” A Question about Jesus' Authority (Mark
text: Si mifɔ Ɣaa miɖe ne, ira biara nɛ miakarɛ wũ i kayi ikparama ame ne, mito miafɔ ne.” Makarɛ Yesu Ngbegɔ Ɔna Ɔ̃ Ɔle Ɔbɔrɛgu.

ref: The men said to Jesus, “Don't you hear what those children are saying?” “Yes, I do!” Jesus answered. “Don't you know that the Scriptures say, ‘Children and infants will sing praises’?”
text: Ne mato makarɛ Yesu sɔ, “Ato anɔ nnɛ mato maɣɛ i ngbe?” Yesu ɔtã ma mmuai sɔ, “Nto lonɔ. Miika itɔ̃me nɛgbe minya i Ɔko Sekelea ame sɔ, ‘Akararã mabiɛtɛ sɔ ma kanya ame i ile nɛ loyi sɛ ibɔrɛ.’ ”

ref: Then Jesus left the city and went out to the village of Bethany, where he spent the night. Jesus Puts a Curse on a Fig Tree (Mark
text: Ne kama ne, Yesu ɔrui ɔnyua ma ɔkɛlɛ Betania ɔmagɛ̃ ame ngbegɔ ɔsɛ ɔ̃arɛ. Yesu Ɔgbarĩ Kuɖedziri Kuwɛ̃.

ref: Jesus answered, “I have just one question to ask you. If you answer it, I will tell you where I got the right to do these things.
text: Yesu ɔtã ma mmuai sɔ, “Mitã lokarɛ mi itɔ̃me iwɛ̃ lonɔ, si mitã me mmuai ne, nto loaɣere mi ɔle gɔ loto lobara ala ara wagbe.

ref: When Jesus got up the next morning, he was hungry. He started out for the city,
text: Kaɖesɛ̃a kaya tututu gɔ ɔ̃ɔkpese ɔto ɔba ɔmagɛ̃ ame ne, kukã ɔkpɛ̃ wũ.

ref: But Jesus said to them, “If you have faith and don't doubt, I promise you can do what I did to this tree. And you will be able to do even more. You can tell this mountain to get up and jump into the sea, and it will.
text: Ne Yesu ɔtã ma mmuai sɔ, “Nto loɣere mi gbaã sɔ si mifɔ miɖe fiɛ miiwararã ne, mito miawo nnɛ lobara kudziri gɔgbe ɔbara. Fiɛ iiɖe ne kere, ɣɛɛ mito miaɣere kube gɔgbe sɔ, ‘Kɛlɛ aafuĩdza so apia i ɔpo ame,’ fiɛ iaba ngbɔ.

ref: So they told Jesus, “We don't know.” Jesus said, “Then I won't tell you who gave me the right to do what I do.” A Story about Two Sons
text: Ne ɔso maɣere Yesu sɔ, “Boiɣe ngbegɔ i ɔ̃ ɔle ɔbɔrɛ.” Ne Yesu ɔ̃ wũ ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Mme wũ loibaɣere mi ngbegɔ i wũ ɔle gɔ loto lobara ala karabara gagbe ɔbɔrɛ.” Yesu Ɔte Ara I Manyiibi Inyɔ Mawɛ̃ Iso.

ref: Jesus told the chief priests and leaders to listen to this story: A land owner once planted a vineyard. He built a wall around it and dug a pit to crush the grapes in. He also built a lookout tower. Then he rented out his vineyard and left the country.
text: Yesu ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Minɔ igbã mama ana. Kpɛ̃dze ɔwɛ̃ ɔwe ku ɔwi gɔ lofe gɔ ɔkpɛ̃ adziribi wa masɛ masu mabara ala ndã kakpɛkɔ̃. Ɔtɛ̃ ɔkpã ɔki ɔlɔ kakpɛkɔ̃, ɔɖi itawo nɛ ame maakã ndã, fiɛ ka ɔtsue iyo kɔlɛa ngbegɔ i ngɔ loadzuɛ̃ kakpɛkɔ̃ aaya. Ɔsu kakpɛkɔ̃ ɔpia i marabaradze nrɔɔ̃ ame fiɛ ɔkɛlɛ ɔri.

ref: Jesus had gone into the temple and was teaching when the chief priests and the leaders of the people came up to him. They asked, “What right do you have to do these things? Who gave you this authority?”
text: Yesu ɔkpese ɔba isɔrɛyo ame ne gɔ ɔto ɔte ara ne, masɔrɛdze makpakpa gu makpakpa ɔba ɔ̃ kɔrɛ makarɛ wũ sɔ, “Lɛ ana ɔle gɔ ato abara ala ara wagbe fiɛ nna lotã-ɔ ɔle gɔmɔ?”

ref: Who gave John the right to baptize? Was it God in heaven or merely some human being?” They thought it over and said to each other, “We can't say God gave John this right. Jesus will ask us why we didn't believe John.
text: Yohanes ne, nna lotã wũ ɔle fiɛ ɔpie maturi Ɣaa ndu? Ɣaa ɛɛbɛrɛ maturi lotã wũ?” Ne matsɛgu so iwararã sɔ, “Be boaɣɛ? Si boɣɛ sɔ Ɣaa kɔrɛ ne, ɔto ɔ̃akarɛ bo sɔ ‘Be ɔso miifɔ Yohanes miɖe?’

ref: On the other hand, these people think John was a prophet, and we are afraid of what they might do to us. That's why we can't say it was merely some human who gave John the right to baptize.”
text: Ɣɛɛ si boɣɛ ana sɔ ‘Maturi kɔrɛ’ ne, boto bonigã maturi alasɔ ma ɔɖuɖu ɔfɔ maɖe sɔ Ɣaa kanyaɖidze ɔɖe.”

ref: His son told him he would not do it, but later he changed his mind and went.
text: “Ɔbi ɔɣere ɔ̃ ɔse sɔ, ‘Loibasɛ,’ ɣɛɛ ne kama ne, ɔfinikira ɔ̃ adzuni ɔrui ɔkɛlɛ sia.

ref: The man then told his younger son to go work in the vineyard. The boy said he would, but he didn't go.
text: “Ɔse ɔkpere ɔbi nyɔare ɔpia wũ katɔ̃me gagbe ame. Ɔtɔrã i ɔ̃ ɔse katɔ̃ sɔ ɔ̃asɛ, ɣɛɛ ɔ̃isɛ.

ref: Which one of the sons obeyed his father? “The older one,” the chief priests and leaders answered. Then Jesus told them: You can be sure tax collectors and prostitutes will get into the kingdom of God before you ever will!
text: “Mabi inyɔ magbe ne, ma ɔmele lobara ɔ̃ ɔse kuɖɔɛ?” Matã mmuai sɔ, “Kpakpare.” Ne Yesu ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Nto loɣere mi sɔ, ma lampofɔdze gu asɔrɔ̃ maɖedze ɔɔɖe mi katɔ̃ makpa Ɣaa sigarakaɖekɔ̃.

ref: When John the Baptist showed you how to do right, you would not believe him. But these evil people did believe. And even when you saw what they did, you still would not change your minds and believe. Renters of a Vineyard (Mark
text: Alasɔ Ndupiedze Yohanes ɔba ɔ̃ate mi sibɔlɔlɔ ɔri, ɣɛɛ miifɔ wũ miɖe, ɣɛɛ ma lampofɔdze gu asɔrɔ̃maɖedze ɔfɔ wũ maɖe. Atoa sɔ minya nwagbe ne, miifinikira mi adzuni mifɔ wũ miɖe.” Yesu Ɔpɛ Igbã I Marabaradze Lalaa Mawɛ̃ Iso.

ref: When it was harvest time, the owner sent some servants to get his share of the grapes.
text: Gɔ adziribi ɔsɔrɛ̃wi ɔɣo ne, ɔpia ɔ̃ maɖabo sɔ masɛ maafɔ ɔ̃ kaɖekɔ̃ mabɔ wũ i marabaradze kɔrɛ.

ref: But the renters grabbed those servants. They beat up one, killed one, and stoned one of them to death.
text: “Marabaradze ɔmɔɛ̃ maɖabo magbe, mapɛ ɔwɛ̃, maɖoe ɔwɛ̃, fiɛ mapɛ ɔwɛ̃ ata.

ref: He then sent more servants than he did the first time. But the renters treated them in the same way.
text: Ɔrɛrɛ̃ gɔgbe ɔledza maɖabo mama isese iɖo maɖeakatɔ̃. Ma wũ ne, marabaradze ɔbara ma lɛ maɖeakatɔ̃.

ref: Finally, the owner sent his own son to the renters, because he thought they would respect him.
text: Kawirikɔ̃ ne, ɔsese ɔ̃ ɔbi ala ɔɣere so sɔ, ‘Loɣe sɔ mato maawarɛ wũ ɔbi.’

ref: So they grabbed him, threw him out of the vineyard, and killed him.
text: Ne ɔso mamɔɛ̃ wũ mafuĩdza i ɔkpã kama fiɛ maɖoe wũ.”

ref: Jesus asked, “When the owner of that vineyard comes, what do you suppose he will do to those renters?”
text: Ne Yesu ɔkarɛ ma sɔ, “Be mifɔ miɖe sɔ kakpɛkɔ̃ sate aabara marabaradze magbe si ɔba?”

ref: The chief priests and leaders answered, “He will kill them in some horrible way. Then he will rent out his vineyard to people who will give him his share of grapes at harvest time.”
text: Matã mmuai sɔ, “Ɔto ɔ̃aɖoe marabaradze lalaa magbe ɔsu kakpɛkɔ̃ ɔtã marabaradze mama ma loasu ɔ̃ kaɖekɔ̃ mabɔ wũ i ɔwi iso si masɔrɛ̃ ɔraɖui.”

ref: Jesus replied, “You surely know that the Scriptures say, ‘The stone the builders tossed aside is now the most important stone of all. This is something the Lord has done, and it is amazing to us.’
text: Ne Yesu ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Miika i Ɔko Sekelea ame sɔ, ‘Ita nɛ i mayotsuedze ɔsɛ̃ sɔ iilɛ ne, ne loba iakpese ita nɛ lofɔ kuɣa iɖo ata ɔɖuɖu pelepele ne. Bosate lobara ira siare nɛgbe fiɛ ibo bo anɔ gbaã!’ ”

ref: I tell you God's kingdom will be taken from you and given to people who will do what he demands.
text: Yesu ɔkɛlɛgu i katɔ̃ sɔ, “Ɣaa to ɔ̃amɔɛ̃ ɔ̃ sigarakaɖekɔ̃ ɔfɔ mi iso ɔsu ɔtã mma loaɣɔ̃ abi sɛɛ.

ref: Anyone who stumbles over this stone will be crushed, and anyone it falls on will be smashed to pieces.”
text: Ngɔ loakpase i itã nɛgbe iso ne, ɔto ɔ̃afɛfɛ̃ ɖukuɖuku, fiɛ ngɔ i ita nɛgbe aakpase iwuĩ ne, ito iakɔtɛ wũ ɖɛkpɛrɛɛ.”

ref: So they looked for a way to arrest Jesus. But they were afraid to, because the people thought he was a prophet.
text: Ne ɔso mabie sɔ si mamɔɛ̃ wũ, ɣɛɛ manigã maturi gbodzoo ma mpia i mmɔ ala masɛ mabu Yesu lɛ Ɣaa kanyaɖidze awe.

ref: The Pharisees got together and planned how they could trick Jesus into saying something wrong.
text: Ma Farisise ɔsɛ maabara adzuni sɔ maamɔɛ̃ Yesu i ma atɔ̃me ikarɛ ame.

ref: Teacher, Moses wrote that if a married man dies and has no children, his brother should marry the widow. Their first son would then be thought of as the son of the dead brother.
text: “Ratedze, Mose ɔɣɛ sɔ si ɔrɛrɛ̃ ɔkpi ɔɣɛ ɔ̃ ɔre fiɛ maiɣe ɔbi ne, ɔ̃ ɔnyiibi si ɔsu ɔrɔ̃go ɔyiri si maɣe mabi. Maanyɔ mabi ma maaɣe mmɔ lɛ ɔ̃ ɔnyiibi gɔ lokpi mabi.

ref: Once again Jesus used stories to teach the people:
text: Yesu ɔledza agbã isu ite ma ara sɔ,

ref: The kingdom of heaven is like what happened when a king gave a wedding banquet for his son.
text: “Ɣaa sigarakaɖekɔ̃ se lɛ ngbe. Ɔwi ɔwɛ̃ ame ne, igara ɔwɛ̃ ɔkɔ ɔrɔ̃go ɔtã ɔ̃ ɔbi.

ref: The king sent some servants to tell the invited guests to come to the banquet, but the guests refused.
text: Ɔpia ɔ̃ maɖabo sɔ masɛ maakpere mma matã itsue sɔ maba iyi kaɖekɔ̃ sɔ ka maba, ɣɛɛ masɛ̃ sɔ maito maba.

ref: He sent other servants to say to the guests, “The banquet is ready! My cattle and prize calves have all been prepared. Everything is ready. Come to the banquet!”
text: “Ne ɔso ɔpia maɖabo mama sɔ maɣere ma sɔ, ‘Lotã maɖoe mabɔi gu ma nantsuĩ ma mba iɖa, mawa aɖera masɛ, ira biara ɔɔkote, ne ɔso ka miba wũ ɔpɔrɔ̃ gɔ lotu lotã irɔ̃gokɔ katɔ̃!’

ref: But the guests did not pay any attention. Some of them left for their farms, and some went to their places of business.
text: “Ɣɛɛ maturi ma makpere iipia wũ kanya. Makɛlɛ ma kurabara iso, ɔwɛ̃ ɔkɛlɛ sia, ɔwɛ̃ ana ɔkɛlɛ kuɣa kaɖekɔ̃.

ref: Others grabbed the servants, then beat them up and killed them.
text: Mawɛ̃ ana ɔmɔɛ̃ maɖabo mamɔ mapɛ ma, maɖoe mma.

ref: This made the king so furious that he sent an army to kill those murderers and burn down their city.
text: Sikpã ɔmɔɛ̃ igara gɔmɔ gbaã, ne ɔso ɔpia ɔ̃ makpakpɛ̃dze masɛ maaɖoe maturiɖoese mamɔ, matsuedza ma ɔmagɛ̃.

ref: Then he said to the servants, “It is time for the wedding banquet, and the invited guests don't deserve to come.
text: “Ne ɔkpere ɔ̃ maɖabo ɔɣere ma sɔ, ‘Wũ ɔrɔ̃go ikɔ aɖera wa mawa ka ase, ɣɛɛ mma lokpere sɔ maba maaɖe ara iikote.

ref: Go out to the street corners and tell everyone you meet to come to the banquet.”
text: Ne ɔso mikɛlɛ siri iso si mikpere ɔbiara gɔ mianya mibɔ.’

ref: When the king went in to meet the guests, he found that one of them wasn't wearing the right kind of clothes for the wedding.
text: “Gɔ igara ɔba ɔ̃anyɔ mma loba aɖera kaɖekɔ̃ ne, ɔnya ɔrɛrɛ̃ ɔwɛ̃ gɔ loipia awu wa masɛ mapia i ɔrɔ̃go kakɔkɔ̃.

ref: Tell us what you think! Should we pay taxes to the Emperor or not?”
text: Ne ɔso ɣere bo fɔ adzuni. Lɛ sɔ boatã Roma Igarakpakpa lampo ɛɛbɛrɛ ita iya i bo mmara iso?”

ref: The same thing happened to the second and third brothers and finally to all seven of them.
text: Ngbɔ ame ibara ɔbirɛrɛĩ nyɔare ɔtɛare gbɔgbɔɔgbɔ isɛ iabo ɔkɔdzɛva ne.

ref: They sent some of their followers and some of Herod's followers to say to him, “Teacher, we know that you are honest. You teach the truth about what God wants people to do. And you treat everyone with the same respect, no matter who they are.
text: Ne ɔso mapia ma marasuãdze gu Herodes masiaidze mawɛ̃ ɔ̃ kɔrɛ makarɛ wũ sɔ, “Ratedze, boɣe sɔ asɛ aɣɛ ɔnukuare, asɛ ate maturi nnɛ nɖe Ɣaa kuɖɔɛ gɔ aisɛ akã siruɛ atsue alasɔ aisɛ anyɔ ɔturi i katɔ̃.

ref: Let me see one of the coins used for paying taxes.” They brought him a silver coin,
text: Mitã me sikã dzɛ misɛ mitã ala lampo lonyɔ.” Ne masu mabɔ wũ ne.

ref: “The Emperor's,” they answered. Then Jesus told them, “Give the Emperor what belongs to him and give God what belongs to God.”
text: Matã wũ mmuai sɔ, “Roma Igarakpakpa ire iɖe.” Ne Yesu ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Misu nnɛ nɖe Kaisaro ire mitã wũ si misu nnɛ nɖe Ɣaa ire ana mitã Ɣaa.”

ref: His answer surprised them so much that they walked away. Life in the Future World (Mark
text: Gɔ manɔ nnɛgbe ne, ibara ma ɣii, ne marui maɣɛ wũ ne. Yesu Ɔte Ara I Makpise Iledza Ita Iso.

ref: At last the woman died.
text: Kawirikɔ̃ ne, ɔrɔ̃go ɔ̃ wũ ɔba ɔ̃akpi.

ref: All the Law of Moses and the Books of the Prophets are based on these two commandments. About David's Son (Mark
text: Mmara aka anyɔ wagbe iso matsue Mose mmara ɔɖuɖu gu Kanyamaɖidze ara ite masia ne.” Nna Ɔbi Nɖe Kristo?

ref: The Sadducees did not believe people would rise to life after death. So that same day some of the Sadducees came to Jesus and said:
text: Iyi nɛmɔ ame ne, ma Zadukise ma nsɛ maɣɛ sɔ makpise iisibata ɔba Yesu kɔrɛ maba maaɣere wũ sɔ,

ref: Jesus answered: You are completely wrong! You don't know what the Scriptures teach. And you don't know anything about the power of God.
text: Yesu ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Mmɔ miɣɛrɛ ne, alasɔ miiɣe Ɔko Sekelea ame atɔ̃me fiɛ miiɣe Ɣaa ɔle ana.

ref: When God raises people to life, they won't marry. They will be like the angels in heaven.
text: Alasɔ si makpise ɔta ne, maase lɛ Ɣaa makpabo ma i kato fiɛ maibayiri so.

ref: And as for people being raised to life, God was speaking to you when he said,
text: Ɣɛɛ i makpise ita iso ne, miika nnɛ i Ɣaa ɔɣere mi? Ɔɣɛ sɔ,

ref: “I am the God worshiped by Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.” He isn't the God of the dead, but of the living.
text: ‘Mme nɖe Abraham Ɣaa gu Isak Ɣaa gu Yakob Ɣaa ne.’ Magbanadze Ɣaa ɔɖe ne iiɖe makpise ɔre ɔɖe.”

ref: The crowds were surprised to hear what Jesus was teaching. The Most Important Commandment (Mark
text: Gɔ maturi gbodzoo ma mpia mmɔ ɔnɔ ara wagbe ne, ɔ̃ ara ite ɔbara ma ɣii. Yesu Ɔte Mmara Mɛ Nɖe Misiare Miɖo.

ref: After Jesus had made the Sadducees look foolish, the Pharisees heard about it and got together.
text: Gɔ ma Farisise ɔnɔ kumɛgɔ i Yesu mmuai ɔpia ma Zadukise ibɔra i kanya ne, maba maasakanya.

ref: “Teacher, what is the most important commandment in the Law?”
text: Ɔkarɛ wũ sɔ, “Ratedze, mme mmara lomɔ miɖo?”

ref: This is the first and most important commandment.
text: Mmɛgbe nɖe mmara ɖeakatɔ̃ gu mmɛ lonya miɖo pelepele ne.

ref: The second most important commandment is like this one. And it is, “Love others as much as you love yourself.”
text: Minyɔare mɛ ka lonya se lɛ miɖeakatɔ̃, ‘Ɖɔɛ fɔ ɔnyii lɛ kumɛgɔ ato aɖɔɛ so.’

ref: While the Pharisees were still there, Jesus asked them,
text: Gɔ ma Farisise mawɛ̃ ɔsakanya ne, Yesu ɔkarɛ ma sɔ,

ref: ‘The Lord said to my Lord: Sit at my right side until I make your enemies into a footstool for you.’
text: ‘Ɣaa ɔɣere Bosate sɔ, “Sɛ i wũ kuɖearɔ̃ iso gbɔgbɔɔgbɔ loatã asu ngba asia i fɔ matsirise iso.” ’

ref: “What do you think about the Messiah? Whose family will he come from?” They answered, “He will be a son of King David.”
text: “Nna mibu sɔ Kristo ɖe? Nna ɔbi ɔɖe?” Matã mmuai sɔ, “Igara David Ɔwa tɛtɛ ɔɖe.”

ref: Jesus replied, “How then could the Spirit lead David to call the Messiah his Lord? David said,
text: Ne Yesu ɔkarɛ ma sɔ, “Si igara David ɔbi ɔɖe ne, lɛ ise ɔso i David ɔki i Ɣaa Siwarã ɔle ame ɔkpere wũ sɔ Bosate? David ɔɣɛ sɔ,

ref: If David called the Messiah his Lord, how can the Messiah be a son of King David?”
text: Si igara David ɔkpere wũ Bosate ne, nda i Kristo aaɖe ɔ̃ ɔbi ɔɣɛ?”

ref: No one was able to give Jesus an answer, and from that day on, no one dared ask him any more questions.
text: Ma kuwɛ̃ iiwo Yesu mmuai ɔtã, ne ɔso ita iyi nɛmɔ iso ne, ma kuwɛ̃ iisikarɛ wũ kuira.

ref: If you put yourself above others, you will be put down. But if you humble yourself, you will be honored.
text: Ɔbiara gɔ lokɔlɛ so kato ne, maabɔ wũ karɔ̃ fiɛ ɔbiara gɔ lobɔ so karɔ̃ ne, makɔlɛ wũ kato. Ndzo Miɖe Mitã Maladze.

ref: And now your temple will be deserted.
text: Ne ɔso fɔ Isɔrɛyo to iakpese iyoku.

ref: Whoever is the greatest should be the servant of the others.
text: Ngɔ nto ɔbie iyere i mi ndɛ̃ si ɔkpese mi ɔɖabo

ref: Jerusalem, Jerusalem! Your people have killed the prophets and have stoned the messengers who were sent to you. I have often wanted to gather your people, as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings. But you wouldn't let me.
text: “Yerusalem, Yerusalem, asɛ aɖoe kanyamaɖidze ma i Ɣaa sɛ ɔpia fɔ kɔrɛ, fiɛ asɛ apɛ Ɣaa maɖabo ma ɔsɛ ɔpia-ɔ ata. Loɔkpɛ̃ sɔ si losu nrɔɔ̃ lofu-ɔ lɛ kumɛgɔ i kɔkɔ ɔnyi sɛ ɔwarɛ abue ɔfu akɔkɔibi, ɣɛɛ asɛ̃ me.

ref: Anyone who swears by the altar also swears by everything on it.
text: Ne ɔso si ɔturi ɔtarĩgu isɔrɛkasege ne, ta ara wa nsia i isɔrɛkasege iso ɔɖuɖu iɖe ɔtarĩgu.

ref: Jesus said to the crowds and to his disciples:
text: Ne Yesu ɔɖegu maturi gbodzoo gu ɔ̃ marasuãdze ika ne.

ref: The Pharisees and the teachers of the Law are experts in the Law of Moses.
text: Ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Mmara matedze gu ma Farisise nɖe mma maɖi ɔri sɔ mate Mose mmara.

ref: So obey everything they teach you, but don't do as they do. After all, they say one thing and do something else.
text: Ne ɔso mikã ma atsue si mibara ibiara nɛ maɣere mi sɔ mibara, ɣɛɛ ne, midaasiai ma kurabarara, alasɔ maisɛ mabara i nnɛ masɛ mate iso.

ref: They pile heavy burdens on people's shoulders and won't lift a finger to help.
text: Masɛ manyi ato wa mba ɔrɔrã fiɛ asɛ aɖa isore masorera maturi, ɣɛɛ maisɛ mabie sɔ maapiagu ma kɔrɔ̃ si mabuai ma masore.

ref: Everything they do is just to show off in front of others. They even make a big show of wearing Scripture verses on their foreheads and arms, and they wear big tassels for everyone to see.
text: “Masɛ mabara ira biara sɔ maturi aanyɔ ma. Minyɔ Ɣaa atɔ̃me siare wa matsɛrɛ i ɔkati iso masu manyi so iti gu nrɔɔ̃ si minyɔ kumɛgɔ i ma awu atsueti amɔ fiɛ akarã!

ref: They love the best seats at banquets and the front seats in the synagogues.
text: Si makpere ma i ɔpɔrɔ̃ katukɔ̃ ne, masɛ maɖɔɛ kusɛkɔ̃ kɔlɛa. Masɛ maɖɔɛ masɛ i kusɛkɔ̃ sɛɛ gu kusɛkɔ̃ kɔlɛa i ma kusarɛkɔ̃ ana.

ref: And when they are in the market, they like to have people greet them as their teachers.
text: Masɛ maɖɔɛ sɔ maturi si maya ma ku ibu i kuɣa ame si makpere ma ratedze.

ref: But none of you should be called a teacher. You have only one teacher, and all of you are like brothers and sisters.
text: “Madaakpere mi sɔ ratedze, alasɔ mi ɔɖuɖu ne, manyii miɖe fiɛ ratedze ɔwɛ̃ saã miba.

ref: Don't call anyone on earth your father. All of you have the same Father in heaven.
text: Midaakpere kuwɛ̃ i karɔ̃ iso ngbe sɔ Tete alasɔ ɔse ɔwɛ̃ saã miba gɔ mpia i kato.

ref: None of you should be called the leader. The Messiah is your only leader.
text: Madaakpere mi sɔ Tɔ̃ɖedze alasɔ mi tɔ̃ɖedze ɔwɛ̃ saã gɔ miba nɖe Kristo.

ref: You Pharisees and teachers of the Law of Moses are in for trouble! You're nothing but show-offs. You lock people out of the kingdom of heaven. You won't go in yourselves, and you keep others from going in.
text: Ndzo miɖe mitã mi mmara matedze gu ma Farisise! Miɖɔɛ Ɣaa i anɔmi anɔmi ame kere! Alasɔ misɛ miɖe makpise mare amumu, fiɛ misɛ mikparama kayi dzoroo i iteso ame. Nwagbe ɔso ne, mi kutsue ikpadzɛ̃ to iamɔ!

ref: You Pharisees and teachers of the Law of Moses are in for trouble! You're nothing but show-offs. You travel over land and sea to win one follower. And when you have done so, you make that person twice as fit for hell as you are.
text: “Ndzo miɖe mitã mi mmara matedze gu ma Farisise! Miɖɔɛ Ɣaa i anɔmi anɔmi ame kere! Alasɔ misɛ miwara ɔpo misɛ nrɔ̃ agbãagbã iso misɛ miaɖi ɔturi ɔwɛ̃ mitã Ɣaa, ɣɛɛ si mibara ngbɔ miro ne, misɛ mibara wũ ngɔ lonyanyarĩ ɔɖo mi i itɔkpe ame ikɛlɛ ame!

ref: You are in for trouble! You are supposed to lead others, but you are blind. You teach that it doesn't matter if a person swears by the temple. But you say it does matter if someone swears by the gold in the temple.
text: “Ndzo miɖe mitã mi ma nto mikɔ maturi ɣɛɛ manɔbiɛdze miɖe! Misɛ mite sɔ, ‘Si ɔturi ɔtarĩgu Ɣaa isɔrɛyo ne, mmara iinyi wũ, ɣɛɛ si ɔtarĩgu sikã dzɛ nse i isɔrɛyo ne, ɔ̃ i mmara ɔnyi ne.’

ref: You blind fools! Which is greater, the gold or the temple that makes the gold sacred?
text: Manɔbiɛdze dzimifɔ! Imele lonya iɖo? Sikã ɛɛbɛrɛ isɔrɛyo nɛ losekelera sikã?

ref: You also teach that it doesn't matter if a person swears by the altar. But you say it does matter if someone swears by the gift on the altar.
text: Misɛ mite ana sɔ, ‘Si ɔturi ɔtarĩgu isɔrɛkasege ga iso masɛ mafɛ̃ ara matã Ɣaa ne, mmara iinyi wũ, ɣɛɛ si ɔtarĩgu karatã ga nsia i isɔrɛkasege iso ɣɛɛ ne, mmara ɔnyi wũ.’

ref: Are you blind? Which is more important, the gift or the altar that makes the gift sacred?
text: Anɔmi ɔbiɛ mi gbaã! Imele lonya iɖo? Karatã ɛɛbɛrɛ isɔrɛkasege ga losekelera karatã?

ref: And anyone who swears by the temple also swears by God, who lives there.
text: Fiɛ si ɔturi ɔtarĩgu Ɣaa isɔrɛyo ne, ɔtarĩgu ta Ɣaa gɔ nsi i mmɔ.

ref: To swear by heaven is the same as swearing by God's throne and by the one who sits on that throne.
text: Fiɛ si ɔrere ɔtarĩgu Kato ne, ɔtarĩgu Ɣaa sigaraiyara gu ngɔ nsi iso.

ref: You Pharisees and teachers are show-offs, and you're in for trouble! You give God a tenth of the spices from your garden, such as mint, dill, and cumin. Yet you neglect the more important matters of the Law, such as justice, mercy, and faithfulness. These are the important things you should have done, though you should not have left the others undone either.
text: “Ndzo miɖe mitã mi mmara matedze gu ma Farisise! Mi ma nsɛ mibara ara sɔ si mibo maturi anɔ! Misɛ miɖi mi araɖea ika weova ta aposarɛbi, ibura sikɔma agbãagbã mitã Ɣaa. Ɣɛɛ misɛ̃ sɔ miibabara ara wa lonya aɖo i mmara ame wa nɖe nnyainyɔ, atɔ̃me iɣɛ i ɔri iso gu ɔnukuare ame. Ara wagbe ikote sɔ miasu mibua i nwa mimɔɛ̃ mito iso ne. Ɣɛɛ miibanyua kuiwɛ̃ misɛ.

ref: You blind leaders! You strain out a small fly but swallow a camel.
text: Matɔ̃ɖedze nɔbiɛdze! Misɛ mitua ɔnidzɔ gɔ lobo i mi ndã ame ɣɛɛ misɛ mimɛ kapuipɔngɔ!

ref: You Pharisees and teachers are show-offs, and you're in for trouble! You wash the outside of your cups and dishes, while inside there is nothing but greed and selfishness.
text: “Ndzo miɖe mitã mi mmara matedze gu ma Farisise! Mi ma nsɛ mibara ara sɔ si mibo maturi anɔ! Misɛ mifore mi akɔi gu arɔi kama, ɣɛɛ wã kame ɔyi ku ara wa miɖe maturi amumu mifɔ ku so ɔle.

ref: You blind Pharisee! First clean the inside of a cup, and then the outside will also be clean.
text: Farisise nɔbiɛdze, fore ikɔi ame tɔtɔ, iyɔ kama ka ito iare sekelee!

ref: You Pharisees and teachers are in for trouble! You're nothing but show-offs. You're like tombs that have been whitewashed. On the outside they are beautiful, but inside they are full of bones and filth.
text: “Ndzo miɖe mitã mi mmara matedze gu ma Farisise! Mi ma nsɛ mibara ara sɔ si mibo maturi anɔ! Mise lɛ maturi akpibiɔ wa matsue makua kukaakɔ, ɣɛɛ wã ame ɔyi ku makpise buria ku akui.

ref: That's what you are like. Outside you look good, but inside you are evil and only pretend to be good.
text: Ngbɔ ame i maturi ɔɖuɖu anɔmi ame ne, mire maturi sɛɛ, ɣɛɛ mi ame ɔyi ku mila gu akpi. Yesu Ɔɣɛ Itɔ̃me I Mmara Matedze Gu Ma Farisise Kutsue Ikpadzɛ̃ Iso.

ref: You Pharisees and teachers are nothing but show-offs, and you're in for trouble! You build monuments for the prophets and decorate the tombs of good people.
text: “Ndzo miɖe mitã mi mmara matedze gu ma Farisise! Mi ma nsɛ mibara ara sɔ si mibo maturi anɔ! Misɛ mitsue akpibiɔ mitã Ɣaa kanyamaɖidze fiɛ misɛ miledza mma losɛ ngbã sɛɛ akpibiɔ kukaakɔ.

ref: And you claim you would not have taken part with your ancestors in killing the prophets.
text: Misɛ miɣɛ sɔ, ‘Si bo nsi ngbã ku bo mawa ɔwi ne, tee boiɖoe kanyamaɖidze mamɔ lɛ bo mawa awe.’

ref: But you prove you really are the relatives of the ones who killed the prophets.
text: Ne ɔso mito misia ita sɔ mma loɖoe Ɣaa kanyamaɖidze mawa miɖe.

ref: So keep on doing everything they did.
text: Mikɛlɛ i katɔ̃ si mibara ara wa i mi mawa ɔsɛ karɔ̃ miwiri.

ref: You are nothing but snakes and the children of snakes! How can you escape going to hell?
text: “Maturi lalaa! Mise lɛ maɣɛ gu ma mabi. Mifɔ miɖe sɔ miawo ɔtere i itɔkpe kutsue ikpadzɛ̃ nɛ nto iba kanya?

ref: I can promise that you people living today will be punished for all these things! Jesus Loves Jerusalem (Luke
text: Nto losia ita lotã mi sɔ ituriɖoe nɛgbe ɔɖuɖu ne, ne kutsue ikpadzɛ̃ to iaba kiniɔgbe maturi iso! Yerusalem Ara Ɔɖo Yesu Kayiri.

ref: You won't see me again until you say, “Blessed is the one who comes in the name of the Lord.”
text: Ita i kiniɔ ne, aisibanya me gbɔgbɔɔgbɔ miaɣɛ sɔ, ‘Ɣaa kusɛkusɛ si kuwegu ngɔ nto ɔba i Bosate iyere ame.’ ”

ref: After Jesus left the temple, his disciples came over and said, “Look at all these buildings!”
text: Gɔ Yesu ɔbɔrɛ i Ɣaa isɔrɛyo ɔto ɔkɛlɛ ne, ɔ̃ marasuãdze ɔba maakarɛ wũ si ɔkarã inɔ i kumɛgɔ matsue ne ayo iso.

ref: Jesus replied, “Do you see these buildings? They will certainly be torn down! Not one stone will be left in place.” Warning about Trouble (Mark
text: Ne ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Minyɔ ara wagbe ɔɖuɖu kukaakɔ. Alasɔ gbaã nto loɣere mi sɔ mato maabiɛbiɛ wã ɔɖuɖu gɔ ita kuiwɛ̃ iibakuti isia i ne ɔlaa iso sɔ maibasisi mapɛyu.” Yesu Ɔɣere Ɔ̃ Marasuãdze Ɔɣiã Gɔ Maaɖe.

ref: Many will come and claim to be me. They will say they are the Messiah, and they will fool many people.
text: Alasɔ maturi gbodzoo to maasu wũ iyere maba maaɣere mi sɔ, ‘Mme nɖe Kristo,’ fiɛ maasɔsɔla maturi gbodzoo.

ref: You will soon hear about wars and threats of wars, but don't be afraid. These things will have to happen first, but that isn't the end.
text: Mito mianɔ kakpã gu kakpã iso atɔ̃me i mi kɔrɛ gu kadzorokɔ̃ ɣɛɛ midaamararã so, ara wagbe igbã to aaba ɣɛɛ iite sɔ kawirikɔ̃ ɔɔɣo.

ref: Later, as Jesus was sitting on the Mount of Olives, his disciples came to him in private and asked, “When will this happen? What will be the sign of your coming and of the end of the world?”
text: Gɔ Yesu ɔsɛ ɔ̃asɛ i Nnɔĩ Adziri Kube iso ne, ɔ̃ marasuãdze ɔba maakarɛ wũ pɔkɔsɔɔ sɔ, “Ɣere bo ɔwi gɔ i ara wagbe aaba, ɔwi gɔ aakpese aba gu nnɛ loate sɔ kawirikɔ̃ ɔɔɣo.”

ref: Nations and kingdoms will go to war against each other. People will starve to death, and in some places there will be earthquakes.
text: Karɔ̃ kawɛ̃ iso maturi to maakpɛ̃gu karɔ̃ mama iso maturi, sigarakaɖekɔ̃ to siakpɛ̃gu sigarakaɖekɔ̃, kukã gu karɔ̃ iti to iaba i karɔ̃ agbãagbã iso.

ref: It will be a terrible time for women who are expecting babies or nursing young children.
text: Ndzo miɖe mitã mayirise gu matalɛpo ma nto mabi ɣɛtɛ ku ɔwi gɔmɔ ame!

ref: Jesus answered: Don't let anyone fool you.
text: Yesu ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Midzuɛ̃ so sɔ kuwɛ̃ iibasɔsɔla mi.

ref: But this is just the beginning of troubles.
text: Nwagbe ɔɖuɖu se lɛ kuɣe iɖoɖo ibo ɖeakatɔ̃.

ref: You will be arrested, punished, and even killed. Because of me, you will be hated by people of all nations.
text: “Mato maamɔɛ̃ mi matã makpadzɛ̃ kutsue fiɛ maaɖoe mi. Nrɔ̃ ɔɖuɖu iso maturi to maatsiri mi ala wũ ɔso.

ref: Many will give up and will betray and hate each other.
text: Maturi gbodzoo to maasɛ̃ ma kafɔkaɖe ku ɔwi gɔmɔ ame fiɛ maaɖi ma malaa matã, matsiri so.

ref: Many false prophets will come and fool a lot of people.
text: Mila kanyamaɖidze gbodzoo to maabɔrɛ masɔsɔla maturi gbodzoo.

ref: Someone may say, “Here is the Messiah!” or “There he is!” But don't believe it.
text: Ne ɔso si ɔrere ɔba ɔ̃aɣere mi sɔ, ‘Nyɔ, ɔɔnya Kristo i ngbe,’ ɣee ɔrere ɔɣere mi sɔ, ‘Ɔpia i mmɔ’ ne, midaafɔ miɖe.

ref: And pray that you won't have to escape in winter or on a Sabbath.
text: Mitã Ɣaa iti sɔ ɔwi gɔ miatere iibaɖe ɔyu ɔwi ɣee iwarãyi iso.

ref: Someday you will see that “Horrible Thing” in the holy place, just as the prophet Daniel said. Everyone who reads this must try to understand!
text: “Mito mianya Ɣaa itsirira ɔba iaya i kasekelekɔ̃, ngbegɔ iikote sɔ iaya lɛ kumɛgɔ i Ɣaa kanyaɖidze Daniel ɔɣɛ ɔsɛ. Ngɔ nto ɔka ɔko gɔgbe si ɔpia kubɛ sɔ ɔ̃anɔ karɔ̃.

ref: If you are living in Judea at that time, run to the mountains.
text: “Ɔwi gɔmɔ ame ne, mma mpia i Yudea si matere makɛlɛ abe iso.

ref: If you are out in the field, don't go back for your coat.
text: Ngɔ mpia i sia ana si ɔdaakɛlɛ ayo ame sɔ ɔ̃asu ɔ̃ awu.

ref: If God doesn't make the time shorter, no one will be left alive. But because of God's chosen ones, he will make the time shorter.
text: Ɣɛɛ ne, Ɣaa ɔɔtu ayi wamɔ koko alasɔ si iiɖe ngbɔ ne, kuwɛ̃ iibana iɖi, ɣɛɛ Ɣaa maturi ma ɔɖi ɔsɛ so ɔso ne, ɔ̃abɛrɛkɛ i ayi wamɔ iso.

ref: So when you see all these things happening, you will know the time has almost come.
text: Ngbɔ ame si minya sɔ ara wagbe ɔɖuɖu ka ato aba ne, mitsue sɔ kawirikɔ̃ ka kaba kaasɛ karɔ̃.

ref: When the Son of Man appears, things will be just as they were when Noah lived.
text: Ɔturi Awune Ɔbi iba to iase lɛ kumɛgɔ i Noa ɔwi ɔse awe.

ref: No one knows the day or hour. The angels in heaven don't know, and the Son himself doesn't know. Only the Father knows.
text: “Kuwɛ̃ iiɣe iyi ɣee ɔwi gɔ i nwagbe aaba. Ɣaa makpabo ma i kato, ɣee Ɔturi Awune Ɔbi kɔra iiɣe iɖo wũ Ɔse ɔnɔwɛ̃ nɣe.

ref: People were eating, drinking, and getting married right up to the day the flood came and Noah went into the big boat.
text: Si kado aaba ɔ̃apɛ ndu ayi minina kayiiso ɔɖuɖu ne, maturi ɔla maɔɖe ara maɔnɛ ndã, marɛrɛ̃ gu marɔ̃go to mayiri so gbɔgbɔɔgbɔ iyi nɛ i Noa ɔya ɔbo i ɔkolo ame.

ref: They didn't know anything was happening until the flood came and swept them all away. This is how it will be when the Son of Man appears.
text: Ɣɛɛ maitsue nnɛ nto iba gbɔgbɔɔgbɔ ndu ɔyi miba miasore ma ɔɖuɖu miruigu. Ngbɔ ame i Ɔturi Awune Ɔbi ɔbawi ana aase ne.

ref: Two men will be in the same field, but only one will be taken. The other will be left.
text: Ɔwi gɔmɔ ame ne, marɛrɛ̃ inyɔ to maaya i kakpɛkɔ̃, Ɣaa aasu ɔwɛ̃ ɔruigu ɔnyua ɔwɛ̃ ɔɣedza.

ref: Two women will be together grinding grain, but only one will be taken. The other will be left.
text: Marɔ̃go inyɔ to maaya i kukɔtɛ katɔ̃ mala maɔkɔtɛ ara, Ɣaa to ɔ̃asu ɔwɛ̃ ɔruigu ɔnyua ɔwɛ̃ ɔsɛ.

ref: So be on your guard! You don't know when your Lord will come.
text: “Ne ɔso midzuɛ̃ so alasɔ miiɣe iyi nɛ i Bosate aaba.

ref: Homeowners never know when a thief is coming, and they are always on guard to keep one from breaking in.
text: Si iyosate ɣe iyi nɛ i yukukpe aaba ɔ̃ayu wũ ne, tee ɔ̃ibarɛ sirɛ, ɔ̃asɛ ɔdzuɛ̃ sɔ ɔ̃ibatã yukukpe ɔbiɛ ɔ̃ iyo ɔbo ɔyu ara.

ref: Always be ready! You don't know when the Son of Man will come. Faithful and Unfaithful Servants (Luke
text: Ne ɔso mi wũ midzuɛ̃ so alasɔ Ɔturi Awune Ɔbi to ɔ̃aba ku ɔwi gɔ miito minyɔ wũ ɔri ame. Mise Lɛ Ɔɖabo Gɔ Nɖe Ɔnukuarese.

ref: But suppose one of the servants thinks the master won't return until late.
text: Ɣɛɛ si ɔɖabo lalaa ɔɖe ne, ɔto ɔ̃aɣere so sɔ, ‘Bokɔse to ɔ̃ana ɔpã fiɛ ɔ̃aba!’

ref: Who are faithful and wise servants? Who are the ones the master will put in charge of giving the other servants their food supplies at the proper time?
text: “Kiniɔ ne, nna nɖe ɔɖabo nukuarese gu nɔgbadze? Ɔ̃ nɖe ngɔ si makɔse ɔsu maɖabo tsɔra ɔpia i ɔ̃ kayirinɔ ne, ɔ̃atã ma aɖera i ɔwi sɛɛ iso.

ref: Servants are fortunate if their master comes and finds them doing their job.
text: So aaɣɔ ɔɖabo gɔmɔ si makɔse ɔkpese ɔba ɔ̃anya sɔ ɔto ɔbara ngbɔ.

ref: You may be sure a servant who is always faithful will be put in charge of everything the master owns.
text: Nto losia ita lotã mi sɔ makɔse to ɔ̃asu ɔ̃ karana ɔɖuɖu ɔpia wũ i nrɔɔ̃ ame sɔ ɔnyɔ iso.

ref: Suppose this evil servant starts beating the other servants and eats and drinks with people who are drunk.
text: Ne ɔso ɔto ɔ̃atsɛ ɔ̃ maɖabo laa ipɛ, itsɛ aɖera iɖe, inɛ ndã ku matarɛ̃dze.

ref: If that happens, the master will surely come on a day and at a time when the servant least expects him.
text: Ɔ̃ ɔkɔse to ɔ̃aba ku iyi nɛ ɔ̃ito ɔnyɔ wũ ɔri ku ɔwi gɔ ɔ̃iɣe.

ref: This servant will then be punished and thrown out with the ones who only pretended to serve their master. There they will cry and grit their teeth in pain.
text: Makɔse to ɔ̃akpadzɛ̃ wũ kutsue gbaã fiɛ ɔ̃ inyɛwe to iakotegu mma nsɛ mabie sɔ mabo maturi anɔ ɔkpɛ i ngbegɔ i kaku gu inyɛwe pia!

ref: The foolish ones took their lamps, but no extra oil.
text: Madzimifɔ ɔsu ma sikaniɛ, ɣɛɛ maisu nnɔĩ mabua i sikaniɛ iso.

ref: The ones who were wise took along extra oil for their lamps.
text: Manɔgbadze ɣɛɛ ne, mato nnɔĩ i abui ame mabua i mmɛ mpia i ɔkaniɛ ame iso.

ref: Then in the middle of the night someone shouted, “Here's the groom! Come to meet him!”
text: “Lɛ kasɛ̃ ndɛ̃ ne, kulu ɔsia sɔ, ‘Ɔrɔ̃go kɔdze ɔɔba ɔ̃abo ne miba miasarɛgu wũ!’

ref: When the women got up and started getting their lamps ready,
text: “Mabitɛ iweo ɔɖuɖu ɔta, ne mayara ma sikaniɛ ne.

ref: the foolish ones said to the others, “Let us have some of your oil! Our lamps are going out.”
text: Ne madzimifɔ to maɣere manɔgbadze sɔ, ‘Mitã bo mi nnɔĩ kɛkɛĩ alasɔ bo sikaniɛ ka sinyi.’

ref: Those who were wise answered, “There's not enough oil for all of us! Go and buy some for yourselves.”
text: “Ne manɔgbadze ɔɣere ma sɔ, ‘Ooɣo! Bo nnɔĩ iibaɣo bo gu mi inyɔ ne mikɛlɛ mma nto marɔdɛ̃ kɔrɛ miaɣa.’

ref: But the groom replied, “I don't even know you!”
text: “Ne ɔrɔ̃go kɔdze ɔtã ma mmuai sɔ, ‘Ooɣo! Loiɣe mi!’ ”

ref: So, my disciples, always be ready! You don't know the day or the time when all this will happen. A Story about Three Servants (Luke
text: Ne Yesu ɔwirigu ma igbã sɔ, “Ne ɔso midzuɛ̃ so alasɔ miiɣe iyi ɣee ɔwi gɔ ame loaba. Kumɛgɔ Boala Ara Wa I Ɣaa Ɔsu Ɔpia Bo I Nrɔɔ̃ Ame Kukaakɔ.

ref: Some time later the master of those servants returned. He called them in and asked what they had done with his money.
text: “Ayi gbodzoo kama ne, makɔse ɔba sɔ si ɔba ɔ̃akigu ma i siko ame ɔnyɔ nnɛ maɔna masia.

ref: You could have at least put my money in the bank, so I could have earned interest on it.”
text: Iyɔ be ɔso fiɛ aisu wũ sikã asɛ aasɛ i sikã kakɔlakɔ̃ sɔ si loba ne, loasɛ loafɔ sɛ̃ ku kusia?’

ref: The kingdom is also like what happened when a man went away and put his three servants in charge of all he owned.
text: “Ɔwi gɔmɔ ame ne, Ɣaa sigarakaɖekɔ̃ aase lɛ ngbe. Ɔwi ɔwɛ̃ ame ne, ɔrɛrɛ̃ ɔwɛ̃ to ɔkɛlɛ ɔri dzoroa, ne ɔso ɔkpere ɔ̃ maɖabo ɔsu ɔ̃ karana ɔpia ma i nrɔɔ̃ ame.

ref: As soon as the man had gone, the servant with the
text: Ɔɖabo gɔ lofɔ ikoto iru ɔkɛlɛ ɔsɛ ɔ̃afinikira nrɔɔ̃ i ɔ̃ sikã iso ɔna ikoto iru mama ɔsia i iso.

ref: “Wonderful!” his master replied. “You are a good and faithful servant. I left you in charge of only a little, but now I will put you in charge of much more. Come and share in my happiness!”
text: “Makɔse ɔɣere wũ sɔ, ‘Ɖafe, ɔɖabo sɛɛ gu nukuarese aɖe! Gɔ aɖe ɔnukuare i ara kosoi ame ɔso ne, loasu ara siare lopia i fɔ nrɔɔ̃ ame. Ba nagu me isoɣɔ!’
