ref: “Wonderful!” his master replied. “You are a good and faithful servant. I left you in charge of only a little, but now I will put you in charge of much more. Come and share in my happiness!”
text: “Makɔse ɔɣere wũ sɔ, ‘Ɖafe, ɔɖabo sɛɛ gu nukuarese aɖe. Kumɛgɔ aɖe ɔnukuare i ara kosoi ame ɔso ne, loasu ara siare lopia-ɔ i nrɔɔ̃ ame. Ba nagu me isoɣɔ!’

ref: I was frightened and went out and hid your money in the ground. Here is every single coin!”
text: Ne ɔso gɔ loto lonigã-ɔ ɔso ne, lokuri karɔ̃ losu fɔ sikã lobiara. Nya fɔ sikã i ngbe!’

ref: The master of the servant told him, “You are lazy and good-for-nothing! You know I harvest what I don't plant and gather crops where I haven't scattered seed.
text: “Ne makɔse ɔfɔ kanya sɔ, ‘Ɔɖabo lalaa gu dãdze aɖe. Aɣe sɔ nsɛ losu aɖera i ngbegɔ loikpɛ̃ fiɛ nsɛ losa ɔraɖui kanya i ngbegɔ loisa ɔraɖui?

ref: Then the master said, “Now your money will be taken away and given to the servant with
text: “Ne ɔ̃ ɔkɔse ɔɣɛ sɔ, ‘Mimɔɛ̃ sikã mifɔ i ɔ̃ kɔrɛ misu mitã ngɔ mba ikoto iweo.’

ref: Everyone who has something will be given more, and they will have more than enough. But everything will be taken from those who don't have anything.
text: Alasɔ ɔbiara gɔ mba ira ne, maatã wũ mabua ɔna isi gbodzoo, ɣɛɛ ngɔ nna kuira ne, kɛkɛĩ gɔ ame ɔba ne, maamɔɛ̃ mafɔ wũ iso.

ref: You are a worthless servant, and you will be thrown out into the dark where people will cry and grit their teeth in pain.” The Final Judgment
text: Ɔɖabo gɔ nna kusia kukuwɛ̃ i ngbe ɣɛɛ ne, mifuĩdza wũ mipia i idududu ame ngbegɔ i kaku gu arɔĩ iɖo ikpa pia. Ɔturi Awune Ɔbi Loaɣɛgu Kayiiso Ɔɖuɖu Atɔ̃me.

ref: When the Son of Man comes in his glory with all his angels, he will sit on his royal throne.
text: “Ɔwi gɔ i Ɔturi Awune Ɔbi aaba lɛ igara gu ɔ̃ makpabo ne, ɔto ɔ̃asɛ i ɔ̃ sigaraiyara iso.

ref: The people of all nations will be brought before him, and he will separate them, as shepherds separate their sheep from their goats.
text: Fiɛ kayiiso maturi ɔɖuɖu aaya i ɔ̃ katɔ̃. Mmɔ ɔ̃aɣɛ ma ame lɛ kumɛgɔ i ngɔ nto ɔɣedza masɛrɛ sɛ ɔɣɛ kutepuĩ gu masɛrɛrerɔ̃ ame ne.

ref: He will place the sheep on his right and the goats on his left.
text: Ɔto ɔ̃atã maturi bɔlɔlɔa ɔya i ɔ̃ kuɖearɔ̃ iso fiɛ malalaa ana aaya i ɔ̃ kubena iso.

ref: Then the king will say to those on his right, “My father has blessed you! Come and receive the kingdom that was prepared for you before the world was created.
text: “Ne igara aaɣere mma nɣɛ wũ i kuɖearɔ̃ iso sɔ, ‘Miba, mi ma i Tete ɔtã isobuɛ, miba miabo i sigarakaɖekɔ̃ ga maledza masɛ mi ta kayi karɔ̃kasɛkɔ̃ ame.

ref: When I was hungry, you gave me something to eat, and when I was thirsty, you gave me something to drink. When I was a stranger, you welcomed me,
text: Alasɔ kukã ɔkpɛ̃ me, mitã me aɖera, ɔme ɔkore me, mitã me ndu lonɛ, loɖe ɔfɔ lobo mi, mifɔ me.

ref: and when I was naked, you gave me clothes to wear. When I was sick, you took care of me, and when I was in jail, you visited me.”
text: Minya me losɛ isɔ̃, mitã me arapia, gɔ lonyɛ ne, minyɔ me iso fiɛ gɔ mamɔɛ̃ me marara i iyo ne, miba minyɔ me.’

ref: Then the ones who pleased the Lord will ask, “When did we give you something to eat or drink?
text: “Mmɔ i maturi bɔlɔlɔa aakarɛ wũ sɔ, ‘Bosate, ku mme ɔwi bonya-ɔ sɔ kukã to-ɔ botã-ɔ aɖera ɣee ɔme ɔkore-ɔ botã-ɔ ndu?

ref: When did we welcome you as a stranger or give you clothes to wear
text: Ku mme ɔwi bonya-ɔ aɖe ɔfɔ aba bo kɔrɛ fiɛ bofɔ-ɔ ɣee asɛ isɔ̃ botã-ɔ arapia?

ref: or visit you while you were sick or in jail?”
text: Ku mme ɔwi bonɔ sɔ ato anyɛ boba boaya-ɔ ɣee mamɔɛ̃-ɔ marara i iyo boba boaya-ɔ?’

ref: The king will answer, “Whenever you did it for any of my people, no matter how unimportant they seemed, you did it for me.”
text: “Mmɔ i igara aatã ma mmuai sɔ, ‘Ira biara nɛ miabara mitã manyii ma loire kuira i ngbe ne, mme mibara mitã.’

ref: Then the king will say to those on his left, “Get away from me! You are under God's curse. Go into the everlasting fire prepared for the devil and his angels!
text: “Ne ka ɔ̃aɣere mma i ɔ̃ kubena iso sɔ, ‘Mirui me i kɔrɛ mi ma i Ɣaa ɔgbarĩ. Mikɛlɛ itɔkpe nɛ mapia masɛ Ɔbosam gu ɔ̃ makpabo fiɛ iisɛ inyi ame!

ref: I was hungry, but you did not give me anything to eat, and I was thirsty, but you did not give me anything to drink.
text: Alasɔ kukã ɔkpɛ̃ me, miitã me aɖera, ɔme ɔkore me, miitã me ndu.

ref: I was a stranger, but you did not welcome me, and I was naked, but you did not give me any clothes to wear. I was sick and in jail, but you did not take care of me.”
text: Loba loabo mi lɛ ɔfɔ, ɣɛɛ miifɔ me, losɛ isɔ̃ miitã me arapia. Nto lonyɛ, miinyɔ me iso mamɔɛ̃ me marara i iyo miiba mianyɔ me.’

ref: Then the people will ask, “Lord, when did we fail to help you when you were hungry or thirsty or a stranger or naked or sick or in jail?”
text: “Mmɔ maakarɛ wũ sɔ, ‘Bosate, ku mme ɔwi bonya sɔ kukã ɣee ɔme ɔkore-ɔ ɣee aɖe ɔfɔ ɣee asɛ isɔ̃ ɣee ato anyɛ ɣee arɛ i iyo fiɛ boibuai-ɔ?’

ref: The king will say to them, “Whenever you failed to help any of my people, no matter how unimportant they seemed, you failed to do it for me.”
text: “Ne igara aatã ma mmuai sɔ, ‘Nto loɣere mi sɔ ira biara nɛ misɛ̃ sɔ miito mibara mitã manyii ma loire kuira i ngbe ne, mme miibara mitã!’

ref: Then Jesus said, “Those people will be punished forever. But the ones who pleased God will have eternal life.”
text: “Mmagbe ne, maakɛlɛ ngbegɔ inyɛwe iibaro inya fiɛ maturi sɛɛ ne, maakɛlɛ isoɣɔ nɛ loibaro inya ame.”

ref: “You know two days from now will be Passover. This is when the Son of Man will be handed over to his enemies and nailed to a cross.”
text: “Miɣe sɔ iɔkuti ayi anyɔ gɔ maaɖe Kukpi Ɔtakuwara Bo Iyi fiɛ maaɖi mme, Ɔturi Awune Ɔbi, matã mapɛ me mamatãra i kudziri iso.”

ref: Finally, Jesus returned to his disciples and said, “Are you still sleeping and resting? The time has come for the Son of Man to be handed over to sinners.
text: Ɔkpese ɔba marasuãdze kɔrɛ fiɛ ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Mikpese mirɛ mito miwarã? Minyɔ ɔwi ɔɔɣo, maɔɖi Ɔturi Awune Ɔbi mapia makpibaradze i nrɔɔ̃ ame.

ref: They planned how they could sneak around and have Jesus arrested and put to death.
text: Ne masɛ iti ame sɔ maamɔɛ̃ Yesu i iwɛrɛ ame maaɖoe.

ref: But they said, “We must not do it during Passover, because the people will riot.” At Bethany (Mark
text: Ɣɛɛ maɣɛ sɔ, “Boiba bara ne ku iyi ɔɖewi, si iiɖe ngbɔ kulu to kuaba.” Masɔla Yesu Nnɔĩ I Betania.

ref: We could have sold this perfume for a lot of money and given it to the poor.”
text: Tee maawo nnɔĩ mɛgbe ɔrɔdɛ̃ ku kuɣa siare masu sikã matã mawɛrɛba!”

ref: But when his disciples saw this, they became angry and complained, “Why such a waste?
text: Gɔ marasuãdze ɔnya nnɛgbe ne, ikpɛ̃ ma sikpã. Ne mato makarɛ sɔ, “Be nɖe karanina gagbe?

ref: Then they sang a hymn and went out to the Mount of Olives. Peter's Promise (Mark
text: Ne gɔ maka kuka male Ɣaa ne, mabɔrɛ makɛlɛ Nnɔĩ Adziri Kube iso. Yesu Ɔɣɛ Ɔsɛ Sɔ Petro To Ɔ̃asɛ̃ Wũ.

ref: and from then on he started looking for a good chance to betray Jesus. Jesus Eats the Passover Meal with His Disciples (Mark
text: Ita i ɔwi gɔmɔ ame ne, Yuda ɔtsɛ ɔri sɛɛ gɔ iso ɔ̃aki fiɛ ɔ̃aɖi Yesu ɔtã ma ibie. Yesu Gu Ɔ̃ Marasuãdze Ɔɖe Kukpi Ɔtakuwara Bo Iyi Aɖera.

ref: When Jesus was eating with his twelve disciples that evening, he said, “One of you will surely hand me over to my enemies.”
text: Ɔwi gɔ mato maɖe ara ne, Yesu ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Nto loɣere mi sɔ mi ndɛ̃ ɔwɛ̃ to ɔ̃aɖi me ɔtã.”

ref: On the first day of the Festival of Thin Bread, Jesus' disciples came to him and asked, “Where do you want us to prepare the Passover meal?”
text: I iyi ɖeakatɔ̃ nɛ iso masɛ maɖe Boɖoboɖo Gɔ Loita Iyi ne, marasuãdze ɔba maakarɛ Yesu sɔ, “Lɛ ato abie sɔ bowa Kukpi Ɔtakuwara Bo iyi aɖera botã-ɔ?”

ref: During the meal Jesus took some bread in his hands. He blessed the bread and broke it. Then he gave it to his disciples and said, “Take this and eat it. This is my body.”
text: Ɔwi gɔ masi mato maɖe ara ne, Yesu ɔsu boɖoboɖo, ne gɔ ɔpɛ Ɣaa siba i ɔ̃ iti ne, ɔsu ɔtã ma ɔɣɛ sɔ, “Mifɔ miɖe, wũ sosina i ngbe ne.”

ref: This is my blood, and with it God makes his agreement with you. It will be poured out, so that many people will have their sins forgiven.
text: Wũ ima nɛ nto isia ita i Ɣaa ndamu ɣɛtɛ iso ne, ima nɛ mafere i karɔ̃ itã maturi gbodzoo akpi isu itsɛ.

ref: From now on I am not going to drink any wine, until I drink new wine with you in my Father's kingdom.”
text: Nto loɣere mi gbaã sɔ loisibanɛ ndã gbɔgbɔɔgbɔ iyi nɛ loanɛgu mi miɣɛtɛ i wũ Ɔse sigarakaɖekɔ̃.”

ref: Jesus said to his disciples, “During this very night, all of you will reject me, as the Scriptures say, ‘I will strike down the shepherd, and the sheep will be scattered.’
text: Ne Yesu ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Kasɛ̃ gagbe ne, mi ɔɖuɖu to miatere minyua me, ikuti wũ ɔnɔwɛ̃, alasɔ Ɔko Sekelea ɔɣɛ sɔ, ‘Ɣaa to ɔ̃aɖoe masɛrɛkɔdze fiɛ ɔ̃ masɛrɛ aasame.’

ref: Jesus replied, “I promise you before a rooster crows tonight, you will say three times that you don't know me.”
text: Ne Yesu ɔɣere Petro sɔ, “Si makɔkɔ aaɖi kanya nɔme ne, ato aasɛ̃ me ikpɛ itɛ sɔ aiɣe me.”

ref: Jesus went with his disciples to a place called Gethsemane. When they got there, he told them, “Sit here while I go over there and pray.”
text: Yesu gu ɔ̃ marasuãdze ɔrui makɛlɛ ngbegɔ marɔ sɔ Getsemane, ne ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Misɛ i ngbe si lokɛlɛ mmɔ loakparama kayi.”

ref: and he said to them, “I am so sad that I feel as if I am dying. Stay here and keep awake with me.”
text: Ɔɣere ma sɔ, “So to sima me ta wũ ɔtu ame gɔ loɔro ɔfɛ̃, misɛ i ngbe si midzuɛ̃gu me.”

ref: Peter spoke up, “Even if all the others reject you, I never will!”
text: Petro ɔtara kanya sɔ, “Si matsɔra ɔɖuɖu aatere maɣɛ-ɔ kɔra ne, mme loibatere loɣɛ-ɔ lonya ɖa!”

ref: But Peter said, “Even if I have to die with you, I will never say I don't know you.” All the others said the same thing. Jesus Prays (Mark
text: Petro ɔtã mmuai sɔ, “Si inya sɔ loakpigu-ɔ kɔra ne, loibasɛ̃-ɔ lonya ɖa.” Marasuãdze tsɔra ana ɔɣɛ ngbɔ ame. Yesu Ɔkparama Kayi I Getsemane.

ref: Again Jesus went to pray and said, “My Father, if there is no other way, and I must suffer, I will still do what you want.”
text: Yesu ɔledza ikɛlɛ isɛ iakparama kayi iɣɛ sɔ, “Tete, si aibasɛnɛkɛ̃ra ikɔi nɛgbe aɖi me iso iɖo sɔ loanɛ ne, bara fɔ kuɖɔɛ, ne iiɖe wũ kure.”

ref: Jesus walked on a little way. Then he knelt with his face to the ground and prayed, “My Father, if it is possible, don't make me suffer by drinking from this cup. But do what you want, and not what I want.”
text: Ne gɔ ɔkɛlɛ katɔ̃ kɛkɛĩ ne, ɔkɔlɛ ɔfu i karɔ̃ ɔkparama kayi ɔɣɛ sɔ, “Tete, si ialɛ ne, tã inyɛwe ɔwi gɔgbe si ɔta ɔwara me! Ɣɛɛ ne, iiɖe wũ kuɖɔɛ, nnɛ i fɔ aɖɔɛ si abara.”

ref: Get up! Let's go. The one who will betray me is already here.” Jesus Is Arrested (Mark
text: Mita si borui! Minyɔ, miɔnya ngɔ nto ɔɖi me ɔtã.” Ma Yudase Ɔmɔɛ̃ Yesu.

ref: Jesus took along Peter and the two brothers, James and John. He was very sad and troubled,
text: Ɔkɔ Petro gu Zebedeo mabi inyɔ, ne ɔtsɛ so imararã iti ikpakpa ne.

ref: He came back and found his disciples sleeping. So he said to Peter, “Can't any of you stay awake with me for just one hour?
text: Gɔ ɔkpese ɔba marasuãdze itɛ mamɔ kɔrɛ ne, ɔnya sɔ maɔrɛ sirɛ, ne ɔɣere Petro sɔ, “Ne ɔso miibawo me ɔsɛ ɔdzuɛ̃gu bidɛ ɔɖɔwũ ɔwɛ̃?

ref: Stay awake and pray that you won't be tested. You want to do what is right, but you are weak.”
text: Misɛ mila miɔdzuɛ̃ si mikparama kayi si miibabo i ilainyɔ ame. Siwarã ɣɛ teteree, ɣɛɛ siturina ɔɔfɛ̃.”

ref: He left them and prayed the same prayer once more.
text: Yesu ɔrui i ma kɔrɛ ɔsɛ ɔ̃aledza kayi ikparama iɣɛ atɔ̃me wamɔ ame.

ref: Jesus replied, “My friend, do what you came for.” The men grabbed Jesus and arrested him.
text: Ne Yesu ɔɣere wũ sɔ, “Wũ ɔlaa nnɛ ɔso aba ne, tã ipɛ-ɔ siwa si abara ne!” Ne mma i Yuda sɛgu ɔbore mabo Yesu fiɛ mamɔɛ̃ wũ teteree ne.

ref: Jesus was still speaking, when Judas the betrayer came up. He was one of the twelve disciples, and a large mob armed with swords and clubs was with him. They had been sent by the chief priests and the nation's leaders.
text: Yesu ɣɛ ɔto ɔɣɛ ara wagbe fiɛ Yuda gɔ nɖe marasuãdze iweo-inyɔ ɔwɛ̃ ɔba ɔ̃abo. Ɔsɛgu maturi gbodzoo ma i masɔrɛdze makpakpa gu kaɖe makpakpa ɔpia fiɛ mato atekpe gu kudziriku.

ref: Judas had told them ahead of time, “Arrest the man I greet with a kiss.”
text: Ngɔ loɖi wũ ɔtã ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Ngɔ minya sɔ loya fiɛ lofiɔ̃ irɛkpo ne, ɔ̃ ɔɖe ne, mimɔɛ̃ wũ!”

ref: Judas walked right up to Jesus and said, “Hello, teacher.” Then Judas kissed him.
text: Yuda ɔsɛ tiĩ ɔkɛlɛ Yesu kɔrɛ, ne ɔya wũ ɔɣɛ sɔ, “Isobuɛ itã-ɔ, ratedze.” Ne ɔfiɔ̃ wũ irɛkpo ne.

ref: One of Jesus' followers pulled out a sword. He struck the servant of the high priest and cut off his ear.
text: Mma nsiai Yesu mmɔ ɔwɛ̃ ɔɖi ɔ̃ itekpe ɔtu masɔrɛdze ɔkpakpa pelepele ɔɖabo kutsue.

ref: But Jesus told him, “Put your sword away. Anyone who lives by fighting will die by fighting.
text: Ne Yesu ɔɣere wũ sɔ, “Pia fɔ itekpe i ɔyuɛ ame ala mma ɔɖuɖu nsɛ masu atekpe i maturi iso ne, atekpe kanya maakpi.

ref: The high priest then tore his robe and said, “This man claims to be God! We don't need any more witnesses! You have heard what he said.
text: Gɔ ɔɣɛ ngbɔ ne, sɔrɛdzekpakpa pelepele ɔkɛ ɔ̃ awu ne ɔɣɛ sɔ, “Mmusuo! Maɖansiɛ kumawɛ̃ iisinya bo. Mi ɔɖuɖu ɔnɔ mmusuo itɔ̃me nɛ ɔɣɛ!

ref: But Jesus did not answer. So the high priest said, “With the living God looking on, you must tell the truth. Are you the Messiah, the Son of God?”
text: Ɣɛɛ Yesu ɔma kanya kpa. Ne sɔrɛdzekpakpa pelepele ɔledza iɣɛ sɔ, “I Ɣaa iyere ame ne, nto lokã ndamu lotã-ɔ sɔ, ɣere bo si fɔ nɖe Kristo gɔ nɖe Ɣaa Ɔbi.”

ref: When Peter had gone out to the gate, another servant girl saw him and said to some people there, “This man was with Jesus from Nazareth.”
text: Ne ɔbɔrɛ ɔkɛlɛ ɔsɛ ɔ̃aya i kayogodɔ̃ ne. Ɔɖaborɔ̃go mama ɔnya wũ gɔ ɔɣɛ, ne ɔɣere marɛrɛ̃ ma nɣɛ i mmɔ sɔ, “Ɔ̃ wũ ɔsiai Yesu Nasaretse.”

ref: After Jesus had been arrested, he was led off to the house of Caiaphas the high priest. The nation's leaders and the teachers of the Law of Moses were meeting there.
text: Mma lomɔɛ̃ Yesu ɔkɔ wũ makɛlɛgu Kayafa iyo ngbegɔ i mmara matedze gu kaɖe makpakpa ɔsakanya.

ref: But Peter followed along at a distance and came to the courtyard of the high priest's palace. He went in and sat down with the guards to see what was going to happen.
text: Petro ɔsiai ma kamakama ɔsɛ ɔ̃abo sɔrɛdzekpakpa pelepele kakpataku ame. Ɔsɛ ɔ̃asɛ i madzuɛ̃se kɔrɛ sɔ si ɔnyɔ kumɛgɔ i ira biara aakɛlɛ i katɔ̃.

ref: The chief priests and the whole council wanted to put Jesus to death. So they tried to find some people who would tell lies about him in court.
text: Masɔrɛdze makpakpa gu matɔ̃meɣɛdze ɔɖuɖu ɔbie itɔ̃me nɛ maanyi masia Yesu iso fiɛ maaɖoe wũ.

ref: But they could not find any, even though many did come and tell lies. At last, two men came forward
text: Ɣɛɛ maina kuiwɛ̃ atoa sɔ maturi gbodzoo ɔba maala mila masia wũ iso. Kama ne, marɛrɛ̃ inyɔ mawɛ̃ ɔba,

ref: and said, “This man claimed he could tear down God's temple and build it again in three days.”
text: ne maɣɛ sɔ, “Ɔrɛrɛ̃ gɔgbe ɔɣɛ sɔ, ‘Loabiɛ Ɣaa isɔrɛyo loledza ne itsue i ayi atɛ ame.’ ”

ref: “That is what you say!” Jesus answered. “But I tell all of you, ‘Soon you will see the Son of Man sitting at the right side of God All-Powerful and coming on the clouds of heaven.’ ”
text: Ne Yesu ɔtã mmuai sɔ, “Aĩ, ngbɔ iɖe ne. Ɣɛɛ nto loɣere mi sɔ ita i kiniɔ ne, mito mianya Ɔturi Awune Ɔbi gɔ ɔsi i Ɔlesate kuɖearɔ̃ iso fiɛ ɔto ɔba i adɔdɔ ame!”

ref: Then they spit in his face and hit him with their fists. Others slapped him
text: Matua minyatu mafere wũ i katɔ̃, mapɛ wũ, fiɛ mma lopɛ wũ sirɔ̃ta

ref: and said, “You think you are the Messiah! So tell us who hit you!” Peter Says He Doesn't Know Jesus (Mark
text: to maɣere wũ sɔ, “Gɔ fɔ nɖe Kristo ne, ɣere bo ngɔ lopɛ-ɔ!” Petro Ɔsɛ̃ Sɔ Ɔ̃iɣe Yesu.

ref: Again Peter denied it, and this time he swore, “I don't even know that man!”
text: Mmɔ ana Petro ɔsɛ̃ ɔɣɛ sɔ, “Lokã sɔ loiɣe ɔrɛrɛ̃ gɔmɔ!”

ref: While Peter was sitting out in the courtyard, a servant girl came up to him and said, “You were with Jesus from Galilee.”
text: Gɔ Petro si i kakpataku ame mmɔ ne, sɔrɛdzekpakpa pelepele ɔɖaborɔ̃go ɔwɛ̃ ɔba ɔ̃aɣere wũ sɔ, “Fɔ wũ asiai Yesu gɔ lobɔrɛ i Galilea karɔ̃ iso.”

ref: But in front of everyone Peter said, “That isn't so! I don't know what you are talking about!”
text: Ɣɛɛ Petro ɔsɛ̃ i ma ɔɖuɖu katɔ̃ sɔ, “Loiɣe nnɛ ato aɣɛ i ngbe.”

ref: A little while later some people standing there walked over to Peter and said, “We know you are one of them. We can tell it because you talk like someone from Galilee.”
text: Iidzoro fiɛ marɛrɛ̃ ma nɣɛ i mmɔ ɔɣere Petro sɔ, “Fɔ wũ ma ndɛ̃ ɔwɛ̃ aɖe ala fɔ ikaɖe ɔɖi-ɔ itã!”

ref: Peter began to curse and swear, “I don't know that man!” Right then a rooster crowed,
text: Ne Petro ɔɣɛ sɔ, “Lokã sɔ ɔnukuare nto loɣɛ! Ɣaa si ɔkpadzɛ̃ me kutsue si iiɖe gbaã iɖe sɔ loiɣe ɔrɛrɛ̃ gɔmɔ!” Ɔluwɛ̃ ne, kɔkɔ ɔɖi kanya.

ref: and Peter remembered that Jesus had said, “Before a rooster crows, you will say three times you don't know me.” Then Peter went out and cried bitterly.
text: Ne Petro ɔnyigi nnɛ i Yesu ɔɣere wũ sɔ, “Si kɔkɔ aaɖi kanya ne, ato aasɛ̃ me ikpɛ itɛ sɔ aiɣe me.” Ne ɔso Petro ɔbɔrɛ ɔkɛlɛ inyamɔ ɔsɛ ɔ̃abiɛ kaku teteree.

ref: Pilate knew the leaders had brought Jesus to him because they were jealous.
text: Ɔbara ngbɔ alasɔ ɔnya sɔ ma Yudase makpakpa ne, sirerɛ ɔso kere maɖi wũ matã.

ref: On the way they met a man named Simon who was from Cyrene, and they forced him to carry Jesus' cross.
text: Gɔ mato mabɔrɛ i ɔmagɛ̃ ame ne, manya ɔrɛrɛ̃ ɔwɛ̃ gɔ lobɔrɛ i Kirene fiɛ marɔ wũ sɔ Simon. Makpakpɛ̃dze ɔya wũ iso sɔ ɔfɔgu Yesu ɔnyigbã gɔ ɔsore.

ref: and said, “I have sinned by betraying a man who has never done anything wrong.” “So what? That's your problem,” they replied.
text: Ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Lobara ikpi, alasɔ loɖi ɔturi gɔ loibara ikpi lotã sɔ maɖoe.” Ne matã wũ mmuai sɔ, “Fɔ itɔ̃me iɖe ne iisu bo kuira i mmɔ.”

ref: Then they had a meeting and decided to buy a field that belonged to someone who made clay pots. They wanted to use it as a graveyard for foreigners.
text: Gɔ maɣɛ manɔgu so karɔ̃ ne, masu sɛ̃ masɛ maaɣa karɔ̃ ga masɛ marɔ sɔ, “Rapɔdze Karɔ̃” sɔ maala ma ɔbiara mafɔ i mmɔ.

ref: This is why people still call that place “Field of Blood.”
text: Ne ɔso fiɛ ipɛ i nɔme ne, masɛ marɔ karɔ̃ gamɔ sɔ Ima Karɔ̃ ne.

ref: They paid it for a potter's field, as the Lord had commanded me.” Pilate Questions Jesus (Mark
text: masɛ maaɣa rapɔdze karɔ̃ lɛ kumɛgɔ i Bosate ɔɣere me.” Pilato Ɔkarɛ Yesu Atɔ̃me.

text: Ibara abã ɔkpakpa ɣaa gbaã kumɛgɔ i Yesu ɔsɛ̃ sɔ ɔ̃ito ɔtã ku itɔ̃me mmuai. Pilato Ɔtã Yesu Kukpi Ipɔ.

text: Pilato ɔnyua Baraba ɔtã ma, ne gɔ ɔtã mapɛ Yesu ala iporoibi ne, ɔnyua wũ ɔtã ma sɔ masɛ maapɛ wũ mamatãra i kudziri iso. Makpakpɛ̃dze Ɔbara Yesu Aɣiãra.

ref: People who passed by said terrible things about Jesus. They shook their heads and
text: Mma nto mafe to matidza ma ati fiɛ mato matia Yesu.

ref: Pilate saw that there was nothing he could do and that the people were starting to riot. So he took some water and washed his hands in front of them and said, “I won't have anything to do with killing this man. You are the ones doing it!”

ref: They made a crown out of thorn branches and placed it on his head, and they put a stick in his right hand. The soldiers knelt down and pretended to worship him. They made fun of him and shouted, “Hey, you king of the Jews!”
text: Masu miyu mabara ala igarakoto matɔ̃medza wũ fiɛ masu ɔdziri matã wũ. Mapɛ agɛgɛ̃ i ɔ̃ katɔ̃, fiɛ ka mato mabara wũ aɣiãra sɔ, “Ii iɖuu! Ma Yudase Igara si ɔsɛ ngbã dzoroo.”

text: Ɔfɔ Ɣaa ɔɖe fiɛ ɔto ɔɣɛ sɔ ɔ̃ nɖe Ɣaa Ɔbi mɔmɔ, mitã bonyɔ si Ɣaa aaso ɔba ɔ̃aɖi wũ!”

ref: When the soldiers had finished making fun of Jesus, they took off the robe. They put his own clothes back on him and led him off to be nailed to a cross.

ref: There they gave Jesus some wine mixed with a drug to ease the pain. But when Jesus tasted what it was, he refused to drink it.
text: Matã Yesu ndã mɛ masakanya ku kua gɔ mba ɔɖo sɔ ɔnɛ, ɣɛɛ gɔ ɔti ɔnyɔ ne, ɔsɛ̃ sɔ ɔ̃ito ɔnɛ.

ref: The soldiers nailed Jesus to a cross and gambled to see who would get his clothes.

text: I ɔ̃ sitikpe ne, matsɛrɛ masia i kakoloi kawɛ̃ iso nnɛ ɔso maɖoe wũ. Matsɛrɛ sɔ, “Yesu, ma Yudase Igara ngbe ne.”

ref: shouted, “So you're the one who claimed you could tear down the temple and build it again in three days! If you are God's Son, save yourself and come down from the cross!”

ref: Others said, “Wait! Let's see if Elijah will come and save him.”

ref: At noon the sky turned dark and stayed that way until three o'clock.

ref: Then about that time Jesus shouted, “Eli, Eli, lema sabachthani?” which means, “My God, my God, why have you deserted me?”
text: Lɛ kakɔme iɖɔwũ itɛ ame ne, Yesu ɔla kulu teteree sɔ, “Eli, Eli, lama sabaktani?” Gɔ karɔ̃ nɖe sɔ, “Wũ Ɣaa, wũ Ɣaa, be ɔso fiɛ anyua me asɛ ngbɔ?”

ref: One of them at once ran and grabbed a sponge. He soaked it in wine, then put it on a stick and held it up to Jesus.
text: Ma ndɛ̃ ɔwɛ̃ ɔtere ɔsu ɖɔti pukupukua ɔpia i ndã bea ame ɔsu ɔɖu i ɔdziri iso ɔtara ɔtã wũ sɔ ɔnɛ.

text: Gɔ kutsuɛ ɔɣo ne, ranase ɔwɛ̃ gɔ marɔ Yosef fiɛ ɔbɔrɛ i Arimatea ɔba. Ɔ̃ wũ ne, Yesu rasuãdze ɔɖe.

ref: They said to the soldiers, “Tell everyone that Jesus' disciples came during the night and stole his body while you were asleep.

text: Makpakpɛ̃dze ɔfɔ sikã fiɛ mabara lɛ kumɛgɔ maɣere ma sɔ mabara, ne ɔso itɔ̃me nɛgbe ɔki ikã i ma Yudase ndɛ̃ iba iapɛ i nɔme. Yesu Ɔɖi So Ɔte Ɔ̃ Marasuãdze.

ref: News about Jesus quickly spread all over Galilee. Jesus Heals Many People (Matthew
text: Ne ɔso Yesu iyere ɔre i Galilea karɔ̃ ɔɖuɖu iso. Yesu Ɔsa Maturi Sinyɛ.

text: Ɔ̃ itɔ̃me nɛ ɔla iɣɛ nɖe sɔ, “Ɔturi gɔ loaba i wũ kama ne, ɔɖo me fafaafã, ngɔ ndokota iikote sɔ loabɔdzo loɖi.

ref: Jesus and his disciples went to the town of Capernaum. Then on the next Sabbath he went into the synagogue and started teaching.

ref: He said, “The time has come! God's kingdom will soon be here. Turn back to God and believe the good news!” Jesus Chooses Four Fishermen (Matthew
text: Mmɔ ɔto ɔɣɛ sɔ, “Ɔwi sɛɛ gɔmɔ ɔɔɣo. Ɣaa sigara ɔɖewi ɔɔbore ɔbo. Mifiniki si mifɔ Itɔ̃me Bielea nɛgbe miɖe.”

ref: At once Jesus asked them to come with him. They left their father in the boat with the hired workers and went with him. A Man with an Evil Spirit (Luke
text: Gɔ Yesu ɔnya ma ne, ɔkpere ma sɔ masiai wũ. Ma wũ mata ɔluwɛ̃, marui i ma ɔse Zebedeo gu ɔ̃ marabaradze kɔrɛ i ɔkolo ame masiai Yesu. Yesu Ɔsa Siwarã Lalaa Ɔɖi I Ɔrɛrɛ̃ Ɔwɛ̃ Ame.

ref: Jesus went to her. He took hold of her hand and helped her up. The fever left her, and she served them a meal.
text: Ne ɔso Yesu ɔbore ɔbo wũ, ɔmɔɛ̃ wũ kɔrɔ̃ ɔtara wũ ɔɣedza. Ɔluwɛ̃ ɔsarɛ ɔta ɔwa aɖera ɔtã ma.

text: Gɔ manya wũ ne, maɣere wũ sɔ, “Maturi fu i mmɔ mato mabie-ɔ!”

ref: Suddenly a man with an evil spirit in him entered the synagogue and yelled,
text: Ɔluwɛ̃ ne, ɔrɛrɛ̃ ɔwɛ̃ gɔ i siwarã lalaa si iso fiɛ ɔwe i kasarɛkɔ̃ mmɔ

ref: “Jesus from Nazareth, what do you want with us? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are! You are God's Holy One.”
text: ɔsiagu kulu teteree sɔ, “Yesu Nasaretse, be mpia i bo gu-ɔ ndɛ̃? Ɔba ɔɔba sɔ si aba aawɔra bo? Nɣe ɔturi gɔ igbã aɖe. Fɔ nɖe Ɣaa Ɔsesea Sekelea ne.”

ref: Jesus told the evil spirit, “Be quiet and come out of the man!”

ref: The spirit shook him. Then it gave a loud shout and left.
text: Siwarã ɔtidza ɔrɛrɛ̃ ku ɔle, sitã ɔla kulu fiɛ sibɔrɛ i ɔ̃ ame sife.

ref: Everyone was completely surprised and kept saying to each other, “What is this? It must be some new kind of powerful teaching! Even the evil spirits obey him.”
text: Ibara mma lowe i mmɔ ɣaa, ne mato makarɛ so sɔ, “Be igbã iɖe ngbe ne? Mme ara ite igbã ka iɖe ngbe? Ngɔgbe ba ɔle ɔto ɔɖegu siwarã lalaa ika fiɛ sito sinɔ sibara i iso!”

ref: Jesus replied, “We must go to the nearby towns, so that I can tell the good news to those people. This is why I have come.”
text: Ɣɛɛ ne, Yesu ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Mitã bokɛlɛ simagɛ̃ dzɛ loki silɔ ame. Ma wũ inya sɔ loaɣere ma Itɔ̃me Bielea, ala ne ɔso loba kayi ne.”

ref: Jesus felt sorry for the man. So he put his hand on him and said, “I want to! Now you are well.”
text: Ɔ̃ itɔ̃me ɔɖo Yesu kayiri, ne ɔso ɔɖi kɔrɔ̃ ɔpɛgu wũ. Ne ɔɣere wũ sɔ, “Loɖɔɛ, se sekelee!”

ref: As soon as Jesus left the synagogue with James and John, they went home with Simon and Andrew.
text: Gɔ Yesu ɔbɔrɛ i kasarɛkɔ̃ mmɔ ne, ɔ̃ gu Yakobo gu Yohanes ɔkɛlɛ Simon gu Andrea iyo.

ref: When they got there, Jesus was told that Simon's mother-in-law was sick in bed with fever.
text: Gɔ mabo ne, mawɛ̃ ɔɣere Yesu sɔ Simon masɔrɔ̃go rɛ ɔto ɔnyɛ ɔbe ɔtɔ.

ref: That evening after sunset, all who were sick or had demons in them were brought to Jesus.
text: Gɔ kuɣɛ̃ ɔbo iyo ne, maturi ɔsore manyɛse gu mma i siwarã lalaa si iso gbodzoo mabɔ Yesu kɔrɛ.

ref: In fact, the whole town gathered around the door of the house.
text: Ne ɔso ɔmagɛ̃ ame maturi ɔɖuɖu ɔbɔrɛ maba maɣɛ i kayogodɔ̃ mato manyɔ.

ref: Jesus healed all kinds of terrible diseases and forced out a lot of demons. But the demons knew who he was, and he did not let them speak.
text: Yesu ɔsa manyɛse ma nto manyɛ sinyɛ agbãagbã fiɛ ɔsa siwarã lalaa ɔɖi i mma ame sipia. Ɔ̃itã siɖe ika alasɔ siɣe sɔ ɔ̃ nɖe Kristo. Yesu Ɔɣɛ Ɣaa Itɔ̃me I Galilea Karɔ̃ Iso.

ref: Very early the next morning before daylight, Jesus got up and went to a place where he could be alone and pray.
text: Ɔɖesɛrã si kaɖe aaɖaɛ ne, Yesu ɔkɛlɛ ngbegɔ i kuwɛ̃ na ɔsɛ ɔ̃akparama kayi.

ref: Simon and the others started looking for him.
text: Ɔwi kama ne, Simon gu ɔ̃ malaa ɔbie Yesu.

ref: Then Jesus went to their synagogues everywhere in Galilee, where he preached and forced out demons. Jesus Heals a Man (Matthew
text: Ne ɔso ɔki i Galilea karɔ̃ iso. Ɔkiki ngbegɔ masɛ masarɛ ɔɣɛ Itɔ̃me Bielea fiɛ ɔsa siwarã lalaa ɔɖi i maturi ame. Yesu Ɔsa Ɔrɛrɛ̃ Ɔwɛ̃ Gɔ Nto Ɔnyɛ Ɔnyɛ Rɛtɛa.

ref: At once the man's leprosy disappeared, and he was well.
text: Ɔluwɛ̃ ɔsarɛ, ɔ̃ iso ɔse sekelee.

ref: After Jesus strictly warned the man, he sent him on his way.
text: Mmɔ ame ne, Yesu ɔɣere wũ sɔ ɔrui ɔkɛlɛ. Ɔsi wũ mmara sɔ,

ref: He said, “Don't tell anyone about this. Just go and show the priest that you are well. Then take a gift to the temple as Moses commanded, and everyone will know that you have been healed.”
text: “Daaɣere kuwɛ̃ nnɛ loba. Ɣɛɛ ne, kɛlɛ aasu so te Ɣaa sɔrɛdze si atã ara wa i Mose mmara ɔkarɛ si ite maturi sɔ ka ase sekelee.”

ref: The man talked about it so much and told so many people, that Jesus could no longer go openly into a town. He had to stay away from the towns, but people still came to him from everywhere.
text: Ɣɛɛ gɔ ɔrui ne, ɔtsɛ ɔtsɛ itɔ̃me ikararã kumɛgɔ i Yesu ɔsa wũ ɔnyɛ. Ne ɔso Yesu iisiwo ɔmagɛ̃ ame ɔbo kpawee. Ne ɔso ɔwe i fafuĩ ame fiɛ maturi ɔbɔrɛ i ngɔi gbodzoo maba ɔ̃ kɔrɛ.

ref: It was during the time of Abiathar the high priest. David went into the house of God and ate the sacred loaves of bread that only priests are allowed to eat. He also gave some to his followers.”
text: Abiata ɖe masɔrɛdze ɔkpakpa. Ɔbo i Ɣaa iyo ɔsɛ ɔ̃asu aɖera wa masu masɔrɛ Ɣaa ɔɖe, ɔkuti ɔtã ɔ̃ masiaidze ɔɖe gɔ mmara iitã ɔri iɖo Ɣaa masɔrɛdze ɔnɔwɛ̃.”

ref: Then so many of them came to the house that there wasn't even standing room left in front of the door. Jesus was still teaching
text: Maturi gbodzoo ɔba mmɔ gɔ kukpa kanɛkɔ̃ iisiwe i iyo nɛ kame mapia gu kayogodɔ̃. Ne ɔsɛ i mmɔ ɔɣɛ Itɔ̃me Bielea ne.

ref: when four people came up, carrying a man on a mat because he could not walk.
text: Marɛrɛ̃ ina mawɛ̃ ɔsore nyɛse gɔ lofɛfɛ̃ mato mabɔ wũ.

ref: But because of the crowd, they could not get him to Jesus. So they made a hole in the roof above him and let the man down in front of everyone.
text: Maina ɔri mabo i iyo alasɔ maturi ɔɣede. Ne ɔso ne, masɛ maabusi isã makigu nyɛse masora wũ i ɔkala ame masɛ i Yesu katɔ̃.

ref: “Why would he say such a thing? He must think he is God! Only God can forgive sins.”
text: “Be ɔso ɔto ɔɖe aka ngbɔ? Imusuora iɖe ɔɣɛ i Ɣaa iso ngbe. Ɣaa kotoo loawo akpi ɔsu ɔtsɛ.”

ref: I will show you that the Son of Man has the right to forgive sins here on earth.” So Jesus said to the man,
text: “Loaɖi lote mi sɔ mme, Ɔturi Awune Ɔbi, ba ɔle i kayi gagbe iso sɔ loasu akpi lotsɛ, ne ɔso minyɔ!” Yesu ɔfiniki ɔnyɔ nyɛse, ne ɔɣere wũ sɔ,

ref: “Get up! Pick up your mat and go on home.”
text: “Ta si asu fɔ ɔkala si akɛlɛ iyo!”

ref: Once again, Jesus went to the shore of Lake Galilee. A large crowd gathered around him, and he taught them.
text: Yesu ɔkpese ɔkɛlɛ Galilea ibu kɔrɛ. Maturi gbodzoo ɔba ɔ̃ kɔrɛ fiɛ ɔtsɛ ma Itɔ̃me Bielea ite.

ref: Jesus finished by saying, “People were not made for the good of the Sabbath. The Sabbath was made for the good of people.
text: Yesu ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Ɣaa ɔbara Iwarãyi ɔtã ɔturi isɛɛ, ɔ̃ibara ɔturi ɔtã Iwarãyi.

ref: Later, Jesus and his disciples were having dinner at Levi's house. Many tax collectors and other sinners had become followers of Jesus, and they were also guests at the dinner.
text: Kama ne, Yesu gu ɔ̃ marasuãdze gu iturikuri nɛ nsiai wũ ɔba Levi iyo ame sɔ maaɖegu wũ aɖera. Ma lampofɔdze gu makpibaradze pia i mma nsiai wũ ndɛ̃.

ref: Some of the teachers of the Law of Moses were Pharisees, and they saw Jesus eating with sinners and tax collectors. So they asked his disciples, “Why does he eat with tax collectors and sinners?”
text: Ne ɔso ɔwi gɔ mmara matedze ma nɖe ma Farisise ɔnya sɔ Yesu si ɔto ɔɖegu makpibaradze gu ma lampofɔdze aɖera ne, makarɛ ɔ̃ marasuãdze sɔ, “Nda ɔso fiɛ mi ɔkɔse gu mmagbe igbã nsi maɖe ara?”

ref: The followers of John the Baptist and the Pharisees often went without eating. Some people came and asked Jesus, “Why do the followers of John and those of the Pharisees often go without eating, while your disciples never do?”
text: Ɔwi gɔmɔ gɔ Yohanes gu ɔ̃ marasuãdze gu ma Farisise sɛ manyi kanya ɔso ne, mawɛ̃ ɔba Yesu kɔrɛ sɔ, “Be ɔso fiɛ fɔ marasuãdze iisɛ manyi kanya lɛ kumɛgɔ i Yohanes gu ma Farisise mare sɛ mabara?”

ref: Jesus answered: The friends of a bridegroom don't go without eating while he is still with them.
text: Yesu ɔtã mmuai i igbã ame sɔ, “Ɔrɔ̃go yiridze malaa sɛ manyi kanya i ɔrɔ̃go kakɔkɔ̃? Ayi kumɛgɔ i ɔrɛrɛ̃ piagu ma ne, maibawo.

ref: But the time will come when he will be taken from them. Then they will go without eating.
text: Ɣɛɛ ne, iyi iwɛ̃ mato maakɔ wũ maruigu i ma kɔrɛ, iyɔ ɔ̃ malaa ka maanyi kanya.

ref: No one patches old clothes by sewing on a piece of new cloth. The new piece would shrink and tear a bigger hole.
text: “Kuwɛ̃ iisɛ ɔta ɔkati kuere ala kakatisɛi ɣɛtɛ. Si abara ngbɔ ne, kakatisɛi ɣɛtɛ gamɔ to kaakarĩ karui iyoi nɛ loabɛ iɖo iɖeakatɔ̃.

ref: No one pours new wine into old wineskins. The wine would swell and burst the old skins. Then the wine would be lost, and the skins would be ruined. New wine must be put into new wineskins. A Question about the Sabbath (Matthew
text: Ne ɔso ana ne, maisu ndã ɣɛtɛ mafere i bɔi ɔko akpo kuere ame. Si ndã ɣɛtɛ ɔtsɛ ifu ne, miatã sɔ ɔko kuere aabiɛ gɔ ndã gu bɔi ɔko akpo ɔɖuɖu aanina. Ne ɔso ndã ɣɛtɛ ne, akpo ɣɛtɛ ame masɛ mafere me.” Yesu Nɖe Iwarãyi Sate.

ref: One Sabbath Jesus and his disciples were walking through some wheat fields. His disciples were picking grains of wheat as they went along.
text: Iwarãyi iwɛ̃ gɔ Yesu sɛ ɔto ɔfe i kakpɛkɔ̃ kawɛ̃ ame gu ɔ̃ marasuãdze ne, marasuãdze ɔtsɛ atita ibɛbɛse iwe.

ref: Some Pharisees asked Jesus, “Why are your disciples picking grain on the Sabbath? They are not supposed to do that!”
text: Ne ma Farisise ɔkarɛ Yesu sɔ, “Be ɔso i fɔ marasuãdze to mabara nnɛ i bo mmara iitã ɔri sɔ maabara ku Iwarãyi.”

ref: Jesus answered, “Haven't you read what David did when he and his followers were hungry and in need?
text: Mmɔ i Yesu ɔkarɛ ma sɔ, “Miika i Ɔko Sekelea ame nnɛ i David ɔbara ɔwi gɔ i kukã ɔkpɛ̃ wũ gu ɔ̃ maturi? David ɔkɛlɛ Ɣaa kasɔrɛkɔ̃ ɔwi gɔ i

ref: So the Son of Man is Lord over the Sabbath.”
text: Ne ɔso Ɔturi Awune Ɔbi nɖe Iwarãyi Sate.”

ref: He even had to tell his disciples to get a boat ready to keep him from being crushed by the crowds.
text: Yesu ɔɣere ɔ̃ marasuãdze sɔ mabie ɔkolo masɛ i ibu kuruɛ si ɔ̃abo ɔsɛ i ɔ̃ ame alasɔ maturi ɔɣede.

ref: The Pharisees wanted to accuse Jesus of doing something wrong, and they kept watching to see if Jesus would heal him on the Sabbath.
text: Ɔwi gɔmɔ ne, Yesu matsirise mawɛ̃ ma nto mabie ɔri gɔ iso maaki fiɛ maaɖaɛ wũ kanya sɔ ɔɣɛrɛ ma mmara ɔkarã anɔ sɔ maanya si ɔ̃asa sinyɛ ku Iwarãyi.

ref: Jesus told the man to stand up where everyone could see him.
text: Yesu ɔɣere ɔrɛrɛ̃ gɔ kɔrɔ̃ lokarĩ sɔ ɔta ɔya i ma katɔ̃.

ref: Jesus was angry as he looked around at the people. Yet he felt sorry for them because they were so stubborn. Then he told the man, “Stretch out your hand.” He did, and his bad hand was healed.
text: Yesu ɔfiniki ɔnyɔ ma ku sikpã, ana ne, iɖo wũ kayiri ala ma situ gbogboroa iti. Ne ɔɣere nyɛse sɔ, “Nabarã fɔ kɔrɔ̃.” Ɔnabarã kɔ̃. Ɔluwɛ̃ kere, ilɛ wũ.

ref: Simon was one of the twelve, and Jesus named him Peter.
text: Ma iweo-inyɔ ma ɔɖi mmɔ nɖe Simon gɔ i Yesu ɔtã iyere sɔ Petro

ref: Jesus decided to ask some of his disciples to go up on a mountain with him, and they went.
text: Ara wagbe kama ne, Yesu ɔkɛlɛ kabemi iso. Mmɔ ɔkpere mma ɔto ɔbie sɔ mawegu wũ ɔbɔ ɔ̃ kɔrɛ ne.

ref: There were also James and John, the two sons of Zebedee. Jesus called them Boanerges, which means “Thunderbolts.”
text: gu Yakobo gu ɔ̃ kpeama Yohanes mma nɖe Zebedeo mabi ma i Yesu ɔtã iyere sɔ Boanerges gɔ karɔ̃ nɖe sɔ Kado Kaɣɛ Mabi.

ref: Andrew, Philip, Bartholomew, Matthew, Thomas, James son of Alphaeus, and Thaddaeus were also apostles. The others were Simon, known as the Eager One,
text: Matsɔra nɖe Andrea gu Filipo gu Bartolomeo gu Mateo gu Toma gu Yakobo gɔ nɖe Alfeo ɔbi gu Tadeo gu Simon gɔ nsɛ ɔɖɔɛ ɔ̃ aɖe kaɖe

ref: Some teachers of the Law of Moses came from Jerusalem and said, “This man is under the power of Beelzebul, the ruler of demons! He is even forcing out demons with the help of Beelzebul.”
text: Mmara matedze ma lobɔrɛ i Yerusalem ɔɣɛ sɔ, “Siwarã lalaa igara lotã wũ ɔle ɔso ɔto ɔwo siwarã lalaa ɔsa ɔɖi i maturi ame.”

ref: The crowd sitting around Jesus told him, “Your mother and your brothers and sisters are outside and want to see you.”
text: Ne mma lomini wũ i mmɔ ɔɣere wũ sɔ, “Nyɔ, fɔ ɔnyi gu manyiibi ɣɛ i inyamɔ mato makpere-ɔ!”

ref: Jesus told the people to gather around him. Then he spoke to them in riddles and said: How can Satan force himself out?
text: Ne ɔso Yesu ɔkpere maturi ɔɖe ma ika i igbã ame sɔ, “Nda iawo ɔba fiɛ Ɔbosam aasa so ɔɖi i ɔturi ame?

ref: Jesus went back home, and once again such a large crowd gathered that there was no chance even to eat.
text: Gɔ Yesu ɔbɔrɛ i mmɔ ɔkɛlɛ iyo ne, maturi gbodzoo ɔba ma kɔrɛ ana. Ne ɔso ne, iwe ɔle gbaã sɔ Yesu gu ɔ̃ marasuãdze aana ɔwi maɖe ara.

ref: A nation whose people fight each other won't last very long.
text: Si iba sɔ sigarakaɖekɔ̃ ɔɣɛɣɛ so mato makpɛ̃gu so ne, sigarakaɖekɔ̃ gamɔ iibawo ɔya.

ref: And a family that fights won't last long either.
text: Si iyo maturi ɔɣɛ so, mata maɣɛ so iso ne, iyo nɛmɔ iibawo ɔya.

ref: So if Satan fights against himself, that will be the end of him.
text: Ne ɔso si Ɔbosam sigarakaɖekɔ̃ ɔta siɣɛ so iso fiɛ siɣɛɣɛ so ne, ɔ̃ sigarakaɖekɔ̃ gamɔ iibawo ɔya, siɔba kawirikɔ̃.

ref: How can anyone break into the house of a strong man and steal his things, unless he first ties up the strong man? Then he can take everything.
text: “Kuwɛ̃ iibawo ɔturi gɔ mba ɔle iyo ɔbo ɔmala ɔ̃ ara iɖo sɔ ɔ̃aɖe katɔ̃ ɔmɔɛ̃ wũ ɔnyi wũ tɔtɔ fiɛ ɔ̃abo ɔ̃ iyo ɔmala ɔ̃ ara.

ref: I promise you that any of the sinful things you say or do can be forgiven, no matter how terrible those things are.
text: “Loto losia ita lotã mi sɔ Ɣaa to ɔ̃asu ara lalaa wa i ɔturi ɔbara gu anyakpi i Ɣaa iso ɔtsɛ.

ref: But if you speak against the Holy Spirit, you can never be forgiven. That sin will be held against you forever.
text: Ɣɛɛ ne, imusuora biara nɛ i ɔturi aaɣɛ i Siwarã Bielea iso ne, Ɣaa iibasu ɔtsɛ wũ kuiyikuiyi. Maibasu, aɣɛrɛ wagbe matsɛ manya.”

ref: Jesus said this because the people were saying that he had an evil spirit in him. Jesus' Mother and Brothers (Matthew
text: Yesu ɔɣɛ ngbɔ alasɔ mato maɣɛ sɔ siwarã lalaa mpia i ɔ̃ ame. Mabe Nɖe Yesu Manyii?

ref: Jesus' mother and brothers came and stood outside. Then they sent someone with a message for him to come out to them.
text: Ɔwi gɔmɔ ne, Yesu ɔnyi gu ɔ̃ manyiibi ɔba maaya i inyamɔ mapia wũ ɔturi sɔ mato mabie wũ.

ref: Jesus asked, “Who is my mother and who are my brothers?”
text: Ne Yesu ɔkarɛ ma sɔ, “Loate mi ngɔ nɖe wũ ɔnyi mɔmɔ gu wũ manyiibi mɔmɔ.”

ref: Then he looked at the people sitting around him and said, “Here are my mother and my brothers.
text: Ne ɔki ɔnyɔ mma nsi maki malɔ wũ i mmɔ ɔɣɛ sɔ, “Minyɔ, mmagbe nɖe wũ ɔnyi gu wũ manyiibi ne!

ref: Anyone who obeys God is my brother or sister or mother.”
text: Ɔbiara gɔ nsɛ ɔbara Ɣaa kuɖɔɛ ne, ɔ̃ nɖe wũ ɔnyiibi gu wũ ɔnyi ne.”

ref: The next time Jesus taught beside Lake Galilee, a big crowd gathered. It was so large that he had to sit in a boat out on the lake, while the people stood on the shore.
text: Gɔ Yesu ɔkɛlɛ Galilea ibu kɔrɛ ana ne, ɔsɛ ɔ̃ate Itɔ̃me Bielea. Gɔ mma loki malɔ wũ ɔsi ɔso ne, ɔya ɔbo ɔsɛ i ɔkolo ame i ndu iso. Ne maturi ka maɣɛ i ɔkpokpo fiɛ Yesu to ɔɖegu ma ika ne.

ref: While the farmer was scattering the seed, some of it fell along the road and was eaten by birds.
text: Gɔ ɔto ɔsa ne, ɔwɛ̃ ɔɣo i ɔri kuruɛ, ne kurodzai ɔrɛrɛ kuɖe ne.

ref: He used stories to teach them many things, and this is part of what he taught:
text: Ɔte ma ara gbodzoo i agbã ame. Ara wa ɔte ma i ngbe ne.

ref: Now listen! A farmer went out to scatter seed in a field.
text: Ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Kpɛ̃dze ɔwɛ̃ ɔsɛ ɔraɖui kasakɔ̃.

ref: Other seeds fell on thin, rocky ground and quickly started growing because the soil wasn't very deep.
text: Ɔwɛ̃ ɔɣo i ata iso ngbegɔ i sise iisi, ne ɔso ɔre mala.

ref: But when the sun came up, the plants were scorched and dried up, because they did not have deep roots.
text: Gɔ kuɣɛ̃ ɔsia kere ne, ɔ̃ ɔɖuɖu ɔkpi, alasɔ ɔ̃ siɖu iibo karɔ̃.

ref: Some other seeds fell where thornbushes grew up and choked out the plants. So they did not produce any grain.
text: Ɔraɖui ɔwɛ̃ ɔɣo i kuposɛ̃ ndɛ̃ ne ɔso gɔ ɔre ne, kuɣa kufu wũ sɔ ɔ̃iwo ɔmɔ fiɛ ɔ̃aɣɔ̃ abi.

ref: Finally, Jesus said: What is God's kingdom like? What story can I use to explain it?
text: Yesu ɔkarɛ sɔ, “Be boasu bokateragu Ɣaa sigarakaɖekɔ̃? Iɖe igbã nɛ loasu lokparara ne?

ref: When Jesus was alone with the twelve apostles and some others, they asked him about these stories.
text: Ɔwi gɔ i Yesu ɔnɔwɛ̃ ka ɔsi ne, mma nsɛ masiai wũ ɔwi biara gu ɔ̃ marasuãdze iweo-inyɔ ɔba maakarɛ wũ sɔ ɔɖi igbã karɔ̃ ɔte ma.

ref: But they don't have roots, and they don't last very long. As soon as life gets hard or the message gets them in trouble, they give up.
text: Ɣɛɛ ne, itɔ̃me siɖu iibo karɔ̃ kukaakɔ. Mafɔ maɖe ɔwi kurukutu kere, ɣɛɛ si iɖaa gu kama iti ɔba ala Itɔ̃me Bielea iti ne, mato maafiniki makpese kama.

ref: The reason is, “These people will look and look, but never see. They will listen and listen, but never understand. If they did, they would turn to God and be forgiven.” Jesus Explains the Story about the Farmer (Matthew
text: Matsɛrɛ masɛ i Ɔko Sekelea ame sɔ, ‘Mato maanya, ɣɛɛ maibanya maɖi ite. Mato maanɔ, ɣɛɛ maibanɔ karɔ̃, sɔ si maibafiniki fiɛ Ɣaa aasu ma akpi ɔtsɛ ma.’ ”

ref: The seeds that fell along the road are the people who hear the message. But Satan soon comes and snatches it away from them.
text: Maturi mawɛ̃ se lɛ ɔraɖui gɔ loɣo i ɔri kuruɛ. Gɔ manɔ ne Ɔbosam ɔmɔɛ̃ ɔɖi i ma iti ame ɔluwɛ̃.

ref: What the farmer is spreading is really the message about the kingdom.
text: Ɔraɖui sadze gɔgbe to ɔsa Ɣaa itɔ̃me.

ref: Jesus also said: You don't light a lamp and put it under a clay pot or under a bed. Don't you put a lamp on a lampstand?
text: Yesu ɔkarɛ ma ana sɔ, “Ɔturi sɛ ɔtsue ɔkaniɛ ɔsu ɔpia i kapoi ame ɣee karɛkɔ̃ kayirinɔ? Iiɖe kasiakɔ̃ masɛ masia ɔkaniɛ fiɛ ɔ̃asa?

ref: Listen carefully to what you hear! The way you treat others will be the way you will be treated—and even worse.
text: Ne ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Mikã atsue kukaakɔ nwa mito minɔ lo! Alasɔ ikerera nɛ misu mikere mitã maturi mama ne, ne ame maasu makere matã mi makuti mafa mi ne!

ref: Again Jesus said: God's kingdom is like what happens when a farmer scatters seed in a field.
text: Yesu ɔɣere ma ana sɔ, “Ɣaa sigarakaɖekɔ̃ se lɛ ɔrɛrɛ̃ gɔ lopɛ ara i kakpɛkɔ̃ ame.

ref: It is the ground that makes the seeds sprout and grow into plants that produce grain.
text: Karɔ̃ ga iso mapɛ ara mɔmɔ lonyɔ ɔraɖui iso ngbɔ. Iɖeakatɔ̃ ne, ɔsɛ ɔre, ɔɖi aɣata, ɔfere aɣɔ̃la, ɔɣɔ̃ abi gbodzoo.

ref: Then when harvest season comes and the grain is ripe, the farmer cuts it with a sickle. A Mustard Seed (Matthew
text: Si abi ɔrɛtɛ ne, kpɛ̃dze to ɔ̃asɔrɛ̃, alasɔ ɔraɖui ɔsɔrɛ̃wi ɔɔɣo.” Igbã Isegu Idziribi Kosoi Iso.

ref: It is like what happens when a mustard seed is planted in the ground. It is the smallest seed in all the world.
text: Ise lɛ idziribi kosoi iwɛ̃ nɛ i ɔrɛrɛ̃ ɔwɛ̃ ɔsu ɔsɛ ɔ̃apɛ.

ref: But once it is planted, it grows larger than any garden plant. It even puts out branches that are big enough for birds to nest in its shade. The Reason for Teaching with Stories (Matthew
text: Isɛ ire ibɔrɛ, imɔ ibara kadzirimi ga lomɔ kaɖo i ayo ame kakpɛkɔ̃ ga matɛ̃ ɔkpã mapia ame, kata akpa katã ɔyu sɔ kato kurodzai aabara ayo i kã ame.”

ref: He did not tell them anything without using stories. But when he was alone with his disciples, he explained everything to them. A Storm (Matthew
text: Ɔ̃isɛ ɔɖe ma ika si iiɖe igbã ɔto ɔpɛ ma. Ɣɛɛ si ɔ̃ gu ɔ̃ marasuãdze ka masi ne, ɔsɛ ɔɖi wã karɔ̃ ɔte ma. Yesu Ɔtindza Awɛ.

ref: That evening, Jesus said to his disciples, “Let's cross to the east side.”
text: Iyi nɛmɔ kutsuɛ ame ne, Yesu ɔkpere marasuãdze ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Mitã bowara ibu bokɛlɛ ɔkpokpo gɔmɔ isɛ.”

ref: Jesus used many other stories when he spoke to the people, and he taught them as much as they could understand.
text: Yesu ɔɣere ma Itɔ̃me Bielea i agbã wagbe ame sɔ si maawo karɔ̃ ɔnɔ.

ref: So they left the crowd, and his disciples started across the lake with him in the boat. Some other boats followed along.
text: Ne marui i iturikuri kɔrɛ mmɔ mabo i ɔkolo ame ku Yesu sɔ maakɛlɛ ibu kama isɛ. Sikolo mama ana se i mmɔ.

ref: Suddenly a storm struck the lake. Waves started splashing into the boat, and it was about to sink.
text: Ɔluwɛ̃ ne, awɛ ɔtsɛ ikpɛ̃, gɔ ato apɛ ndu apia i ɔkolo ame sɔ miɔro ɔyi fiɛ ɔ̃awã.

ref: Jesus was in the back of the boat with his head on a pillow, and he was asleep. His disciples woke him and said, “Teacher, don't you care that we're about to drown?”
text: Ɔwi gɔmɔ ame ne, Yesu rɛ i ɔkolo kama, ɔsu iti ɔsia i isumuɛ iso ɔto ɔrɛ sirɛ. Ne matara wũ sɔ, “Ratedze, bo kukpi iisu-ɔ kuira i mmɔ?”

ref: Jesus got up and ordered the wind and the waves to be quiet. The wind stopped, and everything was calm.
text: Yesu ɔta ɔya ɔtɔ̃me ɔpia awɛ ku silɔ teteree sɔ, “Awɛ, nyua ɔkpɛ̃! Ndu, sɛ kpoo!” Ɔluwɛ̃ awɛ ɔɣɔ kananana, fiɛ ndu iso ana ɔlo kpoo.

ref: Jesus asked his disciples, “Why were you afraid? Don't you have any faith?”
text: Ne Yesu ɔkarɛ ɔ̃ marasuãdze sɔ, “Be ɔso miba ifɔ̃ ngbɔ? Miifɔ me miɖe?”

ref: Now they were more afraid than ever and said to each other, “Who is this? Even the wind and the waves obey him!”
text: Ifɔ̃ siare ɔmɔɛ̃ ma ne, mato makarɛ so sɔ, “Mme ɔturi igbã ɔɖe ngbe? Ndu gu ɔwiri to makã wũ atsue!”

ref: She had said to herself, “If I can just touch his clothes, I will be healed.”
text: Ala ɔɣere so sɔ, “Si loana ɔ̃ awu kere lopɛgu ne, loto loasarɛ.”

ref: Jesus said to the woman, “You are now well because of your faith. May God give you peace! You are healed, and you will no longer be in pain.”
text: Yesu ɔɣere wũ sɔ, “Wũ ɔbi birɔ̃gomi, fɔ kafɔkaɖe losa-ɔ. Rui kɛlɛ i isoɣɔ ame. Fɔ ɔnyɛ ɔɔlɛ-ɔ.”

ref: She had gone to many doctors, and they had not done anything except cause her a lot of pain. She had paid them all the money she had. But instead of getting better, she only got worse.
text: Atoa sɔ sinyɛ masadze to matã wũ awa ne, ɔwe i ibo ame. Ɔ̃ɔnina ɔ̃ sikã ɔɖuɖu ɔsa ɔnyɛ gɔgbe, ɣɛɛ ɔ̃ito ɔsarɛ. Ɣɛɛ ɔnyagɛ̃ kere i ɔnyɛ togu.

ref: Then Jesus went inside and said to them, “Why are you crying and carrying on like this? The child isn't dead. She is just asleep.”
text: ɔbo i ma ndɛ̃, ne ɔkarɛ sɔ, “Be nsɛgu kulu gɔgbe? Be fiɛ mito mibiɛ? Ɔbi iikpi, sirɛ kere ɔto ɔrɛ!”

ref: Jesus and his disciples crossed Lake Galilee and came to shore near the town of Gerasa.
text: Nwagbe kama ne, Yesu gu ɔ̃ marasuãdze ɔsɛ Gadara ibuiti ame i Galilea ibu kama.

ref: from the graveyard where he had been living. No one was able to tie the man up anymore, not even with a chain.
text: Ɔrɛrɛ̃ gɔgbe kawekɔ̃ nɖe maturi kabiarakɔ̃. Kuwɛ̃ iisɛ ɔwo wũ ɔnyi ku ala nane ngɔsɔngɔsɔn.

ref: He had often been put in chains and leg irons, but he broke the chains and smashed the leg irons. No one could control him.
text: Ɔwi gbodzoo si mawo wũ nrɔɔ̃ gu ngba ɔnyi ne, ɔsɛ ɔtu ngɔsɔngɔsɔn gu kugbagɔrɔĩ ɔɖi so. Ɔwe ɔle gɔ kuwɛ̃ iisɛ ɔwo wũ ɔmɔɛ̃.

ref: Night and day he was in the graveyard or on the hills, yelling and cutting himself with stones.
text: Kasɛ̃ ɔɖuɖu gu anɔwã ne, ɔsɛ ɔwe ɔki i kabiarakɔ̃ gu kubemi iso, ɔla kulu, ɔsu ata ɔtutu so.

ref: When the man saw Jesus in the distance, he ran up to him and knelt down.
text: Gɔ ɔnya Yesu dzoroo ne, ɔtere ɔba ɔ̃apɛ agɛgɛ̃ i ɔ̃ katɔ̃.

ref: He shouted, “Jesus, Son of God in heaven, what do you want with me? Promise me in God's name that you won't torture me!”
text: Ne ɔla kulu teteree ɔɣɛ sɔ, “Yesu, Ɣaa gɔ nɖe Ɔlesate Ɔbi! Be mpia i bo gu-ɔ inyɔ ndɛ̃? Loto-ɔ i kukpa i Ɣaa iyere ame sɔ daakpadzɛ̃ me kutsue!”

ref: He then begged Jesus not to send them away.
text: Ɔpɛ Yesu nrɔɔ̃ teteree sɔ ɔdaasa ma ɔɖi i karɔ̃ gamɔ ame.

ref: Over on the hillside a large herd of pigs was feeding.
text: Makparako gbodzoo ɣɛ mato maɖe ara i kabemi kawɛ̃ kuruɛ i mmɔ.

ref: So the evil spirits begged Jesus, “Send us into those pigs! Let us go into them.”
text: Siwarã lalaa ɔtã Yesu iti sɔ, “Sa bo pia i makparako ma nɣɛ i mmɔ ame.”

ref: Jesus let them go, and they went out of the man and into the pigs. The whole herd of about
text: Ne ɔtã ɔri fiɛ siwarã lalaa ɔbɔrɛ i ɔrɛrɛ̃ gɔgbe ame sibo i makparako ame ne. Makparako ma losi lɛ kukpi kunyɔ ɔtere kpitikpitikpiti maso kabemi mabo i itubu ame makpi.

ref: The men taking care of the pigs ran to the town and the farms to spread the news. Then the people came out to see what had happened.
text: Mma nto manyɔ makparako iso ɔtere makɛlɛ miya gu simagɛ̃ tsɔra ame, masɛ maaɣɛ nwa loba. Ne ɔso maturi ɔtere masɛ sɔ maanyɔ.

ref: When they came to Jesus, they saw the man who had once been full of demons. He was sitting there with his clothes on and in his right mind, and they were terrified.
text: Gɔ maba Yesu kɔrɛ ne, manya ngɔ ame i siwarã lalaa ɔwe sɔ ɔsi i karɔ̃, ɔpia awu, iti ame ɔɔkpa wũ waĩ. Ifɔ̃ ɔmɔɛ̃ ma ɔɖuɖu gbaã.

ref: Everyone who had seen what had happened told about the man and the pigs.
text: Mma lonya nnɛ loba ɔɣere maturi nwa loba i ɔrɛrɛ̃ gɔmɔ gu makparako iso.

ref: Then the people started begging Jesus to leave their part of the country.
text: Ne ɔso mapɛ Yesu nrɔɔ̃ sɔ ɔbɔrɛ i ma karɔ̃ iso.

ref: But Jesus would not let him. Instead, he said, “Go home to your family and tell them how much the Lord has done for you and how good he has been to you.”
text: Yesu iitɔrãgu wũ. Ɣɛɛ ne, ɔɣere wũ sɔ, “Kpese kɛlɛ fɔ mɔmɔ maturi kɔrɛ sɛ aaɣere ma kumɛgɔ i Bosate ɔbuai-ɔ fiɛ ɔnyɔ-ɔ nnya.”

ref: The man went away into the region near the ten cities known as Decapolis and began telling everyone how much Jesus had done for him. Everyone who heard what had happened was amazed. A Dying Girl and a Sick Woman (Matthew
text: Ne ɔso ɔrɛrɛ̃ gɔgbe ɔrui ɔkila simagɛ̃ iweo dzɛmɔ ame, ɔɣere ma ibiara nɛ i Yesu ɔbara ɔtã wũ gɔ ibara mma lonɔ ɣii. Yesu Ɔsa Yairo Ɔbi Birɔ̃gomi Gu Ɔrɔ̃go Nyɛse Ɔwɛ̃.

ref: But the people laughed at him. After Jesus had sent them all out of the house, he took the girl's father and mother and his three disciples and went to where she was.
text: Gɔ ɔɣɛ ngbɔ ne, mma nɣɛ i mmɔ ɔtsɛ wũ ima. Ne Yesu ɔɖi ma ɔɖuɖu i iyo ne, ɔtã ɔbi ɔ̃ ɔse gu ɔbi ɔ̃ ɔnyi gu ɔ̃ marasuãdze ɔbo iyo ngbegɔ i ɔbi kpise rɛ.

ref: Once again Jesus got into the boat and crossed Lake Galilee. Then as he stood on the shore, a large crowd gathered around him.
text: Yesu ɔkpese ɔbɔrɛ i mmɔ ɔkɛlɛ ibu ibesɛ nɛ i kama. Gɔ ɔbo mmɔ ne, maturi gbodzoo ɔsarɛgu wũ.

ref: The person in charge of the synagogue was also there. His name was Jairus, and when he saw Jesus, he went over to him. He knelt at Jesus' feet
text: Ɔrɛrɛ̃ ɔwɛ̃ gɔ marɔ Yairo fiɛ ɔɖe ma Yudase kasarɛkɔ̃ ɔkpakpa ɔba mmɔ. Gɔ ɔnya Yesu ne, ɔpɛ agɛgɛ̃ i ɔ̃ katɔ̃.

ref: and started begging him for help. He said, “My little daughter is about to die! Please come and touch her, so she will get well and live.”
text: Ne ɔpɛ wũ nrɔɔ̃ teteree ɔɣɛ sɔ, “Wũ ɔbi birɔ̃gomi to ɔnyɛ gbaã, ne ɔso ba sia wũ fɔ kɔrɔ̃ iso si ɔna isosarɛ!”

ref: Jesus went with Jairus. Many people followed along and kept crowding around.
text: Yesu ɔta ɔsiai wũ. Gɔ maturi ma nsiai wũ ɔsi ɔso ne, mamini wũ fiɛ masɛ.

ref: In the crowd was a woman who had been bleeding for twelve years.
text: Ɔrɔ̃go ɔwɛ̃ gɔ i ara to afere akɔ aweo-anyɔ buu ɔwe i maturi ndɛ̃.

ref: The woman had heard about Jesus, so she came up behind him in the crowd and barely touched his clothes.
text: Ɔnɔ Yesu iyere, ne ɔso ɔbo i maturi ndɛ̃, ɔki i Yesu kama isɛ ɔpɛgu ɔ̃ awu kutsueti.

ref: As soon as she touched them, her bleeding stopped, and she knew she was healed.
text: Ne ɔso gɔ ɔpɛgu Yesu awu kere ne, ara wa nto afere wũ ko loɖaɛ! Ne ɔtsue i ɔ̃ ame sɔ ɔ̃ɔna isosarɛ ne.

ref: At that moment Jesus felt power go out from him. He turned to the crowd and asked, “Who touched my clothes?”
text: Ɔluwɛ̃ Yesu ɔtsue sɔ ɔle ɔwɛ̃ ɔbɔrɛ i ɔ̃ ame. Ne ɔso ɔtui ɔfiniki ɔkarɛ mma nsiai wũ sɔ, “Nna ɔɖe lopɛgu me awu?”

ref: His disciples said to him, “Look at all these people crowding around you! How can you ask who touched you?”
text: Ɔ̃ marasuãdze ɔɣere wũ sɔ, “Nyɔ kumɛgɔ i maturi ɔki malɔ bo. Be ɔso fiɛ ato akarɛ sɔ nna lopɛgu-ɔ?”

ref: But Jesus turned to see who had touched him.
text: Yesu ɣɛ ɔto ɔpiapia anɔmi sɔ ɔ̃anya ngɔ lopɛgu wũ.

ref: The woman knew what had happened to her. So she came trembling with fear and knelt down in front of Jesus. Then she told him the whole story.
text: Ɔrɔ̃go ana ɔtsue kumɛgɔ ka ise wũ. Ne ɔso i ifɔ̃ ame ne, ɔsɛ ɔba ɔ̃apɛ agɛgɛ̃ i Yesu ngbatɔ̃. Ne ɔɖi ɔte wũ kumɛgɔ iba ne.

ref: While Jesus was still speaking, some people came from Jairus' home and said, “Your daughter has died! Why bother the teacher anymore?”
text: Gɔ Yesu togu ika iɖe ne, maturi ɔbɔrɛ i Yario iyo maba maaɣere wũ sɔ, “Fɔ ɔbi birɔ̃gomi ɔɔkpi. Be ɔso akpese ato aɖaa Ratedze?”

ref: Jesus heard what they said, and he said to Jairus, “Don't worry. Just have faith!”
text: Yesu iifɔ itɔ̃me ne maɣɛ mmɔ. Ne ɔɣere Yairo sɔ, “Daanigã, fɔɖe kere!”

ref: Jesus did not let anyone go with him except Peter and the two brothers, James and John.
text: Yesu iibie sɔ kuwɛ̃ si ɔsiai wũ, ne ɔso ɔkɔ Petro gu Yakobo gu ɔ̃ ɔnyiibi Yohanes marui makɛlɛ Yairo iyo.

ref: They went home with Jairus and saw the people crying and making a lot of noise.
text: Gɔ mabo iyo fiɛ Yesu ɔnya sikpabo, kulu gu kaku ga mato mabiɛ ne,

ref: He took the twelve-year-old girl by the hand and said, “Talitha, koum!” which means, “Little girl, get up!” The girl got right up and started walking around. Everyone was greatly surprised.
text: Ɔluwɛ̃ kabirɔ̃gomi ga loɔfɔ akɔ aweo-anyɔ kere lota katsɛ isɛ iki. Nnɛgbe ɔbara ma ɔɖuɖu ɣaa gbaã!

ref: But Jesus ordered them not to tell anyone what had happened. Then he said, “Give her something to eat.”
text: Yesu ɔsi ma mmara sɔ madaaɣere kuwɛ̃ ɖuɖuuɖu. Ne ka ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Mitã wũ aɖera ɔɖe.”

ref: Jesus and his disciples crossed the lake and brought the boat to shore near the town of Gennesaret.
text: Gɔ mawara itubu ne, maba maabo Genezaret karɔ̃ iso, ne ɔso mapɛ ma ɔkolo ikpo i ibu katɔ̃.

ref: But Jesus said, “Prophets are honored by everyone, except the people of their hometown and their relatives and their own family.”
text: Yesu ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Ɣaa kanyaɖidze ba kuwarɛ i kakɔi biara iɖo ɔ̃ mɔmɔ ɔmagɛ̃, ɔ̃ maturi gu ɔ̃ iyo maturi anɔmi ame.”

ref: But when Herod heard about Jesus, he said, “This must be John! I had his head cut off, and now he has come back to life.”
text: Ɔwi gɔ i Herodes ɔnɔ atɔ̃me wagbe ne, ɔ̃ wũ ɔɣɛ sɔ, “Yohanes gɔ lotã matu iti ɔɖe loɔta ɔba ngbã ana!”

ref: After the apostles returned to Jesus, they told him everything they had done and taught.
text: Katɔ̃memasɛdze ma i Yesu ɔsese sɔ masɛ maaki maɣɛ Itɔ̃me Bielea ɔkpese maba maaɣere wũ nwa ɔɖuɖu mabara gu nwa masɛ maate i ma irisɛ ame.

ref: The next Sabbath he taught in the synagogue. Many of the people who heard him were amazed and asked, “How can he do all this? Where did he get such wisdom and the power to work these miracles?
text: Gɔ Iwarãyi ɔɣo ne, ɔsɛ ɔ̃ate ara ɔsegu i Itɔ̃me Bielea iso i ma Yudase kasarɛkɔ̃. Ibara mma lonɔ ɔ̃ atɔ̃me ɣaa. Makarɛ so sɔ, “Lɛ ɔsegu ara wagbe. Mme inɔgba igbã matã wũ fiɛ ɔto ɔwo awawãra wagbe ɔbara?

ref: Isn't he the carpenter, the son of Mary? Aren't James, Joseph, Judas, and Simon his brothers? Don't his sisters still live here in our town?” The people were upset because of what he was doing.
text: Ɛɛbɛrɛ iɖe ɔ̃ nɖe Maria ɔbi gɔ nɖe rawedze? Yakobo gu Yose gu Yuda gu Simon mɛrɛ̃ ɔnyiibi? Fiɛ ɔ̃ manyiiko ana pia i bo ndɛ̃ ngbe.” Ne ɔso maifɔ wũ.

ref: He told them, “You may take along a walking stick. But don't carry food or a traveling bag or any money.
text: Ɔsɛ̃ ɔtã ma sɔ, “Midaasu kuira mila i mi irisɛ ame iɖo ɔsɛdziri. Midaasu aɖera ɣee akpo. Midaapia sikã i mi kukotoi ame.

ref: If any place won't welcome you or listen to your message, leave and shake the dust from your feet as a warning to them.”
text: Ɣɛɛ kakɔi biara ga maturi loifɔ mi ɣee maikã mi atsue ne, mikpakpa mi ngba iso sise mifere lɛ Ɣaa kutsue ikpadzɛ̃ nɛ loaba ikparĩ.”

ref: Herodias had a grudge against John and wanted to kill him. But she could not do it
text: ɔ̃ itɔ̃me to Herodia sikpã, ne ɔso ɔbie sɔ ɔ̃aɖoe wũ, ɣɛɛ ɔ̃iwo.

ref: because Herod was afraid of John and protected him. He knew that John was a good and holy man. Even though Herod was confused by what John said, he was glad to listen to him. And he often did.
text: Herodes ɔla ɔ̃ɔnigã Yohanes, alasɔ ɔɣe sɔ ɔnukuarese gu ɔturi sekelea ɔɖe. Ne ɔso ɔto ɔdzuɛ̃ wũ. Ɔbie sɔ ɔ̃akã ɔ̃ itɔ̃me atsue ɔwi ku ɔwi ame atoa sɔ ɔ̃ atɔ̃me sɛ ɔtsue wũ ɔtu si ɔnɔ.

ref: I swear that I will give you as much as half of my kingdom, if you want it.”
text: Ne ka ɔɣɛ ɔsia sɔ, “Lotarĩgu wũ sigaraiyara sɔ loatã-ɔ ira biara nɛ aakarɛ me, isɛ iatui i wũ sigarakaɖekɔ̃ kasɛ kawɛ̃!”

ref: The girl left and asked her mother, “What do you think I should ask for?” Her mother answered, “The head of John the Baptist!”
text: Ne ɔso, ɔbitɛ gɔgbe ɔsɛ ɔ̃akarɛ ɔ̃ ɔnyi sɔ, “Be si lokarɛ?” Ne ɔ̃ ɔnyi ɔɣere wũ sɔ, “Karɛ wũ Ndupiedze Yohanes iti.”

ref: The girl hurried back and told Herod, “Here and now on a serving plate I want the head of John the Baptist!”
text: Ɔbitɛ ɔpɛ siwa ɔkɛlɛ igara kɔrɛ ɔɣere Herodes sɔ, “Lobie sɔ loafɔ Ndupiedze Yohanes iti i kategelei iso ngbe kiniɔ!”

ref: Herod was very sorry for what he had said. But he did not want to break the promise he had made in front of his guests.
text: Itɔ̃me nɛgbe ɔbo Herodes so kukaakɔ, ɣɛɛ kumɛgɔ ɔkã ndamu i makpakpa ma nsigu wũ i ɔpɔrɔ̃ iti katɔ̃ ɔso ne, ɔ̃isɛ̃ nnɛ i ɔbi ɔkarɛ.

ref: At once he ordered a guard to cut off John's head there in prison.
text: Ɔluwɛ̃ ɔpia ɔ̃ kaɖaɛsime sɔ ɔsɛ ɔ̃atu Yohanes iti ɔbɔ. Ne ɔkɛlɛ ngbegɔ i Yohanes rɛ i iyo ɔsɛ ɔ̃atu wũ iti ne.

ref: The guard put the head on a serving plate and took it to the girl. Then she gave it to her mother.
text: Ɔsu ne ɔsia i kategelei iso, ɔsu ɔba ɔ̃atã ɔbitɛ gɔgbe. Ne ɔfɔ, ɔsu ɔkɛlɛgu ɔ̃ ɔnyi ne.

ref: When John's followers learned that he had been killed, they took his body and put it in a tomb. Jesus Feeds Five Thousand (Matthew
text: Gɔ Yohanes marasuãdze ɔnɔ ne, masɛ maasu wũ masɛ maabiara. Yesu Ɔtã Maturi Kukpi Kuru Aɖera.

ref: But so many people were coming and going that Jesus and the apostles did not even have a chance to eat. Then Jesus said, “Let's go to a place where we can be alone and get some rest.”
text: Maturi ma nto maba ma kɔrɛ ɔfe iɣede sɔ Yesu gu ɔ̃ marasuãdze siina ɔwi sɔ maaɖe nane aɖera. Ne ɔso Yesu ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Mitã bo ɔnɔwɛ̃ saã si bokɛlɛ ngbegɔ i kuwɛ̃ na si bosɛ boawarã kɛkɛĩ.”

ref: They left in a boat for a place where they could be alone.
text: Mata mabo i ɔkolo ame sɔ masɛ ngbegɔ i maturi na.

ref: That evening the disciples came to Jesus and said, “This place is like a desert, and it's already late.
text: Gɔ kutsuɛ ɔɣo ne, marasuãdze ɔbore mabo wũ maɣere wũ sɔ, “Kaɖe ɔɔro fiɛ ngbe ɔdzorogu ayo ame.

ref: Jesus replied, “You give them something to eat.” But they asked him, “Don't you know it would take almost a year's wages to buy all of these people something to eat?”
text: Yesu ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Mi mɔmɔ mitã ma irere maɖe.” Ne maɣere wũ sɔ, “Nnɛmɔ to iafɔ lɛ ɔturi awa farafana kuso. Ato abie sɔ bonina ngbɔ itã ma aɖera?”

ref: Then Jesus said, “How much bread do you have? Go and see!” They found out and answered, “We have five small loaves of bread and two fish.”
text: Ne Yesu ɔkarɛ ma sɔ, “Abolo arɛ̃ miba i mi ara ame? Misɛ mianyɔ.” Gɔ masɛ maanyɔ ne, maɣere wũ sɔ, “Abolo abo aru gu kufarai kunyɔ lokuti.”

ref: Jesus told his disciples to tell the people to sit down on the green grass.
text: Yesu ɔɣere ɔ̃ marasuãdze sɔ matã maturi si masɛ i karɔ̃ akuri ame i sikua iso.

ref: All of them saw him and were terrified. But at this same time he said, “Don't worry! I am Jesus. Don't be afraid.”
text: Manigã gbaã sɔ manya wũ i ndu iso. Ne Yesu ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Midaanigã! Mme loɖe. Mina ɔtu.”

ref: Jesus took the five loaves and the two fish. He looked up toward heaven and blessed the food. Then he broke the bread and handed it to his disciples to give to the people. He also divided the two fish, so everyone could have some.
text: Yesu ɔsu abolo gu afara anyɔ wamɔ, ɔnyɔ kato. Gɔ ɔpɛ Ɣaa siba i wã iti ne, ɔbɛbɛse ɔtã ɔ̃ marasuãdze sɔ maɣɛɣɛ mmamɔ. Ɔkpɛkpɛti kufarai kunyɔ ana ɔtã ma.

ref: After everyone had eaten all they wanted,
text: Gɔ ma ɔɖuɖu ɔɖe ara mami,

ref: Jesus' disciples picked up twelve large baskets of leftover bread and fish.
text: fiɛ marasuãdze ɔsa nwa lobuburi kanya ne, mana kusɛsɛi kuweo-kunyɔ.

ref: At once, Jesus made his disciples get into the boat and start back across to Bethsaida. But he stayed until he had sent the crowds away.
text: Ɔluwɛ̃ Yesu ɔtã ɔ̃ marasuãdze ɔya mabo i ɔkolo ame sɔ mawara makɛlɛ Betsaida. Ne ɔ̃ wũ ka ɔɖi iturikuri ɔri ne.

ref: Then he told them goodbye and went up on the side of a mountain to pray.
text: Gɔ ɔ̃ɔɖi ma ɔri ɔro ne, ɔrui ɔkɛlɛ kube iso sɔ ɔ̃akparama kayi.

ref: Later in the evening he was still there by himself, and the boat was somewhere in the middle of the lake.
text: Si kutsuɛ aaɣo ne, ɔkolo ka ɔsɛ i ibu ndɛ̃, Yesu ɔnɔwɛ̃ ana ɣɛ i ibu katɔ̃.

ref: He could see that the disciples were struggling hard, because they were rowing against the wind. Not long before morning, Jesus came toward them. He was walking on the water and was about to pass the boat.
text: Ɔnya sɔ ɔ̃ maturi ba imɔrɛ̃ i ɔkolo iwa ame, alasɔ ɔwiri to ɔli ma. Ne ɔso si iapɛ kaya iɖɔwũ iru ne, Yesu ɔtsɛ isɛ ikɛlɛ ma kɔrɛ i ibu iso, ɔbie sɔ si ɔfe ma,

ref: He then got into the boat with them, and the wind died down. The disciples were completely confused.
text: Ne ɔya ɔbo i ɔkolo ame fiɛ ɔwiri ɔnyua ikpɛ̃ ne. Ibara ɔ̃ marasuãdze ɣaa gbaã!

ref: Their minds were closed, and they could not understand the true meaning of the loaves of bread. Jesus Heals Sick People in Gennesaret (Matthew
text: Alasɔ maitsue kumɛgɔ ibara fiɛ ɔtã maturi kukpi kuru aɖera maɖe mami. Mainɔ karɔ̃, ne ɔso maifɔ maɖe. Yesu Ɔsa Manyɛse I Genezaret.

ref: As soon as they got out of the boat, the people recognized Jesus.
text: Gɔ maya mabɔrɛ ɔkolo ame kere ne, maturi ɔnya Yesu maɖi ite.

ref: So they ran all over that part of the country to bring their sick people to him on mats. They brought them each time they heard where he was.
text: Ne ɔso matere maɣere matsɔra. Kakɔi biara ga i Yesu ɔbo ne, masore manyɛse mabɔ wũ i ma sikala iso.

ref: In every village or farm or marketplace where Jesus went, the people brought their sick to him. They begged him to let them just touch his clothes, and everyone who did was healed.
text: Ne ɔso ngɔi mɛ pelepele i Yesu ɔki, simagɛ̃ ame o-o, nsɛkɔ̃ o-o ne, maturi ɔsore manyɛse mabɔ kuɣakaɖekɔ̃ fiɛ mapɛ wũ nrɔɔ̃ sɔ ɔtã mapɛgu bidɛ ɔ̃ awu kutsueti kere. Mma ɔɖuɖu lopɛgu wũ ne, mana isosare.

ref: Some Pharisees and several teachers of the Law of Moses from Jerusalem came and gathered around Jesus.
text: Ma Farisise mawɛ̃ gu ma Yudase mmara matedze mawɛ̃ ɔbɔrɛ i Yerusalem maba Yesu kɔrɛ.
